Taxonomy Tree

  • Crop Improvement and Genetics -- Covers the science and practice of developing improved crop varieties through genetic, genomic, and breeding approaches
    • Plant Breeding Methods and Strategies -- Conventional and advanced breeding approaches including participatory, demand-driven, and speed breeding techniques
      • Participatory and Farmer-Centered Breeding -- Breeding approaches that involve farmers and end-users in variety selection and development
        • participatory variety selection 16 -- farmer-involved process for evaluating and selecting crop varieties
        • participatory breeding 11 -- involving farmers in variety selection and development process
        • participatory varietal selection 4 -- farmer involvement in testing and selecting new crop varieties
        • participatory cultivar selection 2 -- farmer involvement in variety evaluation and selection before commercial release
        • community-based breeding programs 3 -- participatory genetic improvement schemes involving farmers in design and implementation
        • community-based breeding 2 -- participatory genetic improvement programs managed by farmers
        • gender-responsive breeding 3 -- breeding programs that consider differentiated needs of men and women farmers
        • demand-driven breeding 3 -- breeding programs guided by end-user needs and market requirements
        • demand-led breeding 1 -- crop improvement driven by market and farmer requirements
        • market-responsive breeding 6 -- crop breeding aligned with specific market demands and consumer preferences
      • Advanced Breeding Techniques -- Modern and accelerated breeding methods
        • speed breeding 6 -- accelerated breeding technique to reduce generation time
        • doubled haploids 3 -- breeding technique producing homozygous lines in one generation
        • doubled haploid breeding 2 -- technique to rapidly produce homozygous inbred lines from heterozygous plants
        • double haploid breeding 1 -- plant breeding technique that produces homozygous lines in a single generation
        • rapid generation advancement 1 -- techniques to reduce breeding cycle time and accelerate genetic gains
        • rapid-cycle genomic selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach using genomic data to reduce crop development time
        • rapid cycle recurrent selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach that reduces cycle time from three years to one year
        • family-based selection 2 -- breeding strategy using family performance data to select superior genetic lines
        • population improvement 1 -- genetic enhancement of breeding populations for desired traits
        • evolutionary plant breeding 1 -- breeding approach maintaining genetic diversity through population mixtures
        • optimal contribution selection 1 -- breeding strategy to optimize genetic contributions while managing inbreeding
        • heterotic group development 1 -- classification of breeding lines based on combining ability for hybrid development
        • heterotic pattern integration 1 -- breeding approach to combine complementary genetic groups for hybrid vigor
      • Breeding Program Design and Management -- Frameworks and processes for organizing and running breeding programs
        • breeding pipeline 8 -- structured sequence of breeding activities and selection stages
        • breeding pipeline development 3 -- establishment of systematic processes for variety development
        • breeding program assessment 4 -- systematic evaluation of breeding program effectiveness and capabilities
        • breeding program costing 6 -- systematic analysis of resource allocation and costs in breeding operations
        • breeding cost analysis 1 -- systematic assessment of operational costs in breeding programs for improved resource allocation
        • breeding objective 1 -- systematic framework defining desired traits and their relative importance for genetic improvement programs
        • target product profiles 13 -- systematic documentation of desired crop characteristics based on market needs
        • target product profile development 1 -- systematic definition of desired characteristics for breeding targets
        • product profile development 3 -- defining target traits and characteristics for specific market segments
        • breeding data management 2 -- systematic organization and tracking of plant breeding information and materials
        • data-driven breeding 2 -- use of analytical tools and databases to guide breeding decisions
        • stage-gate framework 1 -- structured decision-making process with defined milestones and evaluation criteria
        • collaborative crop improvement networks 1 -- multi-institutional partnerships for coordinated plant breeding and research
        • genetic improvement program 6 -- systematic breeding to enhance genetic performance of crops or livestock
        • genetic improvement 3 -- systematic enhancement of livestock or crop traits through breeding programs
      • Specific Breeding Targets -- Breeding programs focused on specific traits or crops
        • disease resistance breeding 14 -- development of crop varieties with genetic resistance to pathogens
        • drought tolerance breeding 2 -- development of varieties adapted to water-limited conditions
        • tropical forage breeding 4 -- development of improved forage varieties adapted to tropical conditions for livestock systems
        • direct seeded rice breeding 1 -- development of rice varieties optimized for direct planting methods
        • low-methane forage breeding 1 -- selection and breeding of feed crops that reduce livestock methane production
        • malting quality breeding 1 -- crop improvement focused on traits suitable for malt production
        • forage improvement 4 -- development and promotion of improved feed crops for livestock
        • biofortification 41 -- enhancing nutritional content of crops through breeding
      • Crossing and Selection Methods -- Specific techniques for crossing and selecting plant material
        • combining ability analysis 2 -- assessment of parental lines' ability to produce superior hybrid offspring
        • combining ability 1 -- genetic capacity of parents to produce superior hybrid offspring
        • testcross evaluation 1 -- crossing experimental lines with standard testers to evaluate performance
        • line × tester analysis 1 -- breeding design to evaluate combining ability and hybrid performance
        • gene pyramiding 2 -- combining multiple resistance genes in a single variety
        • qtl pyramiding 1 -- breeding strategy that stacks multiple quantitative trait loci to combine beneficial traits
        • trait introgression 1 -- transfer of specific genetic traits from wild species into cultivated varieties through breeding
        • multiple resistance gene deployment 1 -- breeding strategy combining several disease resistance genes in single varieties
        • marker-assisted breeding 2 -- use of molecular markers to guide selection in breeding programs
        • molecular breeding 3 -- breeding approach using molecular tools and markers for trait selection
    • Genomics and Molecular Tools -- Genomic selection, genome editing, marker-assisted selection, sequencing, and molecular marker technologies
      • Genomic Selection and Prediction -- Methods using genome-wide markers for selection and prediction
        • genomic selection 22 -- breeding approach using genome-wide markers for trait prediction
        • genomic prediction 5 -- use of genome-wide markers to predict breeding values or phenotypes
        • multi-trait genomic selection 1 -- genomic selection approach considering multiple traits simultaneously
        • genome-wide association study 24 -- statistical approach to identify genetic variants associated with traits
        • genome-wide association mapping 4 -- genetic analysis technique to identify genes associated with specific traits
        • genome-wide association analysis 1 -- statistical approach to identify genetic variants associated with traits across the genome
        • single-step genome-wide association study 1 -- genomic analysis method combining pedigree and marker information to identify trait-associated genetic variants
      • Genome Editing and Engineering -- Technologies for precise modification of plant genomes
        • genome editing 13 -- precise modification of DNA sequences in living organisms
        • gene editing 2 -- biotechnology technique for precise modification of plant genomes
        • prime editing 4 -- precise genome modification technique that enables targeted genetic changes
        • genetic engineering 1 -- direct manipulation of organism's genes using biotechnology
      • Marker-Assisted Selection and QTL -- Molecular marker technologies for trait selection
        • marker-assisted selection 30 -- breeding technique using molecular markers to select for desired traits
        • quantitative trait loci mapping 9 -- identification of genomic regions controlling quantitative traits
        • quantitative trait locus mapping 4 -- genetic technique to identify chromosomal regions controlling quantitative traits
        • quantitative trait nucleotides 1 -- DNA sequence variants that contribute to quantitative trait variation
        • meta-qtl analysis 1 -- statistical method combining qtl data from multiple studies to identify consensus regions
        • haplotype analysis 1 -- study of linked genetic variants inherited together as blocks
        • haplotype stacking 1 -- breeding strategy to combine multiple favorable genetic variants in single varieties
      • Sequencing and Genomic Analysis -- DNA sequencing and genomic characterization methods
        • whole-genome sequencing 1 -- complete DNA analysis technique for pathogen identification and characterization
        • targeted sequencing 1 -- focused DNA sequencing approach to analyze specific genomic regions of interest
        • skim-sequencing 1 -- low-coverage DNA sequencing approach for cost-effective genotyping
        • digital sequence information 3 -- genomic data used for genetic resource characterization and analysis
        • pangenome construction 1 -- assembling comprehensive genomic landscape capturing complete genetic diversity within species
        • pan-genomics 1 -- study of entire gene sets across multiple individuals or varieties of a species
        • population genomics 2 -- study of genetic variation and evolution within and between populations
        • genomic diversity analysis 1 -- use of DNA sequencing data to understand genetic variation patterns in crops
        • comparative genomic analysis 1 -- comparison of genome structure and function between different organisms or varieties
        • population genetics analysis 1 -- study of genetic variation and structure within pathogen or crop populations
        • genetic variance partitioning 1 -- statistical decomposition of trait variation into additive and non-additive components
        • genetic parameter estimation 1 -- statistical methods to quantify heritable variation in traits
      • Molecular Markers and Fingerprinting -- Molecular marker development and application
        • molecular marker analysis 2 -- use of DNA markers to identify genes or traits in breeding programs
        • dna fingerprinting 1 -- genetic technique for identifying crop varieties using molecular markers
        • genetic fingerprinting 1 -- molecular technique for identifying unique genetic profiles of varieties
        • single nucleotide polymorphisms 1 -- DNA sequence variations where single nucleotides differ between individuals
        • polymorphism information content 1 -- measure of genetic marker informativeness based on allele frequencies
        • genomic marker development 1 -- creation of DNA-based tools for genetic analysis and tracking
        • automated dna extraction 1 -- machine-based process for extracting genetic material from biological samples
      • Multi-Omics and Bioinformatics -- Integration of multiple omics data types and computational analysis
        • multi-omics integration 3 -- combining genomics, transcriptomics, and metabolomics data for analysis
        • multi-omics approaches 2 -- integration of genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics and metabolomics data
        • metabolomic profiling 2 -- comprehensive analysis of small molecules and metabolites in biological samples
        • metabolomic analysis 1 -- comprehensive study of metabolites in biological samples
        • metabolomics-assisted breeding 1 -- integration of metabolite profiling with breeding programs to enhance crop improvement
        • transcriptome analysis 1 -- comprehensive study of RNA transcripts produced by genome under specific conditions
        • chromatin accessibility mapping 1 -- technique to identify regions of genome accessible for transcription factor binding
        • bioinformatics analysis 1 -- computational methods for analyzing biological data, particularly genomic sequences
        • rhizosphere microbiome analysis 1 -- study of microbial communities in plant root zones
        • alternative polyadenylation 1 -- molecular mechanism regulating gene expression through RNA processing
    • Phenotyping and Trait Evaluation -- Methods for assessing plant traits including high-throughput phenotyping, multi-environment trials, and stress tolerance screening
      • Phenotyping Methods and Technologies -- Tools and approaches for measuring plant traits
        • high-throughput phenotyping 4 -- automated measurement of plant traits using advanced technologies
        • phenotyping 2 -- measurement and analysis of observable plant characteristics
        • digital phenotyping 2 -- automated collection of plant trait data using digital technologies
        • plant phenotyping 1 -- measurement and analysis of plant physical and biochemical traits
        • computer vision phenotyping 1 -- automated plant trait measurement using image analysis algorithms
        • machine learning phenotyping 1 -- use of computational algorithms to automatically assess plant traits from sensor data
        • sparse phenotyping 1 -- strategy to reduce phenotyping costs while maintaining selection accuracy
        • multi-stress phenotyping 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop performance under multiple environmental stresses
        • hyperspectral imaging 1 -- advanced sensing technique analyzing light interaction across multiple wavelengths
        • near-infrared spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid quality assessment using light absorption
      • Multi-Environment and Multi-Location Testing -- Testing varieties across multiple environments and locations
        • multi-environment trials 4 -- testing genotypes across multiple locations and conditions
        • multi-environment testing 4 -- evaluating varieties across multiple locations and conditions
        • multi-environment trial evaluation 3 -- testing of varieties across multiple locations and seasons
        • multi-location testing 2 -- evaluation of crop varieties across multiple environments to assess performance and adaptation
        • multi-location trials 1 -- testing of crop varieties across multiple geographic locations for validation
        • multilocation trials 1 -- testing varieties across multiple sites to assess performance and adaptation
        • regional variety trials 1 -- multi-location testing of varieties across geographic regions
        • genotype by environment interaction 5 -- differential performance of genotypes across different environments
        • on-farm testing 16 -- evaluating varieties under actual farmer field conditions
        • on-farm trials 1 -- field research conducted on farmers' own land to test innovations under real conditions
        • on-farm verification 1 -- testing crop varieties under actual farmer conditions to validate performance
        • variety trial 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop or forage varieties across different environments
        • variety testing 3 -- systematic evaluation of crop varieties for performance and adaptation
        • variety adaptation trials 2 -- field testing to evaluate crop performance under local conditions
        • multi-site validation 1 -- testing technologies across multiple locations to assess performance and adaptability
      • Stress Tolerance Screening -- Evaluation of plant responses to abiotic and biotic stresses
        • drought tolerance 33 -- plant ability to maintain productivity under water stress
        • drought tolerance screening 2 -- systematic evaluation of crop varieties for resistance to water stress
        • heat stress tolerance 7 -- plant ability to maintain performance under high temperature conditions
        • stress tolerance 8 -- ability of crops to maintain productivity under abiotic or biotic stress conditions
        • stress tolerance indices 2 -- mathematical measures used to quantify plant performance under stress conditions
        • multi-stress tolerance 1 -- plant resistance to multiple biotic and abiotic stresses
        • terminal heat stress 2 -- high temperature exposure during grain filling that reduces crop yields
        • flood tolerance 1 -- ability of crops to survive and produce under flooding conditions
        • salinity tolerance 1 -- plant ability to grow and function in high-salt environments
        • disease resistance screening 4 -- systematic evaluation of genetic materials for pathogen tolerance
        • pathotype screening 1 -- testing plant materials against different strains of pathogens to assess resistance
        • host plant resistance 1 -- natural ability of plants to resist or tolerate pest and disease attacks
        • drought experiments 1 -- controlled research trials to evaluate crop performance under water stress conditions
      • Trait Characterization and Analysis -- Assessment and analysis of specific plant traits
        • trait preferences 3 -- farmer and stakeholder preferences for specific crop characteristics
        • trait deployment 3 -- process of incorporating beneficial genetic traits into crop varieties through breeding programs
        • trait development pipeline 1 -- systematic framework for translating research discoveries into breeding applications
        • trait preference ranking 1 -- systematic evaluation and ordering of livestock or crop characteristics based on stakeholder priorities
        • morphological characterization 4 -- systematic description of physical traits in crop varieties or landraces
        • heritability estimation 2 -- quantification of genetic contribution to trait variation
        • heritability metrics 2 -- statistical measures quantifying genetic versus environmental contribution to trait variation
        • yield stability 2 -- consistent crop performance across different environments
        • yield improvement 6 -- increase in crop productivity per unit area through improved practices
        • grain quality 2 -- nutritional and processing characteristics of cereal grains
        • grain quality assessment 1 -- evaluation of physical and chemical properties affecting grain value
        • root system architecture 3 -- spatial configuration and development of plant root systems
        • senescence rate 2 -- speed of plant aging and chlorophyll degradation
        • nitrogen use efficiency 5 -- crop's ability to convert applied nitrogen into grain yield
        • photoperiod effects 1 -- influence of day length variations on plant growth and flowering cycles
        • anthocyanin content evaluation 1 -- measurement of pigment compounds in plant tissues
        • protein digestibility 1 -- measure of how well proteins can be broken down and absorbed by the body
        • cyanogenesis 1 -- biochemical process of hydrogen cyanide production in plants
        • glycemic index modification 1 -- breeding strategy to alter blood sugar response of food crops
        • nutritional enhancement 5 -- improving the nutritional content of foods through ingredient modification or fortification
        • nutritional quality assessment 2 -- evaluation of nutrient content and bioavailability in crop products
        • herbicide tolerance 2 -- genetic trait allowing crops to survive herbicide application for weed control
        • rust resistance 1 -- plant ability to defend against fungal rust pathogens
        • striga resistance 1 -- crop ability to withstand parasitic striga weed infestation
        • late blight resistance 1 -- plant trait providing protection against phytophthora infestans pathogen
      • Genetic Gain and Performance Assessment -- Measuring genetic progress and breeding performance
        • genetic gain 5 -- rate of improvement in crop performance through breeding
        • genetic gain assessment 1 -- standardized measurement of breeding progress across crops and traits
        • realized genetic gain 1 -- actual improvement in crop traits achieved through breeding over time
        • yield gap analysis 3 -- assessment of differences between potential and actual crop yields
        • yield gap closure 3 -- bridging the difference between current farmer yields and attainable yields under optimal conditions
        • multi-trait selection index 1 -- breeding tool that combines multiple traits into single selection criterion
    • Germplasm and Genetic Resources -- Conservation, characterization, and utilization of genetic diversity including gene banks and germplasm collections
      • Germplasm Conservation and Management -- Methods for preserving and managing genetic resources
        • germplasm utilization 28 -- use of genetic resources from gene banks or research collections for crop improvement
        • germplasm conservation 1 -- preservation of genetic resources for crop improvement and research
        • germplasm screening 1 -- systematic evaluation of genetic material for desired traits
        • germplasm repatriation 1 -- return of genetic material to countries or institutions of origin
        • cryopreservation 10 -- preservation of plant genetic material at ultra-low temperatures
        • seed conservation 5 -- preservation and maintenance of crop genetic diversity through seed storage and management
        • on-farm conservation 3 -- maintaining crop genetic diversity through cultivation on farms
        • indigenous variety conservation 2 -- preservation and promotion of traditional crop varieties
        • community seed banks 4 -- locally managed seed conservation and distribution systems
        • droplet vitrification 1 -- cryopreservation technique using rapid cooling of small tissue samples
      • Genetic Diversity Assessment -- Characterization and analysis of genetic diversity
        • genetic diversity assessment 2 -- evaluation of genetic variation within crop populations
        • genomic profiling 1 -- comprehensive analysis of genetic material to identify specific traits or pathogens
        • phylogenetic analysis 1 -- study of evolutionary relationships between organisms using genetic data
        • flow cytometry 1 -- laboratory technique for determining chromosome numbers and analyzing cellular characteristics
      • Vegetative Propagation and Tissue Culture -- Methods for clonal propagation and in vitro culture
        • vegetative propagation 3 -- plant reproduction using vegetative parts rather than seeds
        • tissue culture propagation 2 -- laboratory-based method for producing disease-free plant material through sterile culture techniques
        • in vitro culture 1 -- laboratory technique for growing plant tissues under sterile conditions
        • macro-propagation 1 -- rapid multiplication of planting materials under controlled conditions
        • macro-propagation techniques 1 -- methods for rapid multiplication of planting materials using vegetative propagation
        • rapid multiplication technology 1 -- accelerated propagation techniques for plant breeding materials
        • rapid stem multiplication 1 -- accelerated propagation technique for vegetatively reproduced crops using controlled environment systems
        • apical rooted cuttings propagation 2 -- vegetative propagation method using shoot tips to produce disease-free planting material
        • rooted apical cuttings 1 -- vegetative propagation technique using rooted shoot tips for plant multiplication
        • leaf-bud cutting propagation 1 -- vegetative reproduction technique using leaf-bud segments
        • virus elimination protocols 2 -- procedures to remove viral infections from plant germplasm
    • Variety Development and Release -- Processes for variety testing, registration, licensing, and deployment of improved varieties to farmers
      • Variety Registration and Licensing -- Regulatory processes for variety approval and commercialization
        • variety registration 52 -- official approval of new varieties for commercial release
        • varietal registration 1 -- official process to approve and license new crop varieties for commercial use
        • variety release procedures 3 -- formal processes for approving and distributing new crop varieties
        • variety licensing 4 -- commercialization strategy where public research institutions license improved varieties to private sector
        • variety positioning 1 -- strategic placement of improved crop varieties in formal seed systems for farmer adoption
        • varietal turnover 2 -- replacement of older crop varieties with newer improved varieties by farmers
        • varietal turnover acceleration 1 -- strategies to speed replacement of older crop varieties with improved ones
      • Variety Adoption and Deployment -- Processes for getting varieties to farmers and tracking adoption
        • varietal adoption 8 -- process by which farmers select and use new crop varieties
        • improved seed adoption 2 -- process by which farmers take up and use enhanced crop varieties
        • hybrid adoption 1 -- uptake and use of hybrid crop varieties by farmers
        • varietal demonstrations 2 -- field trials showcasing performance of different crop varieties to farmers
        • variety demonstration 1 -- field-based showcasing of crop varieties to promote adoption
        • varietal tracking 1 -- monitoring adoption and distribution of specific crop varieties
        • variety trial 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop or forage varieties across different environments
      • Target Population and Market Segmentation -- Defining target environments and market segments for varieties
        • target population of environments 1 -- characterization of growing environments to guide crop variety development and deployment strategies
        • seed product market segmentation 2 -- systematic categorization of seed markets based on standardized criteria
        • recommendation domains 1 -- geographic areas with similar conditions suitable for specific technology adoption
        • product design teams 1 -- structured approach bringing together diverse expertise to make informed crop variety advancement decisions
    • Seed Systems and Seed Supply -- Seed production, certification, distribution, quality testing, and seed system strengthening across the value chain
      • Seed Production -- Production of quality seed at various levels
        • seed production 6 -- organized process of producing quality seeds for agricultural use
        • seed production systems 3 -- organized networks for producing and distributing quality crop seeds
        • early generation seed production 5 -- multiplication of breeder and foundation seed by private sector
        • early generation seed supply 3 -- provision of foundation seed from breeding programs to seed producers
        • community-based seed production 4 -- decentralized seed production and marketing managed by local communities
        • certified seed production 3 -- formal system for producing seeds that meet quality standards and certification requirements
        • quality seed production 2 -- systematic production of seed meeting genetic purity and health standards
        • market-driven seed production 1 -- seed multiplication based on trader and consumer demand
        • clean seed systems 5 -- formal networks for producing and distributing disease-free planting materials
      • Seed Quality and Certification -- Standards and processes for ensuring seed quality
        • seed certification 4 -- formal quality assurance system for verifying seed quality and genetic purity
        • seed certification system 1 -- quality assurance framework ensuring genetic purity and health of planting materials
        • seed certification regulations 1 -- legal frameworks ensuring seed quality and standards
        • seed health testing 2 -- protocols for detecting pathogens and ensuring seed quality
        • seed quality testing 1 -- assessment of seed viability, purity, and health for conservation and distribution
        • quality declared seed 1 -- seed certification system ensuring genetic purity and quality standards
        • phytosanitary testing 1 -- laboratory procedures to detect plant pathogens and ensure plant health
        • phytosanitary measures 2 -- regulations and practices to prevent spread of plant pests and diseases
        • phytosanitary regulation 1 -- policies and standards for preventing plant disease spread through seed systems
        • virus indexing 1 -- systematic testing to detect and identify viral pathogens in plant materials
        • seed priming 1 -- pre-sowing treatment to enhance seed germination and seedling vigor
        • fungicidal seed treatment 2 -- application of fungicides to seeds before planting to control seed-borne diseases
        • seed treatment protocols 1 -- standardized procedures for treating seeds with fungicides to prevent disease
        • seed dormancy breaking 1 -- treatments to overcome seed dormancy and promote germination
      • Seed System Development and Strengthening -- Building and improving seed systems
        • seed systems 10 -- networks of actors, institutions, and processes for seed production and distribution
        • seed system strengthening 9 -- interventions to improve production, distribution, and access to quality planting materials
        • seed system development 1 -- strategies to improve seed production and distribution networks
        • seed system modernization 1 -- improvements to seed production, distribution, and quality assurance for vegetatively propagated crops
        • seed system innovations 1 -- improved methods and technologies for crop seed production and distribution
        • seed sector development 1 -- systematic strengthening of seed production, distribution, and regulatory systems
        • seed roadmap development 2 -- strategic planning process for seed sector development
        • community seed systems 1 -- local networks for producing, storing, and distributing seeds
        • community-based seed systems 1 -- local seed production and distribution networks managed by farming communities
        • community seed schemes 1 -- local seed production and distribution systems operated by farming communities
        • trader-centered seed systems 1 -- seed distribution networks that place traders at the center of connecting producers with farmers
      • Seed Distribution and Delivery -- Systems for getting seed to farmers
        • seed distribution systems 2 -- networks and mechanisms for delivering seeds to farmers
        • seed delivery network 1 -- coordinated system of organizations that multiply and distribute improved crop varieties to farmers
        • direct seed marketing 1 -- policy approach allowing seed producers to sell directly to farmers bypassing state distribution
        • market-driven seed delivery 1 -- seed distribution system responsive to farmer and trader market demands rather than supply-driven
        • last-mile distribution 1 -- final stage of product delivery from businesses to end-users in rural areas
        • seed demand projection 1 -- forecasting future requirements for crop planting materials
        • seed requirement estimation 1 -- data-driven planning for seed production and distribution
        • on-farm seed storage 1 -- farmer-managed preservation of seeds between harvest and planting
        • digital seed inventory management 1 -- systematic approach to tracking and managing seed stocks using digital platforms
        • seed tracking 1 -- monitoring system for certified seed production and distribution
  • Sustainable Farming Systems and Practices -- Covers farming approaches, agronomic practices, and integrated systems that promote productivity, sustainability, and resilience
    • Agroecology and Regenerative Practices -- Agroecological principles, regenerative agriculture, organic farming, and nature-positive production approaches
      • Core Agroecological Approaches -- Foundational agroecological and regenerative farming concepts
        • agroecology 23 -- ecological approach to agriculture that applies ecological principles to farming systems
        • agroecological practices 12 -- farming methods that integrate ecological principles for sustainability
        • agroecological transition 29 -- shift from conventional to sustainable farming practices that work with natural ecosystems
        • agroecology transition 2 -- systematic shift from conventional to ecological agricultural practices and systems
        • regenerative agriculture 12 -- farming practices aimed at rebuilding soil health and enhancing ecosystem function
        • regenerative agricultural practices 1 -- farming methods that restore soil health and ecosystem function through practices like no-till, cover cropping, and crop rotation
        • nature-positive agriculture 3 -- farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem services while maintaining productivity
        • sustainable agriculture 7 -- farming practices that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
        • sustainable agricultural practices 6 -- farming methods that maintain productivity while conserving resources and enhancing resilience
        • agroecological stewardship 4 -- practices that promote environmental sustainability and ecological health in agricultural systems
        • agroecological solutions 1 -- sustainable farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem functions
        • transformational agroecology 1 -- transition toward environmentally sustainable, productive, and socially just agri-food systems
        • agroecological business modeling 1 -- development of sustainable business models based on ecological principles
        • agroecological assessment 2 -- evaluation of agricultural systems using agroecological principles
        • agroecological performance 1 -- measurement of farming system sustainability across multiple dimensions
      • Organic and Circular Farming -- Organic farming, certification, and circular bioeconomy approaches
        • organic farming 5 -- agricultural production system avoiding synthetic inputs and emphasizing natural processes
        • organic certification 4 -- formal verification process ensuring agricultural products meet organic production standards
        • circular bioeconomy 11 -- economic model using biological resources in circular value chains
        • circular economy 7 -- economic model emphasizing resource reuse and waste reduction
        • circular economy solutions 2 -- waste-to-value systems that convert agricultural residues into useful products
        • circular economy approaches 1 -- resource utilization strategies that minimize waste through reuse and recycling
        • circular economy principles 1 -- resource management strategies that minimize waste and maximize nutrient retention within production systems
        • bioinput preparation 4 -- on-farm production of organic fertilizers and pest control agents from locally available materials
        • biological inputs 1 -- environmentally friendly alternatives to chemical fertilizers and pesticides
        • composting 1 -- biological decomposition of organic matter into nutrient-rich soil amendment
        • systematic composting 2 -- structured method for producing compost using organic materials and training
        • vermicompost production 1 -- composting process using earthworms to decompose organic materials
        • aerobic composting 1 -- controlled decomposition of organic matter in the presence of oxygen to produce fertilizer
        • biochar application 2 -- use of charcoal-like material to improve soil properties and carbon sequestration
        • green manuring 1 -- practice of growing and incorporating plants to improve soil fertility
        • bioslurry application 1 -- use of organic waste slurry as fertilizer to enhance crop production and reduce emissions
      • Nature-Based and Ecosystem Approaches -- Nature-based solutions, ecosystem services, and biodiversity integration in farming
        • nature-based solutions 9 -- interventions that use natural processes to address environmental and development challenges
        • ecosystem restoration 9 -- rehabilitation of degraded landscapes to restore ecological functions and services
        • biodiversity conservation 6 -- protection and sustainable management of biological diversity
        • agrobiodiversity conservation 3 -- preservation of genetic diversity in agricultural systems
        • agrobiodiversity monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking of crop diversity and genetic resources in food systems
        • systematic agrobiodiversity monitoring 2 -- standardized tracking of crop diversity conservation status over time and space
        • agrobiodiversity mainstreaming 1 -- integration of crop diversity concepts into education and policy systems
        • on-farm conservation 3 -- maintaining crop genetic diversity through cultivation on farms
        • payment for ecosystem services 2 -- economic mechanism compensating land managers for environmental benefits
        • payment for environmental services 2 -- economic mechanism that compensates land users for providing ecosystem services
        • payments for ecosystem services 1 -- economic incentives for conservation and environmental stewardship
        • payments for environmental services 1 -- economic incentive schemes that reward farmers for environmental stewardship
        • ecosystem services valuation 2 -- quantification and monetization of environmental benefits
        • ecosystem service valuation 1 -- economic quantification of benefits provided by natural systems
        • pollinator protection 1 -- conservation strategies to support pollinating insects
        • habitat enhancement 1 -- modification of agricultural landscapes to support beneficial organisms
        • biodiversity integration 1 -- incorporation of biological diversity principles into agricultural systems
        • biodiversity restoration 1 -- recovery of native species diversity and ecosystem function in degraded landscapes
        • land sparing 2 -- conservation strategy separating intensive agriculture from protected natural areas
      • Climate-Smart Agriculture -- Climate-smart farming approaches and bundled CSA practices
        • climate-smart agriculture 230 -- farming practices that address climate change while improving productivity
        • climate-smart practices 2 -- agricultural techniques that adapt to and mitigate climate change
        • climate-smart villages 3 -- community-based research sites for testing and demonstrating climate adaptation technologies
        • climate-smart village approach 4 -- integrated community-level framework for implementing climate-smart agriculture
        • climate-resilient agriculture 5 -- farming practices adapted to withstand climate variability and extreme weather
        • climate-resilient farming 3 -- agricultural practices that help farmers adapt to climate change impacts
        • bundled cis and csa 5 -- integrated packages combining climate information with smart agriculture practices
        • bundled csa practices 1 -- integrated packages of climate-smart agricultural interventions implemented at scale
        • bundled climate-smart solutions 3 -- integrated package of climate adaptation and mitigation practices for agriculture
        • climate-smart interventions 1 -- agricultural practices that enhance productivity while building climate resilience
        • low-carbon agriculture 2 -- agricultural systems designed to minimize greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • low-emission agriculture 3 -- farming practices designed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • zero-carbon agriculture 3 -- farming systems designed to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions
        • sustainable intensification 5 -- increasing agricultural productivity while maintaining environmental sustainability
        • good agricultural practices 9 -- science-backed farming techniques including timely planting, optimal fertilizer rates, and improved varieties
        • good agronomic practices 2 -- evidence-based crop management techniques for optimal production
        • sustainable production practices 1 -- farming methods that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
    • Integrated Crop and Soil Management -- Crop rotation, intercropping, conservation agriculture, soil health management, and integrated nutrient management
      • Conservation Agriculture and Tillage -- Conservation agriculture practices, tillage management, and crop residue management
        • conservation agriculture 22 -- farming system based on minimal soil disturbance, permanent soil cover and crop rotation
        • zero tillage 3 -- farming practice that eliminates mechanical soil disturbance for planting
        • zero tillage production 2 -- agricultural practice of growing crops without mechanically disturbing the soil
        • zero tillage cultivation 1 -- agricultural practice of growing crops without disturbing soil through tillage
        • no-till planting 1 -- planting method that places seeds directly into soil without prior tillage
        • no-tillage farming 1 -- agricultural practice that avoids mechanical soil disturbance to preserve soil structure and moisture
        • no-till conservation agriculture 1 -- farming practice that maintains soil cover and reduces erosion by avoiding tillage
        • minimum tillage 1 -- reduced soil cultivation practice to maintain soil structure
        • conventional tillage 1 -- traditional farming practice involving mechanical soil preparation through plowing and cultivation
        • crop residue management 1 -- practices for handling plant material left after harvest to improve soil health and sustainability
        • straw management 1 -- practices for handling crop residues to optimize benefits and reduce emissions
        • rice straw transformation 1 -- converting rice straw waste into valuable products for circular economy applications
      • Cropping Systems -- Crop rotation, intercropping, diversification, and cropping system design
        • crop rotation 1 -- sequential cultivation of different crops to improve soil health and productivity
        • intercropping 7 -- growing multiple crop species simultaneously in the same field
        • intercropping systems 4 -- agricultural practice of growing multiple crops simultaneously in the same field
        • crop diversification 21 -- agricultural strategy of growing multiple crop species to reduce risk and improve sustainability
        • cropping system diversification 1 -- practice of growing multiple crop species to improve system resilience and productivity
        • cropping systems design 1 -- systematic approach to planning and optimizing agricultural production systems
        • integrated crop management 4 -- holistic farming approach combining pest, disease, and soil health management practices
        • integrated farming systems 12 -- agricultural approach combining multiple enterprises for synergistic benefits
        • integrated production bundle 4 -- coherent package of agronomic practices, inputs, and management recommendations for specific crops
        • rice-based diversification 1 -- cropping systems that combine rice with other crops to enhance resilience and productivity
        • rice-fallow intensification 2 -- agricultural practice of growing crops in previously fallow rice fields
        • crop intensification 2 -- increasing agricultural productivity per unit area through improved practices
        • agricultural intensification 5 -- increasing production per unit area through enhanced inputs and practices
        • direct-seeded rice 28 -- rice cultivation technique where seeds are sown directly in fields without transplanting
        • mechanized direct seeding 4 -- agricultural practice using machinery to plant seeds directly into soil
        • mechanized direct-seeded rice 4 -- climate-smart rice cultivation technology using machines for precision sowing instead of transplanting
        • direct seeding 1 -- planting seeds directly in the field without transplanting seedlings
        • direct seeded rice breeding 1 -- development of rice varieties optimized for direct planting methods
        • puddled transplanted rice 1 -- traditional rice cultivation involving seedbed preparation and transplanting in puddled soil
        • mechanical transplanting 2 -- use of machines to plant seedlings instead of manual labor
        • precision sowing 2 -- accurate placement of seeds using mechanized equipment for optimal crop establishment
        • precision seeding 1 -- accurate placement of seeds at optimal spacing and depth using specialized equipment
        • rotational fallow practices 1 -- traditional land management alternating cultivation with rest periods for soil regeneration
        • sustainable rice cultivation 2 -- rice production practices that balance environmental, economic and social considerations
        • low-emission rice production 1 -- agricultural practices designed to reduce methane emissions from rice cultivation
        • perennial vegetable production 1 -- cultivation of long-lived vegetable crops that produce over multiple seasons
        • vegetable production 1 -- cultivation and management of vegetable crops
        • home gardening 1 -- small-scale food production at household level for nutrition and food security
        • kitchen gardening 1 -- small-scale vegetable production for household nutrition and income
        • home garden intensification 1 -- systematic enhancement of household food production through integrated crop-livestock systems
        • urban farming 2 -- agricultural production within urban environments to enhance local food security
        • urban agriculture 2 -- food production activities within urban and peri-urban areas
        • floating agriculture 2 -- cultivation on floating beds in waterlogged areas
        • desert farming 1 -- agricultural production in arid environments using specialized techniques
        • guerrilla gardening 3 -- informal cultivation of plants in public or underutilized spaces
      • Soil Health and Fertility Management -- Soil health assessment, fertility management, and nutrient management practices
        • soil health 4 -- capacity of soil to function as a living ecosystem supporting plant and animal productivity
        • soil health management 4 -- practices to maintain and improve soil biological, chemical, and physical properties
        • soil health assessment 7 -- evaluation of soil physical, chemical, and biological properties for agricultural sustainability
        • soil health improvement 2 -- enhancement of soil biological, chemical, and physical properties for sustainable productivity
        • soil health monitoring 2 -- systematic assessment of soil biological, chemical, and physical properties for management decisions
        • soil health diagnostics 1 -- systematic assessment of soil physical, chemical and biological properties
        • soil fertility management 2 -- practices to maintain and improve soil nutrient status
        • integrated soil fertility management 4 -- combined use of fertilizers, organic resources, and improved practices for soil health
        • integrated nutrient management 1 -- holistic approach to managing soil nutrients using multiple sources
        • nutrient management 2 -- strategic application of fertilizers and organic matter to optimize crop nutrition and minimize environmental impact
        • nitrogen management 3 -- optimization of nitrogen fertilizer application rates and timing
        • nitrogen use efficiency 5 -- crop's ability to convert applied nitrogen into grain yield
        • biological nitrification inhibition 3 -- natural process that hinders soil nitrification to improve nitrogen use efficiency
        • nitrogen fixation 2 -- biological process converting atmospheric nitrogen to plant-available forms
        • site-specific fertilizer recommendations 15 -- customized nutrient management based on local soil and climate conditions
        • site-specific fertilizer advisory 3 -- precision nutrient management recommendations based on landscape and soil conditions
        • site-specific recommendations 5 -- tailored agricultural advice based on local conditions and farmer circumstances
        • field-specific nutrient management 3 -- customized fertilizer recommendations based on individual field characteristics and conditions
        • balanced nutrient management 1 -- optimized fertilizer application strategy to improve crop productivity and resource efficiency
        • precision nitrogen management 1 -- optimized fertilizer application matching nitrogen rates to specific field conditions
        • fertilizer deep placement 1 -- precision application of fertilizers at optimal soil depths to improve efficiency
        • blanket fertilizer recommendation 1 -- uniform fertilizer application strategy applied across broad geographic areas
        • soil organic carbon sequestration 2 -- process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon in soil through organic matter accumulation
        • soil organic carbon monitoring 2 -- measurement and tracking of carbon content in agricultural soils
        • soil organic carbon mapping 1 -- spatial assessment of carbon content in soils
        • mineral-associated organic carbon 1 -- soil carbon fraction bound to mineral particles that provides long-term carbon storage
        • soil and water conservation 1 -- practices and technologies to protect soil resources and manage water sustainably
        • soil diagnostics 1 -- field-based assessment of soil physical, chemical, and biological characteristics
        • soil quality index 1 -- composite measure of soil health based on multiple indicators
        • soil fertility assessment 1 -- evaluation of soil nutrient content and quality for agricultural decision-making
        • digital soil mapping 5 -- use of digital tools and algorithms to map soil properties spatially
        • digital soil sensing 1 -- portable field-based technology for rapid soil property assessment
        • soil spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid, non-destructive assessment of soil properties
        • soil moisture monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking of soil water content using remote sensing or field measurements
        • soil erosion risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas prone to soil loss and degradation
        • soil bulk density measurement 1 -- techniques for measuring soil compaction to assess soil health and carbon stocks
        • long-term experiments 1 -- extended research studies conducted over multiple years to assess agricultural and environmental outcomes
      • Precision and Digital Crop Management -- Precision agriculture, digital tools, and data-driven crop management
        • precision agriculture 13 -- data-driven farming approach using technology for site-specific crop management
        • precision farming 1 -- agricultural management approach using technology to optimize field-level crop management
        • on-farm testing 16 -- evaluating varieties under actual farmer field conditions
        • on-farm trials 1 -- field research conducted on farmers' own land to test innovations under real conditions
        • on-farm verification 1 -- testing crop varieties under actual farmer conditions to validate performance
        • farmer field demonstrations 2 -- on-farm testing and showcasing of improved agricultural practices
        • farmer-led demonstration plots 3 -- on-farm trials managed by farmers for peer-to-peer learning and technology validation
        • varietal demonstrations 2 -- field trials showcasing performance of different crop varieties to farmers
        • variety demonstration 1 -- field-based showcasing of crop varieties to promote adoption
        • crop modeling 7 -- mathematical simulation of crop growth and yield under different environmental and management conditions
        • crop simulation modeling 3 -- computational tools for predicting crop growth, yield, and environmental impacts under different scenarios
        • crop growth modeling 3 -- computational simulation of plant development and yield prediction
        • yield gap analysis 3 -- assessment of differences between potential and actual crop yields
        • yield gap closure 3 -- bridging the difference between current farmer yields and attainable yields under optimal conditions
        • yield improvement 6 -- increase in crop productivity per unit area through improved practices
        • yield stability 2 -- consistent crop performance across different environments
        • yield projection 2 -- forecasting future crop production based on various biophysical and socioeconomic factors
        • yield prediction modeling 1 -- use of mathematical models to forecast agricultural crop yields
        • crop monitoring 2 -- systematic observation and assessment of crop growth and conditions
        • satellite-based crop monitoring 4 -- use of satellite imagery and vegetation indices to monitor crop development and phenology
        • drone-based crop monitoring 1 -- use of unmanned aerial vehicles with sensors to assess crop health and growth
        • crop type mapping 2 -- remote sensing identification of agricultural land use patterns
        • data-driven agriculture 3 -- agricultural decision-making based on systematic analysis of climatic, soil, and agronomic data using computational methods
        • digital farming tools 3 -- technology platforms that support agricultural decision-making and farmer participation in sustainable practices
        • decision support systems 11 -- computer-based tools providing information to aid decision-making
        • decision-support tools 4 -- digital platforms and frameworks that assist in agricultural decision-making
        • site-specific recommendations 5 -- tailored agricultural advice based on local conditions and farmer circumstances
        • location-specific recommendations 2 -- tailored agricultural advice based on local environmental and crop conditions
        • smart valleys approach 5 -- integrated water control system for inland valley rice production
        • smart valley farming 1 -- participatory approach targeting improved soil and water management in inland valleys
        • whole-farm innovation bundles 3 -- integrated packages of complementary technologies and practices for entire farming systems
        • socio-technical innovation bundles 4 -- integrated packages combining technological innovations with social interventions
        • integrated technology package 1 -- bundled interventions combining multiple technologies to address interconnected challenges
        • innovation bundling 5 -- integrating technical, digital and institutional solutions to address interconnected challenges
        • bundled innovations 2 -- integrated packages combining multiple complementary technologies and practices
        • innovation package 2 -- bundled set of technologies, practices and services designed to achieve specific outcomes
        • technology bundling 1 -- integrated package of complementary technologies and services for scaling
      • Agricultural Mechanization -- Farm mechanization, mechanized services, and scale-appropriate mechanization
        • agricultural mechanization 4 -- introduction of machinery to replace manual labor in farming operations
        • mechanized agriculture 5 -- use of machinery to perform agricultural operations
        • small-scale mechanization 3 -- use of small machinery like power tillers and mowers in agricultural production
        • scale-appropriate mechanization 3 -- agricultural machinery and tools sized and designed for smallholder farming systems
        • mechanization services 2 -- provision of agricultural machinery and equipment services to farmers
        • mechanization service provider model 2 -- business model where farmers provide mechanization services to other farmers for a fee
        • mechanized agriculture services 2 -- provision of machinery and equipment for agricultural operations
        • sustainable agricultural mechanization 2 -- use of machinery and equipment that reduces environmental impact while improving productivity
        • mechanization hire services 1 -- business model providing agricultural machinery access to farmers through rental arrangements
      • Carbon and Greenhouse Gas Management in Farming -- Carbon sequestration, GHG measurement, and emission reduction in crop systems
        • carbon sequestration 10 -- process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide in soils and vegetation
        • ecosystem carbon sequestration 3 -- natural processes by which ecosystems capture and store atmospheric carbon
        • carbon accounting 4 -- systematic measurement and tracking of carbon emissions and sequestration
        • carbon footprint assessment 3 -- quantification of greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural production systems
        • greenhouse gas emissions 21 -- release of gases that contribute to climate change from agricultural activities
        • greenhouse gas measurement 12 -- quantification of emissions from agricultural systems using standardized protocols and equipment
        • greenhouse gas emission measurement 3 -- techniques to quantify agricultural emissions for climate action
        • greenhouse gas emissions measurement 2 -- quantification of carbon dioxide, methane and nitrous oxide emissions from agricultural systems
        • greenhouse gas emissions quantification 1 -- systematic measurement and assessment of agricultural greenhouse gas emissions using bottom-up approaches
        • greenhouse gas emission reduction 2 -- mitigation of climate-warming gases from agricultural practices
        • greenhouse gas inventory 2 -- systematic accounting of emissions and removals by sources and sinks
        • greenhouse gas budgets 1 -- comprehensive accounting of anthropogenic and natural sources and sinks of greenhouse gases
        • emission factors 4 -- standardized coefficients used to estimate greenhouse gas emissions from activities
        • emission intensity 1 -- greenhouse gas emissions per unit of agricultural output
        • emissions monitoring 4 -- systematic measurement and verification of greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural activities
        • emission monitoring systems 3 -- tools and frameworks for measuring and reporting greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural activities
        • monitoring reporting verification 7 -- framework for tracking and validating environmental compliance
        • monitoring, reporting, and verification 3 -- systems for tracking and validating climate action progress
        • monitoring reporting and verification 1 -- systematic tracking and validation of environmental and agricultural outcomes
        • ipcc tier 2 methodology 3 -- standardized approach for estimating greenhouse gas emissions using country-specific parameters
        • eddy covariance 1 -- micrometeorological technique for measuring ecosystem-scale carbon dioxide and water vapor fluxes
        • carbon uptake 1 -- process by which ecosystems absorb atmospheric carbon dioxide through photosynthesis
        • carbon neutrality 1 -- achieving net-zero carbon dioxide emissions
        • carbon trading 3 -- market-based mechanism for trading carbon credits to offset greenhouse gas emissions
        • carbon credit certification 2 -- formal verification process that enables farmers to monetize emission reductions through carbon markets
        • carbon market readiness 1 -- capacity building to enable participation in carbon trading mechanisms
        • low-carbon behaviors 3 -- individual and household actions that reduce greenhouse gas emissions
        • life cycle assessment 1 -- evaluation of environmental impacts throughout a product's entire life cycle
        • water footprint assessment 1 -- evaluation of green and blue water consumption patterns in agricultural systems
        • true cost accounting 1 -- comprehensive assessment including environmental and social costs of food production
    • Irrigation and Water Management -- Water-saving irrigation technologies, water productivity, agricultural water management, and irrigation systems
      • Irrigation Systems and Technologies -- Irrigation methods, technologies, and water-saving approaches
        • alternate wetting and drying 22 -- water-saving rice cultivation technique alternating between flooded and non-flooded field conditions

        • solar-based irrigation 2 -- water delivery system powered by solar energy for agricultural production

        • drip irrigation 1 -- water-efficient irrigation method that delivers water directly to plant roots
        • micro-drip irrigation 1 -- water-efficient irrigation method delivering small amounts of water directly to plant roots
        • small-scale irrigation 3 -- water management systems for smallholder agricultural production
        • supplemental irrigation 2 -- strategic water application to supplement rainfall in dryland systems
        • supplementary irrigation 1 -- targeted water application during critical crop growth stages in rainfed systems
        • farmer-led irrigation development 2 -- smallholder-driven investment and management of irrigation systems

        • water-as-a-service 1 -- business model providing irrigation services rather than selling equipment
        • water-saving irrigation 1 -- irrigation techniques that reduce water consumption while maintaining crop productivity
        • water-saving technologies 1 -- innovations and practices that reduce agricultural water consumption

        • agri-voltaic systems 1 -- co-location of solar panels and agricultural production
      • Water Productivity and Efficiency -- Water use efficiency, productivity, and water-efficient farming practices
        • water productivity 10 -- efficiency of water use in producing agricultural output per unit of water consumed

        • irrigation water productivity 2 -- measure of crop yield per unit of irrigation water applied
        • water productivity optimization 1 -- maximizing agricultural output per unit of water used
        • water-efficient farming 3 -- agricultural techniques that optimize water use while maintaining or improving crop productivity
        • water-efficient practices 2 -- techniques and methods to optimize water use in agricultural systems
        • water-efficient agriculture 2 -- farming practices that optimize water use while maintaining productivity
        • nitrogen use efficiency 5 -- crop's ability to convert applied nitrogen into grain yield

        • water management 8 -- strategies for efficient use and conservation of water resources in agriculture

        • integrated water resources management 2 -- coordinated development and management of water, land and related resources
        • groundwater management 5 -- sustainable use and conservation of underground water resources
        • groundwater governance 2 -- institutional frameworks and practices for sustainable groundwater resource management
        • groundwater recharge schemes 1 -- interventions designed to replenish depleted groundwater resources
        • groundwater integration 1 -- systematic inclusion of groundwater considerations in water service planning
        • managed aquifer recharge 2 -- intentional replenishment of groundwater through controlled water infiltration
        • transboundary water management 3 -- coordinated governance of water resources across national or regional boundaries
        • water accounting 9 -- systematic assessment and monitoring of water resources and their utilization
        • water balance model 2 -- computational framework quantifying water inputs, outputs, and storage changes in agricultural systems
        • water governance 1 -- institutional frameworks and processes for managing water resources
        • inclusive water governance 2 -- water management frameworks that ensure equitable participation and benefit-sharing
        • gender-inclusive water management 1 -- water governance approaches that ensure equitable participation of women in decision-making
        • water resilience planning 4 -- strategic planning to ensure water security under climate change

        • water-energy-food-ecosystems nexus 3 -- integrated approach addressing interconnections between water, energy, food, and ecosystem management
        • water-energy-food-ecosystem nexus 2 -- integrated management of interconnected resource systems
        • integrated catchment management 1 -- landscape-level approach integrating land, water, forestry and livestock management
        • irrigation demand management 1 -- strategies to optimize water use efficiency in irrigation systems
        • irrigation demand forecasting 6 -- prediction of crop water requirements to optimize irrigation scheduling and water allocation
        • rainwater harvesting 3 -- collection and storage of rainwater for agricultural use
        • water harvesting 1 -- collection and storage of rainwater or surface water for agricultural use
        • floodwater management 1 -- strategies for controlling and utilizing flood waters for agricultural benefits
        • conjunctive water use 1 -- combined use of different water sources to optimize water resource management
        • polder zone management 1 -- agricultural and water management strategies for coastal reclaimed land areas
        • community-engaged sluice gate operation 1 -- participatory water management involving local communities in infrastructure control
        • drainage infrastructure 1 -- water management systems designed to remove excess water from agricultural land
        • constructed wetlands 1 -- engineered systems mimicking natural wetlands for water treatment
        • salinity management 2 -- strategies and technologies to address soil and water salinity in agricultural systems
        • salinity intrusion 3 -- movement of salt water into freshwater areas affecting soil and crop production
        • water quality monitoring 1 -- systematic assessment of water parameters to ensure suitability for aquaculture and other uses
        • hydrological modeling 3 -- mathematical simulation of water cycle processes for water resource planning and management
        • hydrological modelling 1 -- mathematical simulation of water cycle processes for water resource management
        • hydrological monitoring 1 -- systematic measurement and tracking of water levels and flow in water bodies
        • hydrological resilience 1 -- capacity of water systems to withstand and recover from disturbances
        • hydro-economic modeling 1 -- integrated analysis combining hydrological and economic data for water resource management
        • water monitoring system 1 -- integrated platform for tracking and analyzing water resource availability and use
        • digital water body inventory 1 -- interactive platform mapping and managing water infrastructure using satellite data
        • digital water innovations 1 -- technology-based solutions for water monitoring and management
        • evidence-based water resource management 1 -- water planning and allocation using data-driven analysis and modeling
        • integrated water storage assessment 1 -- comprehensive evaluation of multiple water storage types and their temporal dynamics for resource management
        • water consumption mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of water use patterns in agricultural systems
        • aquifer recharge zone mapping 1 -- identification and mapping of areas where groundwater can be naturally replenished
        • community-led water governance 1 -- participatory management of water resources through strategic infrastructure operation
        • global water governance 1 -- international frameworks and institutions for managing water resources across borders
        • water justice 1 -- equitable access to water resources and inclusive decision-making in water management
        • environmental flows 1 -- water flow regimes required to maintain healthy river ecosystems and their services
        • flood modeling 1 -- computational simulation of water flow patterns and flood behavior
        • flood prediction 1 -- forecasting approach to anticipate inundation extent and timing for agricultural risk management
        • flood risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas vulnerable to flooding and quantify risk levels
        • climate-resilient water infrastructure 1 -- water systems designed to withstand and adapt to climate change impacts
        • catchment risk and resilience index 1 -- integrated framework to assess vulnerabilities and adaptive capacity across river basins
        • reservoir forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling of future water storage levels using multiple data sources
        • watershed modeling 1 -- simulation of water flow and management at landscape scale
    • Pest, Disease, and Weed Management -- Integrated pest management, biological control, disease resistance, and phytosanitary measures
      • Integrated Pest Management -- IPM approaches, pest control strategies, and pest risk management
        • integrated pest management 20 -- holistic approach combining multiple pest control methods sustainably

        • push-pull technology 1 -- intercropping system using companion plants to repel pests and attract beneficial insects

        • pest risk identification 2 -- systematic surveillance and early warning systems for agricultural pest and disease threats
        • remote sensing pest detection 1 -- use of satellite imagery to identify and map crop pest infestations
        • weed control efficiency 1 -- measure of effectiveness in reducing weed populations and competition
        • herbicide tolerance 2 -- genetic trait allowing crops to survive herbicide application for weed control
        • herbicide injection 1 -- direct application of herbicides into plant tissue for targeted destruction
        • chemical control trials 1 -- field experiments testing effectiveness of chemical treatments for pest and disease management
        • oviposition repellency 1 -- deterrent effect on insect egg-laying behavior
        • striga resistance 1 -- crop ability to withstand parasitic striga weed infestation
      • Biological Control -- Biological control agents and biocontrol approaches
        • biological control 6 -- pest management using natural enemies and beneficial organisms

        • biocontrol 1 -- use of biological agents to control pests, diseases, or contaminants
        • parasitism rate 2 -- proportion of pest population attacked by parasitoid natural enemies
        • host plant resistance 1 -- natural ability of plants to resist or tolerate pest and disease attacks
      • Disease Management -- Disease resistance, disease management, and disease surveillance in crops
        • disease resistance 13 -- plant ability to defend against pathogen infection

        • disease resistance screening 4 -- systematic evaluation of genetic materials for pathogen tolerance
        • disease diagnosis 3 -- systematic identification and characterization of plant diseases
        • disease surveillance 4 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant diseases across geographic areas
        • pathotype screening 1 -- testing plant materials against different strains of pathogens to assess resistance
        • rust resistance 1 -- plant ability to defend against fungal rust pathogens
        • late blight resistance 1 -- plant trait providing protection against phytophthora infestans pathogen
        • multiple resistance gene deployment 1 -- breeding strategy combining several disease resistance genes in single varieties
        • fungicidal seed treatment 2 -- application of fungicides to seeds before planting to control seed-borne diseases
        • seed health testing 2 -- protocols for detecting pathogens and ensuring seed quality
        • phytosanitary measures 2 -- regulations and practices to prevent spread of plant pests and diseases
        • phytosanitary regulation 1 -- policies and standards for preventing plant disease spread through seed systems
        • phytosanitary testing 1 -- laboratory procedures to detect plant pathogens and ensure plant health
        • post-entry quarantine 1 -- controlled isolation and testing of introduced plant material to prevent pest and disease spread
        • integrated pathogen screening 1 -- comprehensive testing approach combining multiple methods to identify disease resistance
        • virus elimination protocols 2 -- procedures to remove viral infections from plant germplasm
        • virus indexing 1 -- systematic testing to detect and identify viral pathogens in plant materials
        • aflatoxin contamination 1 -- presence of toxic compounds produced by fungi that cause liver cancer and stunting

        • livestock production 3 -- agricultural system focused on raising animals for food, income, and other products

        • livestock intensification 1 -- increasing animal productivity through improved feeding, genetics, and management
        • sustainable cattle ranching 1 -- livestock production practices that minimize environmental impact while maintaining productivity
        • dairy production 1 -- livestock management system focused on milk production

        • estrus detection 1 -- identification of optimal breeding timing in livestock reproduction

        • reproductive biotechnology 1 -- application of biotechnological tools to monitor and improve reproductive performance in livestock
        • bodyweight estimation 1 -- prediction of animal weight using measurable physical characteristics
        • animal welfare assessment 1 -- evaluation of animal well-being in production systems
        • breed evaluation 1 -- systematic assessment of animal genetic resources for performance traits
        • average daily gain 1 -- measure of livestock weight increase per day
        • dry matter production 1 -- amount of dry biomass produced by plants or consumed by animals
        • livestock mobility 1 -- movement of animals along traditional grazing routes for feed and water access
        • wildlife farming 2 -- commercial raising of wild animal species for meat, medicine, or trade
