        • waste-to-feed conversion 1 -- process of converting agricultural by-products into livestock feed
        • bioconversion 1 -- biological transformation of organic materials into useful products through living organisms
      • Herd Health Management -- Animal health, disease management, and biosecurity in livestock systems
        • herd health management 4 -- comprehensive approach to maintaining animal health at population level
        • biosecurity 7 -- measures to prevent introduction and spread of diseases in livestock
        • biosecurity practices 2 -- preventive measures to reduce disease transmission in livestock production
        • biosecurity measures 1 -- practices to prevent introduction and spread of diseases in livestock systems
        • biosecurity assessment 1 -- evaluation of measures to prevent pathogen introduction and spread
        • biosafety protocols 2 -- standardized procedures to prevent biological contamination and exposure
        • biosafety regulatory compliance 1 -- adherence to safety protocols and regulations for genetically modified organisms
        • disease eradication 4 -- complete elimination of disease from affected areas through systematic removal
        • disease outbreak management 1 -- coordinated response to prevent and control disease spread
        • zoonotic disease management 2 -- prevention and control of diseases transmissible between animals and humans
        • zoonotic disease surveillance 6 -- monitoring and detection of diseases transmissible between animals and humans
        • zoonotic disease epidemiology 2 -- study of disease patterns and transmission dynamics between animals and humans
        • zoonotic disease transmission 1 -- spread of infectious diseases between animals and humans
        • viral disease surveillance 1 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant viral pathogens
        • antimicrobial resistance 5 -- ability of microorganisms to survive exposure to antimicrobial drugs
        • antimicrobial resistance surveillance 11 -- systematic monitoring of antimicrobial resistance patterns in pathogens to inform public health responses
        • antimicrobial stewardship 6 -- coordinated interventions to optimize antimicrobial use and minimize resistance development
        • antibiotic resistance monitoring 3 -- surveillance and measurement of antimicrobial resistance in bacterial populations
        • acaricide resistance 1 -- reduced effectiveness of chemical treatments against ticks due to genetic adaptation
        • thermotolerant vaccines 1 -- vaccines designed to remain stable at ambient temperatures without refrigeration
        • cold chain management 1 -- temperature-controlled supply chain for preserving vaccine potency
        • vaccine wastage 1 -- loss of vaccine doses due to handling, storage, or administration issues
        • mass vaccination campaign 1 -- large-scale immunization program targeting animal or human populations
        • serosurvey 1 -- epidemiological study measuring antibodies in blood samples
        • integrated herd health packages 1 -- comprehensive livestock health interventions using one health principles
        • animal health loss envelope 1 -- metric measuring financial burden of disease by comparing current vs ideal health scenarios
        • stochastic dynamic population modeling 1 -- probabilistic modeling approach for livestock population health and economics
        • livestock health extension 2 -- delivery of animal health knowledge and services to livestock producers
        • livestock early warning systems 1 -- integrated systems providing seasonal forecasts and decision support for pastoral livestock management
        • one health 6 -- integrated approach addressing interconnected human, animal, and environmental health
        • one health approach 30 -- collaborative framework integrating human, animal, and environmental health

        • feed management 3 -- strategies for optimizing animal nutrition and feed resource utilization

        • tropical forage breeding 4 -- development of improved forage varieties adapted to tropical conditions for livestock systems

        • feed and forage innovations 2 -- improved feeding technologies and practices for livestock in mixed farming systems

        • rangeland restoration 27 -- practices aimed at rehabilitating degraded rangeland ecosystems to improve productivity and sustainability

        • bush encroachment 1 -- invasion of grasslands by woody plants reducing grazing capacity
        • carrying capacity 1 -- maximum livestock density that land can sustainably support
        • herder-farmer conflict 3 -- violent conflict between nomadic herders and settled agricultural communities over land and resources
        • farmer-herder conflict 1 -- disputes between agricultural and pastoral communities over land and resource use
        • livestock mobility 1 -- movement of animals along traditional grazing routes for feed and water access
      • Livestock Methane and Emissions -- Methane mitigation, enteric fermentation, and emission reduction in livestock
        • methane mitigation 6 -- reduction of methane emissions from livestock systems
        • methane emissions 1 -- release of methane gas from livestock digestion processes
        • methane emission reduction 2 -- practices to decrease methane gas release from agricultural systems
        • enteric fermentation 2 -- digestive process in ruminants that produces methane as a byproduct
        • enteric methane reduction 1 -- strategies to reduce methane emissions from livestock digestion
        • enteric methane measurement 1 -- direct quantification of methane emissions from livestock digestive systems under field conditions
        • anti-methanogenic compounds 1 -- substances that reduce methane production in ruminant digestion
        • anti-methanogenic screening 1 -- laboratory testing to identify compounds that reduce methane production
        • emission intensity per weight gain 1 -- ratio of greenhouse gas emissions to livestock weight gain
        • biogas production 4 -- anaerobic digestion process converting organic matter to methane-rich gas
        • biogas digester performance 3 -- operational efficiency and gas production capacity of small-scale anaerobic digestion systems
        • biogas digester technology 1 -- anaerobic digestion system converting organic waste to energy
        • biomethane potential 1 -- measure of maximum methane yield from organic feedstock under anaerobic conditions
        • anaerobic digestion 1 -- biological process that breaks down organic matter in oxygen-free environment to produce biogas
        • co-digestion 1 -- anaerobic digestion of multiple organic feedstocks together to optimize biogas production
        • livestock waste recovery 1 -- conversion of animal manure into useful products like biogas or compost
      • Livestock Value Chains and Services -- Livestock value chains, market linkages, and service delivery for livestock
        • livestock value chains 5 -- sequence of activities from livestock production to final consumption
        • bundled livestock insurance 1 -- integrated insurance products combining multiple risk protections for pastoral communities
        • livestock-based lending 1 -- financial model using livestock as collateral to provide credit access to smallholder farmers
    • Aquaculture and Aquatic Food Systems -- Aquaculture development, integrated rice-fish systems, small-scale fisheries, and aquatic food production
      • Aquaculture Systems -- Aquaculture development, production, and management systems
        • aquaculture 2 -- farming of fish and other aquatic organisms in controlled environments
        • aquaculture development 3 -- establishment and improvement of fish farming systems
        • aquaculture production 1 -- controlled cultivation of aquatic organisms for food production
        • integrated multitrophic aquaculture 15 -- farming system combining species from different trophic levels to enhance resource efficiency and sustainability
        • homestead aquaculture 2 -- small-scale fish farming practiced at household level for food security and income generation
        • community-based aquaculture 1 -- fish farming systems developed and managed by local communities
        • wastewater aquaculture 1 -- fish farming using treated or untreated wastewater as input
        • solar-powered aquaculture 1 -- fish production systems using renewable solar energy
        • low trophic aquaculture 1 -- fish farming systems using species that feed low in the food chain
        • aquaponics 1 -- integrated farming system combining aquaculture and hydroponics
        • semi-autotrophic hydroponics 3 -- plant propagation system using sterile growing media under controlled conditions
        • aquaculture decision support tool 3 -- multi-criteria analysis platform for aquaculture site selection and planning
        • fish genetic improvement 1 -- systematic breeding approach to enhance genetic traits of fish species for aquaculture
        • fish value addition 1 -- processing and enhancement techniques to increase economic value of fish products
        • feed formulation 1 -- process of combining ingredients to create nutritionally balanced animal feed
      • Integrated Rice-Fish and Aquatic Farming -- Integrated rice-fish systems, rice-field pond systems, and crop-aquaculture integration
        • integrated rice-fish systems 2 -- farming system combining rice cultivation with fish production in the same field
        • integrated rice-fish farming 1 -- aquaculture system combining rice cultivation with fish production
        • rice-fish polyculture 1 -- integrated farming system combining rice cultivation with fish production
        • rice-field pond systems 5 -- integrated farming combining rice cultivation with aquaculture in field ponds
        • integrated rice-field pond systems 4 -- farming approach combining rice cultivation with aquaculture in the same field system
        • integrated rice-shrimp systems 1 -- farming system combining rice cultivation with shrimp aquaculture in the same field
        • integrated aquaculture agriculture 3 -- farming system combining fish production with crop cultivation to maximize resource efficiency
        • crop-aquaculture farming 1 -- integrated production system combining crop cultivation with fish farming
        • community fish refuge management 2 -- community-based governance of aquatic resources to maintain fish populations and livelihoods
      • Fisheries and Aquatic Food Systems -- Small-scale fisheries, aquatic food systems, and fisheries management
        • small-scale fisheries 3 -- artisanal fishing operations typically characterized by low capital investment and local community involvement
        • aquatic food systems 7 -- integrated networks of fisheries and aquaculture production, processing, and distribution
        • purse seine fisheries 1 -- commercial fishing method using large nets to encircle fish schools
        • fisheries subsidies 1 -- government financial support to fishing industry operations
        • environmental dna monitoring 1 -- biodiversity assessment using genetic material extracted from environmental samples
        • island food systems 2 -- food production and distribution systems specific to island environments
    • Agroforestry and Landscape Farming -- Agroforestry systems, silvopastoral systems, multifunctional landscapes, and tree-based farming approaches
      • Agroforestry Systems -- Agroforestry practices, silvopastoral systems, and tree-based farming
        • agroforestry 10 -- integration of trees with agricultural crops in production systems
        • agroforestry systems 1 -- integrated land-use combining trees with crops or livestock
        • silvopastoral systems 15 -- integrated agricultural systems combining trees, livestock, and pastures
        • agrosilvopastoral systems 1 -- integrated land-use systems combining trees, crops, and livestock for multiple benefits
        • agroforestry co-design 1 -- collaborative process to design diversified tree-crop systems aligned with producer needs
        • agroforestry monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking of tree planting and forest management activities
        • nursery management 3 -- systematic practices for rearing young organisms in controlled environments before transfer to production systems
        • tree-based restoration 1 -- ecosystem rehabilitation using tree planting and forest regeneration techniques
        • farmer-managed natural regeneration 1 -- restoration technique using indigenous vegetation management by farmers
      • Landscape and Land Management -- Multifunctional landscapes, land restoration, and landscape-level farming approaches
        • multifunctional landscapes 29 -- land management strategy that integrates multiple functions and services across landscapes
        • multifunctional landscape interventions 1 -- integrated interventions addressing multiple functions across agricultural landscapes
        • multifunctional forest landscapes 1 -- forest areas managed to provide multiple ecosystem services and benefits simultaneously
        • landscape restoration 8 -- ecosystem-based interventions to restore degraded land and seascape functions
        • land restoration 3 -- practices to rehabilitate degraded landscapes and ecosystems
        • land degradation 4 -- decline in land quality and productivity due to human activities and climate factors
        • sustainable land management 5 -- land use practices that maintain ecosystem services while meeting human needs
        • forest landscape restoration 2 -- long-term process of regaining ecological functionality across deforested landscapes
        • forest restoration 2 -- activities to restore degraded forest ecosystems
        • forest management practices 1 -- systematic methods for managing forest resources and ecosystems
        • deforestation monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of forest loss over time using remote sensing and ground data
        • zero deforestation 1 -- agricultural practices that prevent forest loss while maintaining productive farming systems
        • zero deforestation compliance 2 -- monitoring and verification systems to ensure supply chains do not contribute to forest loss
        • zero deforestation monitoring 1 -- tracking and verification of forest-free agricultural production
        • zero deforestation agreements 1 -- voluntary public-private initiatives to reduce deforestation in agricultural supply chains
        • post-mined land restoration 1 -- rehabilitation of degraded mining sites for productive agricultural use
        • land reclamation 1 -- restoration of degraded land for productive use
        • land tenure security 1 -- legal and institutional frameworks that provide secure rights to land ownership or use
        • bamboo carbon sink 1 -- carbon storage capacity of bamboo forests and plantations
        • blue carbon opportunities 1 -- carbon sequestration potential in marine and coastal ecosystems
        • ecosystem-based management 1 -- holistic management strategy considering entire ecosystem interactions
        • landscape performance indicators 1 -- metrics used to assess sustainability and functionality of agricultural landscapes
        • payment for ecosystem services 2 -- economic mechanism compensating land managers for environmental benefits
        • eco-compensation 2 -- payment mechanism to compensate for environmental services and conservation efforts
        • biodiversity nexus 1 -- interconnected relationships between biodiversity and multiple sectors
    • Farming Systems Innovation and Scaling -- Innovation in farming systems, technology adoption, and scaling of agricultural practices
      • Technology Adoption and Diffusion -- Adoption of agricultural technologies and innovations
        • technology adoption 9 -- process by which farmers accept and implement new agricultural technologies or practices
        • technology diffusion 3 -- spread and adoption of new technologies across markets and users
        • technology transfer 6 -- process of moving innovations from research to practical application
        • technology dissemination 2 -- process of spreading improved agricultural technologies to farmers
        • varietal adoption 8 -- process by which farmers select and use new crop varieties
        • improved seed adoption 2 -- process by which farmers take up and use enhanced crop varieties
        • hybrid adoption 1 -- uptake and use of hybrid crop varieties by farmers
        • fertilizer adoption 1 -- farmer uptake and use of mineral fertilizers
        • adoption dynamics 1 -- patterns of technology uptake including initial use, consistent adoption, dis-adoption, and non-adoption
        • technology adoption dynamics 2 -- patterns of farmer uptake and abandonment of agricultural innovations
        • innovation scaling 6 -- process of expanding successful agricultural innovations to broader adoption
        • scaling for impact 18 -- systematic approach to expanding successful innovations to achieve broader development outcomes
        • scaling pathways 12 -- systematic approaches to expand adoption of innovations
        • scaling strategies 5 -- systematic methods for expanding successful interventions to broader populations
        • scaling framework 7 -- systematic approach for expanding successful interventions to broader contexts
        • scaling readiness 5 -- systematic preparation of innovations for widespread adoption and implementation
        • scaling readiness assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of factors affecting the scaling of innovations
        • scaling assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of factors affecting innovation adoption and diffusion
        • inclusive scaling 4 -- strategy for expanding improved varieties that ensures broad access across different farm types and socioeconomic levels
        • adaptive scaling 2 -- approach to scaling innovations that adapts to local contexts and market conditions
        • responsible scaling 2 -- expansion of innovations while anticipating and mitigating social and environmental risks
        • producer-led scaling 4 -- farmer-driven expansion of successful practices through peer-to-peer learning
        • participatory scaling 1 -- collaborative approach to expanding successful interventions through stakeholder engagement
        • gender-responsive scaling 1 -- scaling strategies that integrate gender considerations and address gendered barriers
        • selective scaling 1 -- targeted expansion strategy focusing on specific contexts or beneficiary groups for improved varieties
        • scaling champions 2 -- trusted individuals who coordinate actors and support innovation adoption
        • scaling hubs 1 -- regional platforms that coordinate partnerships and knowledge sharing for agricultural innovation adoption
        • scaling fund 1 -- financial mechanism supporting innovation expansion and market penetration
        • scaling scan tool 1 -- assessment framework to evaluate innovation scalability potential
        • science of scaling 1 -- evidence-based understanding of how innovations achieve widespread adoption and impact
        • innovation validation 1 -- systematic testing of prototypes and assumptions with real users before full implementation
        • innovation bundling 5 -- integrating technical, digital and institutional solutions to address interconnected challenges
        • innovation hubs 2 -- territorial platforms that connect actors, capacities, and knowledge for technology validation and scaling
        • innovation networks 7 -- collaborative platforms connecting multiple actors to facilitate knowledge exchange and scaling
        • innovation platforms 5 -- multi-stakeholder forums for collaborative problem-solving and knowledge sharing
        • agricultural innovation systems 2 -- networks of organizations, policies, and processes that generate, adapt, and diffuse agricultural technologies
        • agricultural innovation 1 -- development and adoption of new technologies and practices in farming systems
        • farmer-led innovation 2 -- innovation processes initiated and driven by farmers themselves
        • community-led innovation 1 -- innovation processes initiated and controlled by local communities
        • responsible innovation 2 -- innovation processes that consider ethical, social, and environmental implications
        • living lab 6 -- collaborative innovation platform where stakeholders co-create and test solutions in real-world settings
        • agroecosystem living labs 2 -- collaborative research approach engaging farmers and communities to test climate-smart practices in real farming systems
        • model farms 2 -- demonstration sites showcasing integrated agricultural technologies and practices
        • farmer group model 1 -- organizational structure for collective farmer action and resource sharing
        • hub model 3 -- territorial multistakeholder platforms integrating research, innovation and extension
        • three-legged stool approach 1 -- innovative framework for improving meat safety in informal sectors
      • Agricultural Extension and Advisory Services -- Extension services, advisory systems, and farmer training approaches
        • agricultural extension 5 -- knowledge transfer system connecting research to farmers through advisory services
        • extension services 3 -- educational and advisory services that transfer knowledge and technologies to farmers
        • digital extension 13 -- delivery of agricultural advisory services through digital platforms and mobile applications
        • digital advisory services 22 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital extension platforms 5 -- technology-enabled systems for delivering agricultural advisory services to farmers
        • digital advisory platforms 5 -- technology-based systems for delivering agricultural and climate advisories
        • digital agro-advisory services 4 -- technology-enabled platforms delivering agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital agricultural extension 3 -- technology-enabled delivery of agricultural advisory services and information to farmers
        • digital extension services 2 -- technology-enabled platforms for delivering agricultural advisory and information services to farmers
        • digital extension integration 2 -- combining digital tools with traditional extension services for farmer outreach
        • digital agricultural advisory 2 -- technology-enabled extension services delivering agricultural recommendations
        • digital agricultural advisory services 1 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural information and support to farmers
        • ai-driven agricultural extension 4 -- use of artificial intelligence to deliver agricultural advice and information to farmers
        • farmer-led extension 2 -- agricultural advisory systems driven by peer-to-peer knowledge sharing among farmers
        • community-based extension 1 -- extension service delivery model using local community members as agents
        • community-driven extension 1 -- extension services led and owned by local communities rather than external agencies
        • participatory extension 2 -- collaborative method of delivering agricultural knowledge involving farmer participation
        • traditional extension 1 -- conventional top-down method of delivering agricultural information to farmers
        • radio-based extension 1 -- using radio broadcasts to deliver agricultural information and climate advisories
        • mobile-based extension 1 -- delivering agricultural advisory services through mobile phone platforms
        • mobile application 1 -- software platform designed for mobile devices to deliver services or information
        • interactive voice response 1 -- technology that allows interaction with computer systems through voice commands and responses
        • digital advisories 3 -- technology-based platforms delivering agricultural guidance to farmers
        • bundled advisory services 3 -- integrated delivery of multiple agricultural services including advisories, inputs, credit, and insurance
        • multimodal advisory services 1 -- agricultural extension using multiple communication channels and formats
        • agro-advisories 1 -- agricultural advice services based on weather and climate information
        • agro-advisory provision 1 -- services providing agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • weather-informed agro-advisories 1 -- agricultural guidance services that incorporate weather forecast data for farming decisions
        • forecast-based advisory 1 -- decision support system using weather forecasts to guide agricultural practices
        • digital decision-support tools 1 -- technology platforms that provide data-driven recommendations for agricultural management
        • farmer-to-farmer learning 1 -- peer-to-peer knowledge exchange methods that validate farmers as sources of innovation
        • demonstration plot training 1 -- hands-on learning approach using field demonstrations to transfer technology
        • farmer field demonstrations 2 -- on-farm testing and showcasing of improved agricultural practices
        • last-mile delivery 2 -- reaching end users through local agent networks and service providers
        • last-mile distribution 1 -- final stage of product delivery from businesses to end-users in rural areas
        • decentralized service delivery 1 -- distribution of services through local providers rather than centralized institutions
        • service delivery integration 1 -- coordination of multiple services to provide holistic support to farmers
      • Participatory and Farmer-Centered Approaches -- Participatory research, co-design, and farmer-centered innovation approaches
        • participatory research 32 -- research approach involving stakeholders as active participants in knowledge generation
        • participatory approach 13 -- research and development methods involving stakeholder participation
        • participatory methods 5 -- research approaches involving active participation of study subjects
        • participatory methodology 2 -- research approach that actively involves community members in the design and implementation process
        • participatory learning 8 -- educational methodology where stakeholders actively engage in knowledge creation and sharing through collaborative activities
        • participatory learning methodology 2 -- educational approach that engages participants in dialogue and reflection to foster learning and behavior change
        • participatory evaluation 5 -- research approach involving farmers and stakeholders in assessment of technologies
        • participatory monitoring and evaluation 3 -- community-led approach to tracking progress and assessing outcomes using locally relevant indicators
        • participatory diagnosis 5 -- collaborative assessment of needs and priorities involving stakeholders
        • participatory mapping 7 -- collaborative approach involving farmers in adaptation planning and field-level solutions
        • participatory co-design 3 -- collaborative approach involving stakeholders in designing research and interventions
        • participatory design 2 -- collaborative approach involving end-users in the design and development process
        • participatory technology development 1 -- collaborative approach involving stakeholders in designing and developing technologies
        • participatory action research 1 -- collaborative research approach involving stakeholders in problem-solving
        • participatory action learning 1 -- collaborative approach integrating local knowledge with scientific methods
        • participatory rural appraisal 1 -- community-based research method using local knowledge and participation
        • participatory foresight 1 -- collaborative approach to exploring and planning for future scenarios
        • participatory scenario development 1 -- collaborative process for creating future scenarios with stakeholder input
        • participatory assessment 1 -- evaluation approach involving stakeholder participation in research and development
        • participatory validation 1 -- research approach involving stakeholders in confirming and refining findings
        • participatory framework 1 -- structured approach involving multiple stakeholders in decision-making processes
        • participatory guarantee systems 1 -- community-based certification mechanisms for organic and agroecological production
        • participatory geographic information systems 1 -- community-based mapping approach integrating local knowledge with GIS technology
        • co-design 17 -- collaborative development process involving multiple stakeholders in innovation creation
        • co-creation 4 -- collaborative development process involving multiple stakeholders
        • co-production 2 -- collaborative development process involving multiple stakeholders
        • co-creation approach 1 -- collaborative development process involving community members as partners
        • co-production of knowledge 1 -- collaborative research approach involving communities in knowledge generation
        • human-centered design 19 -- participatory design approach that prioritizes user needs and experiences in technology development
        • user-centered design 3 -- design approach that prioritizes end-user needs and experiences throughout development
        • tricot approach 3 -- triadic comparison method for on-farm testing where farmers rank three technologies
        • triadic comparisons of technology options 15 -- research approach to decentralize technology evaluation using large numbers of participants for higher external validity
        • triadic comparison of technologies 2 -- participatory evaluation method where farmers compare three technologies simultaneously
        • pioneer positive deviance 8 -- approach that identifies and supports farmers already developing innovative local solutions
        • positive deviance approach 3 -- identifying and learning from individuals or groups with better outcomes despite similar constraints
        • positive deviance 1 -- approach identifying successful practices among those facing similar challenges
        • on-farm testing 16 -- evaluating varieties under actual farmer field conditions
        • on-farm trials 1 -- field research conducted on farmers' own land to test innovations under real conditions
        • on-farm verification 1 -- testing crop varieties under actual farmer conditions to validate performance
        • citizen science 12 -- scientific research conducted by members of the general public in collaboration with professional scientists
        • village-led action research 1 -- community-driven research approach where locals identify and address challenges
        • action research 1 -- research approach combining investigation with direct action for social change
        • transdisciplinary research 1 -- problem-focused research approach that integrates knowledge from multiple disciplines and stakeholders
        • transdisciplinary approaches 1 -- research and intervention methods that integrate multiple disciplines and stakeholder perspectives
        • interdisciplinary research 3 -- research approach combining multiple scientific disciplines to address complex problems
        • systems approach 3 -- holistic methodology analyzing interconnected components and feedback loops
        • systems thinking 2 -- holistic approach to understanding complex problems by examining interconnections and leverage points
        • systems science 1 -- interdisciplinary study of complex systems and their emergent properties
      • Smallholder and Community Farming -- Smallholder farming systems, community-based approaches, and inclusive farming
        • smallholder farming 12 -- small-scale agricultural production by resource-limited farmers
        • community-based management 6 -- natural resource management approach led by local communities
        • community-based resource management 2 -- participatory strategy where local communities manage their natural resources
        • community-led initiatives 2 -- locally driven programs managed by community members
        • collective action 2 -- coordinated community efforts to address shared challenges
        • cooperative development 3 -- building organizational capacity and governance structures for farmer collective action
        • agricultural commercialization clusters 3 -- geographic zones organized for coordinated agricultural development and market access
        • contract farming 2 -- agricultural production under forward contracts between farmers and buyers
        • inclusive business models 3 -- commercial strategies ensuring access to services for smallholder farmers and marginalized groups
        • better management practices 3 -- standardized guidelines for sustainable and productive farming operations
        • good agricultural practices 9 -- science-backed farming techniques including timely planting, optimal fertilizer rates, and improved varieties
        • sustainable agricultural practices 6 -- farming methods that maintain productivity while conserving resources and enhancing resilience
    • Seed Systems and Crop Improvement -- Seed systems, seed production, and crop improvement for sustainable farming
      • Seed Systems Development -- Seed system strengthening, seed production, and seed delivery
        • seed systems 10 -- networks of actors, institutions, and processes for seed production and distribution
        • seed system strengthening 9 -- interventions to improve production, distribution, and access to quality planting materials
        • seed system development 1 -- strategies to improve seed production and distribution networks
        • seed system modernization 1 -- improvements to seed production, distribution, and quality assurance for vegetatively propagated crops
        • seed system innovations 1 -- improved methods and technologies for crop seed production and distribution
        • seed sector development 1 -- systematic strengthening of seed production, distribution, and regulatory systems
        • seed production 6 -- organized process of producing quality seeds for agricultural use
        • seed production systems 3 -- organized networks for producing and distributing quality crop seeds
        • early generation seed production 5 -- multiplication of breeder and foundation seed by private sector
        • early generation seed supply 3 -- provision of foundation seed from breeding programs to seed producers
        • community-based seed production 4 -- decentralized seed production and marketing managed by local communities
        • certified seed production 3 -- formal system for producing seeds that meet quality standards and certification requirements
        • quality seed production 2 -- systematic production of seed meeting genetic purity and health standards
        • market-driven seed production 1 -- seed multiplication based on trader and consumer demand
        • clean seed systems 5 -- formal networks for producing and distributing disease-free planting materials
        • seed certification 4 -- formal quality assurance system for verifying seed quality and genetic purity
        • seed certification system 1 -- quality assurance framework ensuring genetic purity and health of planting materials
        • seed certification regulations 1 -- legal frameworks ensuring seed quality and standards
        • quality declared seed 1 -- seed certification system ensuring genetic purity and quality standards
        • seed conservation 5 -- preservation and maintenance of crop genetic diversity through seed storage and management
        • community seed banks 4 -- locally managed seed conservation and distribution systems
        • community seed systems 1 -- local networks for producing, storing, and distributing seeds
        • community-based seed systems 1 -- local seed production and distribution networks managed by farming communities
        • community seed schemes 1 -- local seed production and distribution systems operated by farming communities
        • on-farm seed storage 1 -- farmer-managed preservation of seeds between harvest and planting
        • seed distribution systems 2 -- networks and mechanisms for delivering seeds to farmers
        • seed delivery network 1 -- coordinated system of organizations that multiply and distribute improved crop varieties to farmers
        • market-driven seed delivery 1 -- seed distribution system responsive to farmer and trader market demands rather than supply-driven
        • direct seed marketing 1 -- policy approach allowing seed producers to sell directly to farmers bypassing state distribution
        • trader-centered seed systems 1 -- seed distribution networks that place traders at the center of connecting producers with farmers
        • seed roadmap development 2 -- strategic planning process for seed sector development
        • seed requirement estimation 1 -- data-driven planning for seed production and distribution
        • seed demand projection 1 -- forecasting future requirements for crop planting materials
        • seed tracking 1 -- monitoring system for certified seed production and distribution
        • digital seed inventory management 1 -- systematic approach to tracking and managing seed stocks using digital platforms
        • seed priming 1 -- pre-sowing treatment to enhance seed germination and seedling vigor
        • seed dormancy breaking 1 -- treatments to overcome seed dormancy and promote germination
        • seed treatment protocols 1 -- standardized procedures for treating seeds with fungicides to prevent disease
        • seed quality testing 1 -- assessment of seed viability, purity, and health for conservation and distribution
        • seed health testing 2 -- protocols for detecting pathogens and ensuring seed quality
        • variety registration 52 -- official approval of new varieties for commercial release
        • variety release procedures 3 -- formal processes for approving and distributing new crop varieties
        • variety licensing 4 -- commercialization strategy where public research institutions license improved varieties to private sector
        • varietal registration 1 -- official process to approve and license new crop varieties for commercial use
        • variety testing 3 -- systematic evaluation of crop varieties for performance and adaptation
        • variety trial 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop or forage varieties across different environments
        • variety adaptation trials 2 -- field testing to evaluate crop performance under local conditions
        • multi-environment testing 4 -- evaluating varieties across multiple locations and conditions
        • multi-environment trials 4 -- testing genotypes across multiple locations and conditions
        • multi-environment trial evaluation 3 -- testing of varieties across multiple locations and seasons
        • multi-location testing 2 -- evaluation of crop varieties across multiple environments to assess performance and adaptation
        • multi-location trials 1 -- testing of crop varieties across multiple geographic locations for validation
        • multilocation trials 1 -- testing varieties across multiple sites to assess performance and adaptation
        • regional variety trials 1 -- multi-location testing of varieties across geographic regions
        • multi-site validation 1 -- testing technologies across multiple locations to assess performance and adaptability
        • varietal turnover 2 -- replacement of older crop varieties with newer improved varieties by farmers
        • varietal turnover acceleration 1 -- strategies to speed replacement of older crop varieties with improved ones
        • varietal tracking 1 -- monitoring adoption and distribution of specific crop varieties
        • variety positioning 1 -- strategic placement of improved crop varieties in formal seed systems for farmer adoption
        • participatory variety selection 16 -- farmer-involved process for evaluating and selecting crop varieties
        • participatory varietal selection 4 -- farmer involvement in testing and selecting new crop varieties
        • participatory cultivar selection 2 -- farmer involvement in variety evaluation and selection before commercial release
        • indigenous variety conservation 2 -- preservation and promotion of traditional crop varieties
      • Crop Genetic Improvement -- Breeding programs, genetic improvement, and crop variety development
        • participatory breeding 11 -- involving farmers in variety selection and development process
        • community-based breeding programs 3 -- participatory genetic improvement schemes involving farmers in design and implementation
        • demand-driven breeding 3 -- breeding programs guided by end-user needs and market requirements
        • demand-led breeding 1 -- crop improvement driven by market and farmer requirements
        • market-responsive breeding 6 -- crop breeding aligned with specific market demands and consumer preferences
        • gender-responsive breeding 3 -- breeding programs that consider differentiated needs of men and women farmers
        • disease resistance breeding 14 -- development of crop varieties with genetic resistance to pathogens
        • drought tolerance breeding 2 -- development of varieties adapted to water-limited conditions
        • direct seeded rice breeding 1 -- development of rice varieties optimized for direct planting methods
        • malting quality breeding 1 -- crop improvement focused on traits suitable for malt production
        • low-methane forage breeding 1 -- selection and breeding of feed crops that reduce livestock methane production
        • tropical forage breeding 4 -- development of improved forage varieties adapted to tropical conditions for livestock systems
        • evolutionary plant breeding 1 -- breeding approach maintaining genetic diversity through population mixtures
        • speed breeding 6 -- accelerated breeding technique to reduce generation time
        • rapid generation advancement 1 -- techniques to reduce breeding cycle time and accelerate genetic gains
        • rapid cycle recurrent selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach that reduces cycle time from three years to one year
        • doubled haploids 3 -- breeding technique producing homozygous lines in one generation
        • doubled haploid breeding 2 -- technique to rapidly produce homozygous inbred lines from heterozygous plants
        • double haploid breeding 1 -- plant breeding technique that produces homozygous lines in a single generation
        • genetic improvement 3 -- systematic enhancement of livestock or crop traits through breeding programs
        • genetic improvement program 6 -- systematic breeding to enhance genetic performance of crops or livestock
        • breeding pipeline 8 -- structured sequence of breeding activities and selection stages
        • breeding pipeline development 3 -- establishment of systematic processes for variety development
        • breeding program assessment 4 -- systematic evaluation of breeding program effectiveness and capabilities
        • breeding program costing 6 -- systematic analysis of resource allocation and costs in breeding operations
        • breeding cost analysis 1 -- systematic assessment of operational costs in breeding programs for improved resource allocation
        • breeding data management 2 -- systematic organization and tracking of plant breeding information and materials
        • breeding objective 1 -- systematic framework defining desired traits and their relative importance for genetic improvement programs
        • target product profiles 13 -- systematic documentation of desired crop characteristics based on market needs
        • target product profile development 1 -- systematic definition of desired characteristics for breeding targets
        • product profile development 3 -- defining target traits and characteristics for specific market segments
        • trait preferences 3 -- farmer and stakeholder preferences for specific crop characteristics
        • trait preference ranking 1 -- systematic evaluation and ordering of livestock or crop characteristics based on stakeholder priorities
        • trait deployment 3 -- process of incorporating beneficial genetic traits into crop varieties through breeding programs
        • trait introgression 1 -- transfer of specific genetic traits from wild species into cultivated varieties through breeding
        • trait development pipeline 1 -- systematic framework for translating research discoveries into breeding applications
        • genetic gain 5 -- rate of improvement in crop performance through breeding
        • realized genetic gain 1 -- actual improvement in crop traits achieved through breeding over time
        • genetic gain assessment 1 -- standardized measurement of breeding progress across crops and traits
        • population improvement 1 -- genetic enhancement of breeding populations for desired traits
        • collaborative crop improvement networks 1 -- multi-institutional partnerships for coordinated plant breeding and research
        • germplasm utilization 28 -- use of genetic resources from gene banks or research collections for crop improvement
        • germplasm conservation 1 -- preservation of genetic resources for crop improvement and research
        • germplasm screening 1 -- systematic evaluation of genetic material for desired traits
        • germplasm repatriation 1 -- return of genetic material to countries or institutions of origin
        • cryopreservation 10 -- preservation of plant genetic material at ultra-low temperatures
        • seed conservation 5 -- preservation and maintenance of crop genetic diversity through seed storage and management
        • on-farm conservation 3 -- maintaining crop genetic diversity through cultivation on farms
        • stress tolerance 8 -- ability of crops to maintain productivity under abiotic or biotic stress conditions
        • heat stress tolerance 7 -- plant ability to maintain performance under high temperature conditions
        • drought tolerance 33 -- plant ability to maintain productivity under water stress
        • flood tolerance 1 -- ability of crops to survive and produce under flooding conditions
        • salinity tolerance 1 -- plant ability to grow and function in high-salt environments
        • multi-stress tolerance 1 -- plant resistance to multiple biotic and abiotic stresses
        • stress tolerance indices 2 -- mathematical measures used to quantify plant performance under stress conditions
        • drought tolerance screening 2 -- systematic evaluation of crop varieties for resistance to water stress
        • drought adaptation 1 -- ability of crops or livestock to maintain productivity under water stress conditions
        • drought stress management 2 -- strategies to mitigate water deficit impacts on crop production
        • terminal heat stress 2 -- high temperature exposure during grain filling that reduces crop yields
        • heat stress 1 -- physiological stress caused by exposure to high temperatures
        • heat stress analysis 4 -- quantitative assessment of temperature impacts on crops, livestock, and human populations
        • elevated temperature stress 1 -- plant stress condition caused by higher than optimal growing temperatures
        • photoperiod effects 1 -- influence of day length variations on plant growth and flowering cycles
        • genotype by environment interaction 5 -- differential performance of genotypes across different environments
        • multi-trait selection index 1 -- breeding tool that combines multiple traits into single selection criterion
        • combining ability 1 -- genetic capacity of parents to produce superior hybrid offspring
        • combining ability analysis 2 -- assessment of parental lines' ability to produce superior hybrid offspring
        • line × tester analysis 1 -- breeding design to evaluate combining ability and hybrid performance
        • heterotic group development 1 -- classification of breeding lines based on combining ability for hybrid development
        • heterotic pattern integration 1 -- breeding approach to combine complementary genetic groups for hybrid vigor
        • haplotype analysis 1 -- study of linked genetic variants inherited together as blocks
        • haplotype stacking 1 -- breeding strategy to combine multiple favorable genetic variants in single varieties
        • qtl pyramiding 1 -- breeding strategy that stacks multiple quantitative trait loci to combine beneficial traits
        • gene pyramiding 2 -- combining multiple resistance genes in a single variety
        • population genetics analysis 1 -- study of genetic variation and structure within pathogen or crop populations
        • genetic variance partitioning 1 -- statistical decomposition of trait variation into additive and non-additive components
        • genetic parameter estimation 1 -- statistical methods to quantify heritable variation in traits
        • heritability estimation 2 -- quantification of genetic contribution to trait variation
        • heritability metrics 2 -- statistical measures quantifying genetic versus environmental contribution to trait variation
        • family-based selection 2 -- breeding strategy using family performance data to select superior genetic lines
        • optimal contribution selection 1 -- breeding strategy to optimize genetic contributions while managing inbreeding
        • rapid-cycle genomic selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach using genomic data to reduce crop development time
        • multi-trait genomic selection 1 -- genomic selection approach considering multiple traits simultaneously
        • genomic prediction 5 -- use of genome-wide markers to predict breeding values or phenotypes
        • genomic selection 22 -- breeding approach using genome-wide markers for trait prediction
        • genomic diversity analysis 1 -- use of DNA sequencing data to understand genetic variation patterns in crops
        • genomic profiling 1 -- comprehensive analysis of genetic material to identify specific traits or pathogens
        • genomic marker development 1 -- creation of DNA-based tools for genetic analysis and tracking
        • genome-wide association study 24 -- statistical approach to identify genetic variants associated with traits
        • genome-wide association mapping 4 -- genetic analysis technique to identify genes associated with specific traits
        • genome-wide association analysis 1 -- statistical approach to identify genetic variants associated with traits across the genome
        • single-step genome-wide association study 1 -- genomic analysis method combining pedigree and marker information to identify trait-associated genetic variants
        • quantitative trait loci mapping 9 -- identification of genomic regions controlling quantitative traits
        • quantitative trait locus mapping 4 -- genetic technique to identify chromosomal regions controlling quantitative traits
        • quantitative trait nucleotides 1 -- DNA sequence variants that contribute to quantitative trait variation
        • meta-qtl analysis 1 -- statistical method combining qtl data from multiple studies to identify consensus regions
        • marker-assisted selection 30 -- breeding technique using molecular markers to select for desired traits
        • marker-assisted breeding 2 -- use of molecular markers to guide selection in breeding programs
        • molecular breeding 3 -- breeding approach using molecular tools and markers for trait selection
        • molecular marker analysis 2 -- use of DNA markers to identify genes or traits in breeding programs
        • dna fingerprinting 1 -- genetic technique for identifying crop varieties using molecular markers
        • genetic fingerprinting 1 -- molecular technique for identifying unique genetic profiles of varieties
        • population genomics 2 -- study of genetic variation and evolution within and between populations
        • pan-genomics 1 -- study of entire gene sets across multiple individuals or varieties of a species
        • pangenome construction 1 -- assembling comprehensive genomic landscape capturing complete genetic diversity within species
        • multi-omics integration 3 -- combining genomics, transcriptomics, and metabolomics data for analysis
        • multi-omics approaches 2 -- integration of genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics and metabolomics data
        • metabolomics-assisted breeding 1 -- integration of metabolite profiling with breeding programs to enhance crop improvement
        • metabolomic profiling 2 -- comprehensive analysis of small molecules and metabolites in biological samples
        • metabolomic analysis 1 -- comprehensive study of metabolites in biological samples
        • transcriptome analysis 1 -- comprehensive study of RNA transcripts produced by genome under specific conditions
        • rhizosphere microbiome analysis 1 -- study of microbial communities in plant root zones
        • microbial diversity assessment 1 -- analysis of bacterial community composition and structure in environmental samples
        • genome editing 13 -- precise modification of DNA sequences in living organisms
        • gene editing 2 -- biotechnology technique for precise modification of plant genomes
        • prime editing 4 -- precise genome modification technique that enables targeted genetic changes
        • genetic engineering 1 -- direct manipulation of organism's genes using biotechnology
        • high-throughput phenotyping 4 -- automated measurement of plant traits using advanced technologies
        • phenotyping 2 -- measurement and analysis of observable plant characteristics
        • digital phenotyping 2 -- automated collection of plant trait data using digital technologies
        • plant phenotyping 1 -- measurement and analysis of plant physical and biochemical traits
        • computer vision phenotyping 1 -- automated plant trait measurement using image analysis algorithms
        • machine learning phenotyping 1 -- use of computational algorithms to automatically assess plant traits from sensor data
        • sparse phenotyping 1 -- strategy to reduce phenotyping costs while maintaining selection accuracy
        • multi-stress phenotyping 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop performance under multiple environmental stresses
        • root system architecture 3 -- spatial configuration and development of plant root systems
        • senescence rate 2 -- speed of plant aging and chlorophyll degradation
        • grain quality 2 -- nutritional and processing characteristics of cereal grains
        • grain quality assessment 1 -- evaluation of physical and chemical properties affecting grain value
        • nutritional enhancement 5 -- improving the nutritional content of foods through ingredient modification or fortification
        • biofortification 41 -- enhancing nutritional content of crops through breeding
        • glycemic index modification 1 -- breeding strategy to alter blood sugar response of food crops
        • protein digestibility 1 -- measure of how well proteins can be broken down and absorbed by the body
        • cyanogenesis 1 -- biochemical process of hydrogen cyanide production in plants
        • anthocyanin content evaluation 1 -- measurement of pigment compounds in plant tissues
        • near-infrared spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid quality assessment using light absorption
        • high-performance liquid chromatography 1 -- analytical technique for separating, identifying and quantifying compounds in mixtures
        • phenolic compound extraction 1 -- process of isolating and purifying phenolic compounds from plant materials
        • flow cytometry 1 -- laboratory technique for determining chromosome numbers and analyzing cellular characteristics
        • skim-sequencing 1 -- low-coverage DNA sequencing approach for cost-effective genotyping
        • targeted sequencing 1 -- focused DNA sequencing approach to analyze specific genomic regions of interest
        • whole-genome sequencing 1 -- complete DNA analysis technique for pathogen identification and characterization
        • population dynamics 1 -- changes in organism population characteristics over time and space
        • alternative polyadenylation 1 -- molecular mechanism regulating gene expression through RNA processing
        • chromatin accessibility mapping 1 -- technique to identify regions of genome accessible for transcription factor binding
        • comparative genomic analysis 1 -- comparison of genome structure and function between different organisms or varieties
        • bioinformatics analysis 1 -- computational methods for analyzing biological data, particularly genomic sequences
        • data-driven breeding 2 -- use of analytical tools and databases to guide breeding decisions
        • breeding data management 2 -- systematic organization and tracking of plant breeding information and materials
        • digital sequence information 3 -- genomic data used for genetic resource characterization and analysis
        • vegetative propagation 3 -- plant reproduction using vegetative parts rather than seeds
        • rapid multiplication technology 1 -- accelerated propagation techniques for plant breeding materials
        • rapid stem multiplication 1 -- accelerated propagation technique for vegetatively reproduced crops using controlled environment systems
        • macro-propagation 1 -- rapid multiplication of planting materials under controlled conditions
        • macro-propagation techniques 1 -- methods for rapid multiplication of planting materials using vegetative propagation
        • apical rooted cuttings propagation 2 -- vegetative propagation method using shoot tips to produce disease-free planting material
        • rooted apical cuttings 1 -- vegetative propagation technique using rooted shoot tips for plant multiplication
        • leaf-bud cutting propagation 1 -- vegetative reproduction technique using leaf-bud segments
        • tissue culture propagation 2 -- laboratory-based method for producing disease-free plant material through sterile culture techniques
        • in vitro culture 1 -- laboratory technique for growing plant tissues under sterile conditions
        • droplet vitrification 1 -- cryopreservation technique using rapid cooling of small tissue samples
        • nano-culture technology 1 -- plant tissue culture technique using minimal space and resources
        • iron-coated seed technology 1 -- seed treatment technology that improves crop establishment and reduces emissions
        • morphological characterization 4 -- systematic description of physical traits in crop varieties or landraces
        • genetic diversity assessment 2 -- evaluation of genetic variation within crop populations
        • disease resistance screening 4 -- systematic evaluation of genetic materials for pathogen tolerance
        • drought tolerance screening 2 -- systematic evaluation of crop varieties for resistance to water stress
        • pathotype screening 1 -- testing plant materials against different strains of pathogens to assess resistance
        • testcross evaluation 1 -- crossing experimental lines with standard testers to evaluate performance
        • multi-location trials 1 -- testing of crop varieties across multiple geographic locations for validation
        • yield gap analysis 3 -- assessment of differences between potential and actual crop yields
        • yield gap closure 3 -- bridging the difference between current farmer yields and attainable yields under optimal conditions
        • cassava production 2 -- cultivation and management of cassava crops
        • rapid cycle recurrent selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach that reduces cycle time from three years to one year
    • Post-Harvest and Food Loss Management -- Post-harvest management, food loss reduction, and storage technologies
      • post-harvest losses 5 -- reduction in crop quantity or quality after harvest
      • post-harvest management 1 -- practices and technologies for handling, processing and storing crops after harvest
      • post-harvest processing 3 -- handling and treatment of crops after harvest
      • post-harvest technology 2 -- methods and equipment for processing, preserving, and storing agricultural products
      • post-harvest loss reduction 1 -- minimizing food waste and quality degradation after crop harvest through improved handling
      • postharvest loss reduction 2 -- strategies to minimize food waste after harvest
      • post-harvest food loss 1 -- reduction in food quantity or quality that occurs after harvest but before consumption
      • food loss and waste reduction 1 -- strategies to minimize food losses along value chains and consumer waste
      • hermetic storage 1 -- airtight storage systems that prevent pest infestation and maintain grain quality
      • on-farm seed storage 1 -- farmer-managed preservation of seeds between harvest and planting
      • cold chain transportation 1 -- temperature-controlled logistics system for preserving product quality during transport
      • two-step drying 1 -- seed processing technique using initial warm drying followed by cooler drying to improve storage longevity
      • parboiling 1 -- rice processing technique involving partial boiling in the husk to improve nutritional quality
      • gem parboiling technique 2 -- improved rice processing method for enhanced quality and efficiency
      • food recovery 2 -- systematic collection and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve access
      • food rescue 2 -- recovery and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve food security
      • waste-to-value technologies 1 -- methods to convert organic waste into useful products like compost, biogas, or protein
      • resource recovery and reuse 1 -- circular economy approach to extract value from waste materials including biomass and wastewater
      • traceability 2 -- ability to track products through supply chain stages
      • traceability systems 1 -- systems for tracking products through supply chains
      • quality assurance 2 -- systematic processes to ensure products or services meet specified standards
      • quality control 2 -- systematic processes to ensure product standards
      • quality management systems 1 -- structured framework for ensuring consistent product quality and safety standards
      • food safety 6 -- assurance that food will not cause harm when prepared or consumed
      • food safety management 2 -- systematic approach to ensuring safe food production and handling
      • food safety monitoring 2 -- systematic surveillance of contaminants and hazards in food systems
      • food safety training 1 -- education programs to improve food handling and safety practices
      • aflatoxin contamination 1 -- presence of toxic compounds produced by fungi that cause liver cancer and stunting
      • pesticide residue detection 1 -- analytical techniques to identify and quantify chemical residues in crops
  • Climate Change, Environment, and Natural Resources -- Covers climate adaptation, mitigation, environmental sustainability, and natural resource management in agricultural contexts
    • Climate Adaptation and Resilience -- Climate-smart agriculture, adaptation planning, climate resilience building, and locally led adaptation approaches
      • Climate-Smart Agriculture Approaches -- Integrated approaches combining productivity, resilience, and mitigation
        • climate-smart agriculture 230 -- farming practices that address climate change while improving productivity
        • climate-resilient agriculture 5 -- farming practices adapted to withstand climate variability and extreme weather
        • climate-smart villages 3 -- community-based research sites for testing and demonstrating climate adaptation technologies
        • climate-smart village approach 4 -- integrated community-level framework for implementing climate-smart agriculture
        • bundled cis and csa 5 -- integrated packages combining climate information with smart agriculture practices
        • bundled climate-smart solutions 3 -- integrated package of climate adaptation and mitigation practices for agriculture
        • bundled csa practices 1 -- integrated packages of climate-smart agricultural interventions implemented at scale
        • climate-smart practices 2 -- agricultural techniques that adapt to and mitigate climate change
        • climate-smart interventions 1 -- agricultural practices that enhance productivity while building climate resilience
      • Adaptation Planning and Implementation -- Strategies and frameworks for planning and implementing climate adaptation
        • climate adaptation 87 -- adjustments in systems to reduce vulnerability to climate change impacts
        • climate change adaptation 16 -- strategies and measures to adjust to climate change impacts and reduce vulnerability
        • climate adaptation planning 10 -- systematic planning to reduce climate vulnerability
        • adaptation planning 2 -- systematic process for developing climate change adaptation strategies
        • national adaptation plans 5 -- medium to long-term national planning processes for climate adaptation
        • transformational adaptation 4 -- fundamental changes in agricultural systems to address climate change impacts
        • transformative adaptation 3 -- fundamental changes in systems to address climate impacts
        • climate adaptation practices 2 -- strategies and actions taken to adjust to climate change impacts and reduce vulnerability
        • climate adaptation indicators 2 -- metrics used to measure progress in building resilience to climate change
        • adaptation tracking 1 -- systematic monitoring and evaluation of climate adaptation interventions and outcomes
        • adaptation finance 3 -- financial resources dedicated to climate change adaptation activities
        • global goal on adaptation 1 -- international climate policy framework for adaptation planning and implementation
        • maladaptation 2 -- adaptation efforts that unintentionally create new risks or vulnerabilities
        • inclusive climate planning 1 -- participatory planning processes that ensure diverse stakeholder representation in climate initiatives
      • Climate Resilience Building -- Building resilience to climate shocks and stresses
        • climate resilience 65 -- ability to withstand and adapt to climate change impacts
        • climate resilience assessment 4 -- systematic evaluation of systems' ability to withstand climate impacts
        • adaptive capacity 3 -- ability of systems to adjust to climate change and reduce vulnerability
        • socio-ecological resilience 3 -- capacity of social-ecological systems to adapt and maintain function under stress
        • food systems resilience 4 -- capacity of food systems to withstand and recover from shocks while maintaining functionality
        • climate-resilient farming 3 -- agricultural practices that help farmers adapt to climate change impacts
        • climate-resilient livestock management 1 -- livestock practices adapted to withstand climate variability
        • climate-resilient seed production 2 -- development and multiplication of crop varieties adapted to climate change impacts
        • climate-resilient irrigation 1 -- water management systems designed to withstand climate variability and change
        • climate-resilient wash 1 -- water, sanitation and hygiene systems designed to withstand climate impacts
        • hydrological resilience 1 -- capacity of water systems to withstand and recover from disturbances
        • water resilience planning 4 -- strategic planning to ensure water security under climate change
      • Locally Led and Community-Based Adaptation -- Community-driven and locally led approaches to climate adaptation
        • locally led adaptation 4 -- community-driven climate adaptation strategies based on local knowledge and needs
        • locally-led climate adaptation 8 -- community-driven adaptation strategies that integrate local knowledge with scientific data
        • locally led climate action 4 -- climate initiatives driven and controlled by local communities
        • community-based climate observation 3 -- local monitoring of climate variables by community members to support decision-making and early warning
        • participatory climate services 8 -- collaborative approach to developing and delivering climate information that involves end-users in the design and implementation process
      • Climate-Conflict and Displacement Nexus -- Linkages between climate change, conflict, and displacement
        • climate-conflict nexus 12 -- the interconnected relationship between climate change impacts and violent conflict emergence
        • climate security 16 -- framework addressing security risks arising from climate change impacts
        • climate-peace-security nexus 5 -- integrated framework addressing interconnected climate, peace and security challenges
        • climate-displacement nexus 4 -- relationship between climate change impacts and human displacement
        • climate-induced displacement 4 -- forced migration resulting from climate change impacts and extreme weather events
        • climate fragility 2 -- vulnerability of systems to climate-related stresses and shocks
        • climate-conflict analysis 1 -- analytical approach to measure connections between climate change and conflict occurrence
      • Agroecological Approaches to Adaptation -- Agroecological practices supporting climate adaptation
        • agroecological transition 29 -- shift from conventional to sustainable farming practices that work with natural ecosystems
        • agroecology 23 -- ecological approach to agriculture that applies ecological principles to farming systems
        • agroecological practices 12 -- farming methods that integrate ecological principles for sustainability
        • agroecological stewardship 4 -- practices that promote environmental sustainability and ecological health in agricultural systems
        • regenerative agriculture 12 -- farming practices aimed at rebuilding soil health and enhancing ecosystem function
        • nature-based solutions 9 -- interventions that use natural processes to address environmental and development challenges
        • nature-positive agriculture 3 -- farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem services while maintaining productivity
        • agroecological assessment 2 -- evaluation of agricultural systems using agroecological principles
        • transformational agroecology 1 -- transition toward environmentally sustainable, productive, and socially just agri-food systems
        • agroecological solutions 1 -- sustainable farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem functions
        • agroecological change 1 -- transformation toward ecological farming practices and systems
    • Climate Mitigation and Low-Carbon Agriculture -- Greenhouse gas measurement, emission reduction, carbon sequestration, and low-emission agricultural practices
      • Greenhouse Gas Measurement and Monitoring -- Methods and systems for measuring and monitoring agricultural greenhouse gas emissions
        • greenhouse gas emissions 21 -- release of gases that contribute to climate change from agricultural activities
        • greenhouse gas measurement 12 -- quantification of emissions from agricultural systems using standardized protocols and equipment
        • greenhouse gas emission measurement 3 -- techniques to quantify agricultural emissions for climate action
        • greenhouse gas emissions measurement 2 -- quantification of carbon dioxide, methane and nitrous oxide emissions from agricultural systems
        • greenhouse gas emissions quantification 1 -- systematic measurement and assessment of agricultural greenhouse gas emissions using bottom-up approaches
        • greenhouse gas emission reduction 2 -- mitigation of climate-warming gases from agricultural practices
        • greenhouse gas inventory 2 -- systematic accounting of emissions and removals by sources and sinks
        • greenhouse gas budgets 1 -- comprehensive accounting of anthropogenic and natural sources and sinks of greenhouse gases
        • emission factors 4 -- standardized coefficients used to estimate greenhouse gas emissions from activities
        • emissions monitoring 4 -- systematic measurement and verification of greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural activities
        • emission monitoring systems 3 -- tools and frameworks for measuring and reporting greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural activities
        • monitoring reporting verification 7 -- framework for tracking and validating environmental compliance
        • monitoring, reporting, and verification 3 -- systems for tracking and validating climate action progress
        • monitoring reporting and verification 1 -- systematic tracking and validation of environmental and agricultural outcomes
        • ipcc tier 2 methodology 3 -- standardized approach for estimating greenhouse gas emissions using country-specific parameters
        • eddy covariance 1 -- micrometeorological technique for measuring ecosystem-scale carbon dioxide and water vapor fluxes
        • emission intensity 1 -- greenhouse gas emissions per unit of agricultural output
        • enteric methane measurement 1 -- direct quantification of methane emissions from livestock digestive systems under field conditions
      • Carbon Sequestration and Carbon Markets -- Carbon storage, accounting, and market mechanisms
        • carbon sequestration 10 -- process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide in soils and vegetation
        • ecosystem carbon sequestration 3 -- natural processes by which ecosystems capture and store atmospheric carbon
        • carbon accounting 4 -- systematic measurement and tracking of carbon emissions and sequestration
        • carbon footprint assessment 3 -- quantification of greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural production systems
        • carbon trading 3 -- market-based mechanism for trading carbon credits to offset greenhouse gas emissions
        • carbon credit certification 2 -- formal verification process that enables farmers to monetize emission reductions through carbon markets
        • carbon market readiness 1 -- capacity building to enable participation in carbon trading mechanisms
        • carbon neutrality 1 -- achieving net-zero carbon dioxide emissions
        • carbon uptake 1 -- process by which ecosystems absorb atmospheric carbon dioxide through photosynthesis
        • soil organic carbon sequestration 2 -- process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon in soil through organic matter accumulation
        • blue carbon opportunities 1 -- carbon sequestration potential in marine and coastal ecosystems
        • bamboo carbon sink 1 -- carbon storage capacity of bamboo forests and plantations
      • Methane and Emission Reduction Practices -- Practices targeting methane and other agricultural emission reductions
        • methane mitigation 6 -- reduction of methane emissions from livestock systems
        • methane emission reduction 2 -- practices to decrease methane gas release from agricultural systems
        • methane emissions 1 -- release of methane gas from livestock digestion processes
        • enteric fermentation 2 -- digestive process in ruminants that produces methane as a byproduct
        • enteric methane reduction 1 -- strategies to reduce methane emissions from livestock digestion
        • low-methane forage breeding 1 -- selection and breeding of feed crops that reduce livestock methane production
        • anti-methanogenic compounds 1 -- substances that reduce methane production in ruminant digestion
        • anti-methanogenic screening 1 -- laboratory testing to identify compounds that reduce methane production
        • alternate wetting and drying 22 -- water-saving rice cultivation technique alternating between flooded and non-flooded field conditions
        • low-emission rice production 1 -- agricultural practices designed to reduce methane emissions from rice cultivation
        • emission intensity per weight gain 1 -- ratio of greenhouse gas emissions to livestock weight gain
      • Low-Carbon and Zero-Carbon Agriculture -- Low-emission and zero-carbon agricultural systems and practices
        • climate change mitigation 12 -- actions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and limit global warming
        • climate mitigation 3 -- actions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and limit climate change
        • low-carbon agriculture 2 -- agricultural systems designed to minimize greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • low-emission agriculture 3 -- farming practices designed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • low-carbon behaviors 3 -- individual and household actions that reduce greenhouse gas emissions
        • zero-carbon agriculture 3 -- farming systems designed to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions
        • zero-carbon villages 1 -- rural communities designed to achieve net-zero carbon emissions
        • just transition 3 -- equitable shift to sustainable economy that leaves no one behind and creates decent work opportunities
        • climate action 18 -- coordinated efforts to address climate change through mitigation and adaptation strategies
        • life cycle assessment 1 -- evaluation of environmental impacts throughout a product's entire life cycle
        • true cost accounting 1 -- comprehensive assessment including environmental and social costs of food production
      • Biogas and Circular Bioeconomy -- Biogas production and circular bioeconomy approaches for emission reduction
        • biogas production 4 -- anaerobic digestion process converting organic matter to methane-rich gas
        • biogas digester performance 3 -- operational efficiency and gas production capacity of small-scale anaerobic digestion systems
        • biogas digester technology 1 -- anaerobic digestion system converting organic waste to energy
        • anaerobic digestion 1 -- biological process that breaks down organic matter in oxygen-free environment to produce biogas
        • biomethane potential 1 -- measure of maximum methane yield from organic feedstock under anaerobic conditions
        • co-digestion 1 -- anaerobic digestion of multiple organic feedstocks together to optimize biogas production
