        • circular bioeconomy 11 -- economic model using biological resources in circular value chains
        • circular economy 7 -- economic model emphasizing resource reuse and waste reduction
        • circular economy solutions 2 -- waste-to-value systems that convert agricultural residues into useful products
        • circular economy principles 1 -- resource management strategies that minimize waste and maximize nutrient retention within production systems
        • circular economy approaches 1 -- resource utilization strategies that minimize waste through reuse and recycling
        • resource recovery and reuse 1 -- circular economy approach to extract value from waste materials including biomass and wastewater
        • bioslurry application 1 -- use of organic waste slurry as fertilizer to enhance crop production and reduce emissions
    • Climate Information and Risk Management -- Climate services, early warning systems, climate risk assessment, seasonal forecasting, and climate advisory services
      • Climate Information Services -- Services providing climate information to farmers and decision-makers
        • climate information services 82 -- delivery of weather and climate data to support decision-making
        • climate services 15 -- provision of climate information and forecasts to support agricultural decision-making
        • agroclimatic information services 7 -- platforms providing weather and climate data for agricultural decision-making
        • climate advisory services 3 -- information systems providing weather and climate guidance for agricultural decision-making
        • digital climate advisory services 3 -- technology-enabled platforms providing climate information and agricultural guidance to farmers
        • agroclimatic information 1 -- weather and climate data specifically relevant for agricultural decision-making
        • agro-climatic bulletin 5 -- weather-based agricultural advisory system providing farmers with climate information for decision-making
        • agroclimatic intelligence 1 -- integration of climate data, ai and agricultural knowledge for decision support
        • weather-informed agro-advisories 1 -- agricultural guidance services that incorporate weather forecast data for farming decisions
        • agro-advisories 1 -- agricultural advice services based on weather and climate information
        • climate-informed decision-making 2 -- incorporating climate data and projections into planning and management
        • climate risk information 2 -- data and analysis about climate-related hazards used for decision-making
      • Climate Forecasting and Early Warning -- Forecasting systems and early warning for climate-related risks
        • early warning systems 35 -- monitoring and forecasting systems to predict and prepare for emergencies
        • climate forecasting 4 -- prediction of future climate conditions to support agricultural and disaster preparedness planning
        • agroclimatic forecasting 3 -- prediction of weather and climate conditions relevant to agricultural planning
        • agroclimatic monitoring 3 -- systematic observation and tracking of climate conditions affecting agricultural production
        • seasonal climate forecast 1 -- long-range weather prediction providing probabilistic outlooks for upcoming growing seasons
        • sub-seasonal forecasting 1 -- weather and climate prediction for timescales of 2 weeks to 2 months
        • rainfall forecasting 1 -- prediction of precipitation patterns to support agricultural decision-making
        • seasonal rainfall forecasting 1 -- prediction of precipitation patterns over seasonal timescales for agricultural planning
        • drought monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of drought conditions using indicators
        • drought prediction 2 -- forecasting techniques to anticipate drought conditions using meteorological and satellite data
        • flood prediction 1 -- forecasting approach to anticipate inundation extent and timing for agricultural risk management
        • impact-based forecasting 1 -- weather prediction approach that focuses on potential impacts rather than just meteorological conditions
        • forecast-based advisory 1 -- decision support system using weather forecasts to guide agricultural practices
        • anticipatory action 9 -- proactive interventions based on early warning signals to prevent crises
        • food crisis early warning 2 -- systems to predict and alert about impending food security emergencies
        • livestock early warning systems 1 -- integrated systems providing seasonal forecasts and decision support for pastoral livestock management
        • seasonal forage forecasting 1 -- prediction of feed availability based on climate and vegetation data for livestock planning
      • Climate Risk Assessment and Vulnerability -- Assessing climate risks and vulnerabilities in agricultural systems
        • climate risk assessment 16 -- evaluation of climate hazard exposure and vulnerability
        • climate risk management 11 -- strategies and practices for identifying, assessing, and mitigating climate-related risks
        • climate risk profiling 4 -- systematic assessment of climate hazards and vulnerabilities for agricultural planning
        • climate vulnerability 9 -- increased susceptibility to climate-related risks and impacts
        • climate vulnerability assessment 3 -- systematic evaluation of exposure, sensitivity, and adaptive capacity to climate risks
        • vulnerability assessment 6 -- systematic evaluation of exposure and susceptibility to risks
        • climate variability 8 -- variations in climate parameters over time scales from months to decades
        • climate variability adaptation 1 -- strategies to adjust agricultural practices to changing weather patterns
        • climate impact projections 2 -- quantitative assessment of expected climate change effects on agricultural systems and crop yields
        • climate attribution 2 -- scientific methods to quantify human influence on extreme weather events
        • hydroclimatic extremes 3 -- severe weather events including droughts, floods, and irregular rainfall patterns affecting agriculture
        • extreme weather events 1 -- severe meteorological conditions that deviate significantly from normal patterns
        • climate hazard mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of current and projected climate risks
        • climate risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis and visualization of climate-related hazards and vulnerabilities
        • climate suitability mapping 2 -- spatial analysis to identify optimal locations for crops under current and future climate conditions
        • spatiotemporal climate analysis 1 -- statistical analysis of climate patterns across space and time dimensions
        • compound risk assessment 1 -- analysis of interconnected and cascading risks across multiple systems
        • drought risk assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of drought hazards, exposure and vulnerability
        • drought risk management 1 -- strategies and systems to anticipate, prepare for, and respond to drought events
        • flood risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas vulnerable to flooding and quantify risk levels
        • vulnerability hotspot mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas of high climate and social risk
        • climate disinformation monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking and response to climate-related misinformation
      • Climate Risk Finance and Insurance -- Financial instruments for managing climate risks
        • index insurance 3 -- insurance products that use objective indices to trigger payouts rather than individual loss assessments
        • weather index-based insurance 1 -- agricultural insurance that pays based on weather parameters rather than actual crop losses
        • satellite-based crop insurance 1 -- risk management mechanism using remote sensing data to assess crop losses and trigger payouts
        • picture-based insurance 1 -- digital crop insurance using smartphone images to assess and indemnify crop damage
        • risk contingent credit 3 -- financial mechanism that links agricultural credit with insurance coverage to manage production risks
        • climate-smart credit scoring 2 -- financial assessment tool incorporating climate resilience factors for agricultural lending
        • climate-linked credit scoring 1 -- financial risk assessment integrating climate data with borrower profiles
        • bundled livestock insurance 1 -- integrated insurance products combining multiple risk protections for pastoral communities
        • disaster risk management 2 -- systematic approach to identifying, assessing, and reducing risks from natural hazards
    • Land, Soil, and Ecosystem Management -- Sustainable land management, ecosystem restoration, soil health, land degradation, and landscape restoration
      • Sustainable Land Management -- Practices for sustainable management of land resources
        • sustainable land management 5 -- land use practices that maintain ecosystem services while meeting human needs
        • land degradation 4 -- decline in land quality and productivity due to human activities and climate factors
        • land restoration 3 -- practices to rehabilitate degraded landscapes and ecosystems
        • landscape restoration 8 -- ecosystem-based interventions to restore degraded land and seascape functions
        • forest landscape restoration 2 -- long-term process of regaining ecological functionality across deforested landscapes
        • forest restoration 2 -- activities to restore degraded forest ecosystems
        • grassland restoration 1 -- rehabilitation of degraded rangeland ecosystems through improved species composition and management
        • rangeland restoration 27 -- practices aimed at rehabilitating degraded rangeland ecosystems to improve productivity and sustainability
        • rangeland management 3 -- sustainable management of grassland ecosystems for livestock production and ecosystem services
        • participatory rangeland management 10 -- community-led approach to managing rangeland resources through stakeholder engagement and collaborative decision-making
        • rangeland governance 2 -- institutional arrangements for managing communal grazing lands and resources
        • sustainable rangeland management 1 -- integrated approach to managing grassland ecosystems for ecological resilience and rural livelihoods
        • land tenure security 1 -- legal and institutional frameworks that provide secure rights to land ownership or use
        • land sparing 2 -- conservation strategy separating intensive agriculture from protected natural areas
        • post-mined land restoration 1 -- rehabilitation of degraded mining sites for productive agricultural use
        • land reclamation 1 -- restoration of degraded land for productive use
        • zero deforestation 1 -- agricultural practices that prevent forest loss while maintaining productive farming systems
        • zero deforestation compliance 2 -- monitoring and verification systems to ensure supply chains do not contribute to forest loss
        • zero deforestation monitoring 1 -- tracking and verification of forest-free agricultural production
        • zero deforestation agreements 1 -- voluntary public-private initiatives to reduce deforestation in agricultural supply chains
        • deforestation monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of forest loss over time using remote sensing and ground data
        • farmer-managed natural regeneration 1 -- restoration technique using indigenous vegetation management by farmers
        • tree-based restoration 1 -- ecosystem rehabilitation using tree planting and forest regeneration techniques
        • multifunctional landscapes 29 -- land management strategy that integrates multiple functions and services across landscapes
        • multifunctional landscape interventions 1 -- integrated interventions addressing multiple functions across agricultural landscapes
        • multifunctional forest landscapes 1 -- forest areas managed to provide multiple ecosystem services and benefits simultaneously
        • landscape performance indicators 1 -- metrics used to assess sustainability and functionality of agricultural landscapes
      • Soil Health and Management -- Soil health assessment, monitoring, and improvement practices
        • soil health 4 -- capacity of soil to function as a living ecosystem supporting plant and animal productivity
        • soil health assessment 7 -- evaluation of soil physical, chemical, and biological properties for agricultural sustainability
        • soil health management 4 -- practices to maintain and improve soil biological, chemical, and physical properties
        • soil health improvement 2 -- enhancement of soil biological, chemical, and physical properties for sustainable productivity
        • soil health monitoring 2 -- systematic assessment of soil biological, chemical, and physical properties for management decisions
        • soil health diagnostics 1 -- systematic assessment of soil physical, chemical and biological properties
        • soil health diagnostics 1 -- systematic assessment of soil physical, chemical and biological properties
        • integrated soil fertility management 4 -- combined use of fertilizers, organic resources, and improved practices for soil health
        • soil organic carbon monitoring 2 -- measurement and tracking of carbon content in agricultural soils
        • soil organic carbon mapping 1 -- spatial assessment of carbon content in soils
        • digital soil mapping 5 -- use of digital tools and algorithms to map soil properties spatially
        • soil erosion risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas prone to soil loss and degradation
        • soil and water conservation 1 -- practices and technologies to protect soil resources and manage water sustainably
        • conservation agriculture 22 -- farming system based on minimal soil disturbance, permanent soil cover and crop rotation
        • zero tillage 3 -- farming practice that eliminates mechanical soil disturbance for planting
        • no-till planting 1 -- planting method that places seeds directly into soil without prior tillage
        • no-tillage farming 1 -- agricultural practice that avoids mechanical soil disturbance to preserve soil structure and moisture
        • no-till conservation agriculture 1 -- farming practice that maintains soil cover and reduces erosion by avoiding tillage
        • zero tillage cultivation 1 -- agricultural practice of growing crops without disturbing soil through tillage
        • zero tillage production 2 -- agricultural practice of growing crops without mechanically disturbing the soil
        • minimum tillage 1 -- reduced soil cultivation practice to maintain soil structure
        • crop residue management 1 -- practices for handling plant material left after harvest to improve soil health and sustainability
        • biochar application 2 -- use of charcoal-like material to improve soil properties and carbon sequestration
        • mineral-associated organic carbon 1 -- soil carbon fraction bound to mineral particles that provides long-term carbon storage
        • soil quality index 1 -- composite measure of soil health based on multiple indicators
        • soil bulk density measurement 1 -- techniques for measuring soil compaction to assess soil health and carbon stocks
        • soil moisture monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking of soil water content using remote sensing or field measurements
        • digital soil sensing 1 -- portable field-based technology for rapid soil property assessment
        • soil spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid, non-destructive assessment of soil properties
        • soil diagnostics 1 -- field-based assessment of soil physical, chemical, and biological characteristics
      • Ecosystem Restoration and Services -- Restoring ecosystems and valuing ecosystem services
        • ecosystem restoration 9 -- rehabilitation of degraded landscapes to restore ecological functions and services
        • ecosystem services valuation 2 -- quantification and monetization of environmental benefits
        • ecosystem service valuation 1 -- economic quantification of benefits provided by natural systems
        • payment for ecosystem services 2 -- economic mechanism compensating land managers for environmental benefits
        • payments for ecosystem services 1 -- economic incentives for conservation and environmental stewardship
        • payment for environmental services 2 -- economic mechanism that compensates land users for providing ecosystem services
        • payments for environmental services 1 -- economic incentive schemes that reward farmers for environmental stewardship
        • eco-compensation 2 -- payment mechanism to compensate for environmental services and conservation efforts
        • ecosystem-based management 1 -- holistic management strategy considering entire ecosystem interactions
        • environmental flows 1 -- water flow regimes required to maintain healthy river ecosystems and their services
        • social-ecological systems 1 -- integrated systems of humans and natural components
        • planetary boundaries 1 -- safe operating limits for Earth system processes that regulate planet stability
        • environmental sustainability 3 -- management practices that maintain ecological integrity while meeting human needs
        • environmental impact assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of environmental effects of agricultural practices
        • sustainability assessment 1 -- evaluation framework measuring environmental, social and economic impacts
      • Agroforestry and Silvopastoral Systems -- Tree-based farming systems integrating crops, livestock, and trees
        • agroforestry 10 -- integration of trees with agricultural crops in production systems
        • silvopastoral systems 15 -- integrated agricultural systems combining trees, livestock, and pastures
        • agrosilvopastoral systems 1 -- integrated land-use systems combining trees, crops, and livestock for multiple benefits
        • agroforestry systems 1 -- integrated land-use combining trees with crops or livestock
        • agroforestry co-design 1 -- collaborative process to design diversified tree-crop systems aligned with producer needs
        • agroforestry monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking of tree planting and forest management activities
    • Water Resources and Governance -- Water governance, groundwater management, integrated water resources management, and transboundary water issues
      • Water Governance and Management -- Governance frameworks and management approaches for water resources
        • water governance 1 -- institutional frameworks and processes for managing water resources
        • integrated water resources management 2 -- coordinated development and management of water, land and related resources
        • groundwater management 5 -- sustainable use and conservation of underground water resources
        • groundwater governance 2 -- institutional frameworks and practices for sustainable groundwater resource management
        • transboundary water management 3 -- coordinated governance of water resources across national or regional boundaries
        • global water governance 1 -- international frameworks and institutions for managing water resources across borders
        • inclusive water governance 2 -- water management frameworks that ensure equitable participation and benefit-sharing
        • community-led water governance 1 -- participatory management of water resources through strategic infrastructure operation
        • polycentric governance 1 -- multi-level decision-making involving multiple centers of authority
        • water management 8 -- strategies for efficient use and conservation of water resources in agriculture
        • water management planning 3 -- strategic allocation and scheduling of water resources for agricultural production
        • agricultural water management 2 -- integrated approach to managing water resources for crop production
        • water-energy-food nexus 8 -- integrated management of water, energy and food systems
        • water-energy-food-environment nexus 3 -- integrated framework for managing interconnected water, energy, food and environmental systems
        • water-energy-food-ecosystems nexus 3 -- integrated approach addressing interconnections between water, energy, food, and ecosystem management
        • water-energy-food-ecosystem nexus 2 -- integrated management of interconnected resource systems
        • water justice 1 -- equitable access to water resources and inclusive decision-making in water management
        • gender-inclusive water management 1 -- water governance approaches that ensure equitable participation of women in decision-making
        • evidence-based water resource management 1 -- water planning and allocation using data-driven analysis and modeling
      • Water Productivity and Efficiency -- Improving water use efficiency and productivity in agriculture
        • water productivity 10 -- efficiency of water use in producing agricultural output per unit of water consumed
        • water-use efficiency 10 -- optimization of water resources to maximize agricultural productivity per unit of water consumed
        • water productivity optimization 1 -- maximizing agricultural output per unit of water used
        • irrigation water productivity 2 -- measure of crop yield per unit of irrigation water applied
        • water-efficient farming 3 -- agricultural techniques that optimize water use while maintaining or improving crop productivity
        • water-efficient practices 2 -- techniques and methods to optimize water use in agricultural systems
        • water-efficient agriculture 2 -- farming practices that optimize water use while maintaining productivity
        • water-saving technologies 1 -- innovations and practices that reduce agricultural water consumption
        • water-saving irrigation 1 -- irrigation techniques that reduce water consumption while maintaining crop productivity
        • water accounting 9 -- systematic assessment and monitoring of water resources and their utilization
        • water footprint assessment 1 -- evaluation of green and blue water consumption patterns in agricultural systems
        • water consumption mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of water use patterns in agricultural systems
        • water balance model 2 -- computational framework quantifying water inputs, outputs, and storage changes in agricultural systems
      • Irrigation and Water Infrastructure -- Irrigation systems and water infrastructure for agriculture
        • solar irrigation 8 -- irrigation systems powered by solar energy for sustainable water management
        • solar-powered irrigation 5 -- irrigation systems powered by solar energy for sustainable water management
        • solar-based irrigation 2 -- water delivery system powered by solar energy for agricultural production
        • solar pump irrigation 1 -- irrigation system powered by solar energy for water pumping and distribution
        • small-scale irrigation 3 -- water management systems for smallholder agricultural production
        • supplemental irrigation 2 -- strategic water application to supplement rainfall in dryland systems
        • supplementary irrigation 1 -- targeted water application during critical crop growth stages in rainfed systems
        • drip irrigation 1 -- water-efficient irrigation method that delivers water directly to plant roots
        • micro-drip irrigation 1 -- water-efficient irrigation method delivering small amounts of water directly to plant roots
        • farmer-led irrigation development 2 -- smallholder-driven investment and management of irrigation systems
        • irrigation demand forecasting 6 -- prediction of crop water requirements to optimize irrigation scheduling and water allocation
        • irrigation demand management 1 -- strategies to optimize water use efficiency in irrigation systems
        • irrigation-as-a-service 2 -- business model where irrigation services are provided by third parties rather than owned by farmers
        • climate-resilient water infrastructure 1 -- water systems designed to withstand and adapt to climate change impacts
        • rainwater harvesting 3 -- collection and storage of rainwater for agricultural use
        • water harvesting 1 -- collection and storage of rainwater or surface water for agricultural use
        • managed aquifer recharge 2 -- intentional replenishment of groundwater through controlled water infiltration
        • groundwater recharge schemes 1 -- interventions designed to replenish depleted groundwater resources
        • groundwater integration 1 -- systematic inclusion of groundwater considerations in water service planning
        • conjunctive water use 1 -- combined use of different water sources to optimize water resource management
        • floodwater management 1 -- strategies for controlling and utilizing flood waters for agricultural benefits
        • drainage infrastructure 1 -- water management systems designed to remove excess water from agricultural land
        • constructed wetlands 1 -- engineered systems mimicking natural wetlands for water treatment
        • polder zone management 1 -- agricultural and water management strategies for coastal reclaimed land areas
        • community-engaged sluice gate operation 1 -- participatory water management involving local communities in infrastructure control
        • digital water innovations 1 -- technology-based solutions for water monitoring and management
        • digital water body inventory 1 -- interactive platform mapping and managing water infrastructure using satellite data
        • water monitoring system 1 -- integrated platform for tracking and analyzing water resource availability and use
        • water quality monitoring 1 -- systematic assessment of water parameters to ensure suitability for aquaculture and other uses
        • hydrological monitoring 1 -- systematic measurement and tracking of water levels and flow in water bodies
        • integrated water storage assessment 1 -- comprehensive evaluation of multiple water storage types and their temporal dynamics for resource management
        • reservoir forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling of future water storage levels using multiple data sources
        • aquifer recharge zone mapping 1 -- identification and mapping of areas where groundwater can be naturally replenished
        • water-as-a-service 1 -- business model providing irrigation services rather than selling equipment
        • smart valleys approach 5 -- integrated water control system for inland valley rice production
      • Hydrological Modeling and Analysis -- Modeling and analysis of hydrological systems
        • hydrological modeling 3 -- mathematical simulation of water cycle processes for water resource planning and management
        • hydrological modelling 1 -- mathematical simulation of water cycle processes for water resource management
        • hydro-economic modeling 1 -- integrated analysis combining hydrological and economic data for water resource management
        • watershed modeling 1 -- simulation of water flow and management at landscape scale
        • flood modeling 1 -- computational simulation of water flow patterns and flood behavior
        • integrated catchment management 1 -- landscape-level approach integrating land, water, forestry and livestock management
        • salinity intrusion 3 -- movement of salt water into freshwater areas affecting soil and crop production
        • salinity management 2 -- strategies and technologies to address soil and water salinity in agricultural systems
    • Biodiversity and Agrobiodiversity -- Biodiversity conservation, agrobiodiversity monitoring, ecosystem services, and biodiversity-positive practices
      • Biodiversity Conservation -- Conservation of biological diversity in agricultural landscapes
        • biodiversity conservation 6 -- protection and sustainable management of biological diversity
        • agrobiodiversity conservation 3 -- preservation of genetic diversity in agricultural systems
        • agrobiodiversity monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking of crop diversity and genetic resources in food systems
        • systematic agrobiodiversity monitoring 2 -- standardized tracking of crop diversity conservation status over time and space
        • biodiversity restoration 1 -- recovery of native species diversity and ecosystem function in degraded landscapes
        • biodiversity integration 1 -- incorporation of biological diversity principles into agricultural systems
        • biodiversity-positive financing 1 -- investment mechanisms that channel finance toward biodiversity conservation and sustainable livelihoods
        • biodiversity nexus 1 -- interconnected relationships between biodiversity and multiple sectors
        • on-farm conservation 3 -- maintaining crop genetic diversity through cultivation on farms
        • community seed banks 4 -- locally managed seed conservation and distribution systems
        • indigenous variety conservation 2 -- preservation and promotion of traditional crop varieties
        • agrobiodiversity mainstreaming 1 -- integration of crop diversity concepts into education and policy systems
        • environmental dna monitoring 1 -- biodiversity assessment using genetic material extracted from environmental samples
        • pollinator protection 1 -- conservation strategies to support pollinating insects
        • habitat enhancement 1 -- modification of agricultural landscapes to support beneficial organisms
        • pollination services 1 -- ecosystem service provided by pollinators for plant reproduction
      • Landscape and Ecosystem Approaches -- Landscape-level approaches integrating biodiversity and production
        • nexus approach 3 -- integrated analysis of water-energy-food-environment interactions and trade-offs
        • traditional ecological knowledge 1 -- indigenous knowledge systems about environmental relationships and resource management
        • traditional knowledge systems 2 -- indigenous practices and understanding passed through generations
        • proximity to natural habitat hypothesis 1 -- theory that nearness to natural areas enhances pollination services in agricultural systems
        • carrying capacity 1 -- maximum livestock density that land can sustainably support
        • bush encroachment 1 -- invasion of grasslands by woody plants reducing grazing capacity
        • rotational grazing 3 -- systematic movement of livestock between pasture areas
        • pastoral mobility 2 -- traditional practice of moving livestock to access seasonal grazing resources
        • pastoral resource management 1 -- practices for managing grazing lands and livestock in pastoral systems
        • pastoral adaptation 2 -- changes in pastoralist practices to cope with environmental and climate challenges
        • agropastoralism 1 -- integrated farming system combining crop production and livestock keeping
    • Climate Policy and Finance -- Climate finance, nationally determined contributions, climate policy integration, and adaptation finance mechanisms
      • Climate Policy and Governance -- Policy frameworks and governance for climate action
        • nationally determined contributions 20 -- country-specific climate action plans under the Paris Agreement
        • climate policy integration 11 -- systematic inclusion of sector-specific solutions into national and international climate strategies
        • climate governance 1 -- institutional frameworks and processes for climate change decision-making
        • climate policy coordination 1 -- systematic coordination of climate action and policy implementation across government levels
        • climate negotiations 1 -- international diplomatic processes to address climate change
        • climate justice 2 -- fair treatment of all people regarding climate change impacts and solutions
        • environmental justice 2 -- equitable distribution of environmental benefits and burdens across social groups
        • environmental peacebuilding 2 -- use of environmental cooperation to promote peace and social cohesion in post-conflict areas
        • climate loss and damage 1 -- economic and non-economic losses from climate change impacts that cannot be avoided through adaptation
        • loss and damage assessment 1 -- quantification of climate-related losses that cannot be avoided through adaptation
      • Climate Finance -- Finance mechanisms and tracking for climate action
        • climate finance 11 -- financial mechanisms including carbon credits and international funds to support climate solutions
        • climate finance mechanisms 4 -- financial instruments and structures designed to fund climate mitigation and adaptation projects
        • climate finance tracking 6 -- systems for monitoring flow and use of climate-related funding
        • climate finance mobilization 1 -- strategies to attract and deploy financial resources for climate adaptation and mitigation
        • climate finance taxonomy 1 -- classification framework for directing climate investments toward specific sectors and activities
        • blended finance 8 -- combination of public and private funding to reduce investment risks
        • adaptation finance 3 -- financial resources dedicated to climate change adaptation activities
    • Remote Sensing and Earth Observation for Environment -- Remote sensing and earth observation tools applied to environmental monitoring
      • Satellite and Remote Sensing Applications -- Satellite-based monitoring of environmental and agricultural systems
        • satellite remote sensing 8 -- use of satellite imagery and radar data to monitor agricultural systems
        • remote sensing 5 -- use of satellite or aerial imagery for agricultural monitoring
        • earth observation 6 -- use of satellite and remote sensing technologies to monitor environmental and agricultural parameters
        • earth observation integration 2 -- combining satellite data with ground-based information for agricultural monitoring
        • satellite-based monitoring 3 -- use of satellite imagery and remote sensing for crop and weather monitoring
        • satellite monitoring 2 -- use of satellite imagery and data for agricultural and environmental monitoring
        • satellite imagery analysis 2 -- use of satellite data to monitor crop conditions and agricultural landscapes
        • synthetic aperture radar 3 -- remote sensing technique using radar signals to monitor surface conditions and vegetation
        • hyperspectral remote sensing 1 -- imaging technology using multiple spectral bands to detect crop conditions
        • uav-based remote sensing 1 -- collection of agricultural data using unmanned aerial vehicles equipped with sensors
        • drone-based monitoring 2 -- use of unmanned aerial vehicles for data collection and measurement
        • open data cube architecture 1 -- structured system for organizing and accessing large spatial datasets
        • spatiotemporal asset catalog 1 -- standardized metadata specification for spatiotemporal data assets
        • canopy height modeling 1 -- estimation of crop structural parameters using remote sensing or other technologies
        • crop type mapping 2 -- remote sensing identification of agricultural land use patterns
        • geospatial analysis 2 -- examination of spatial data to understand geographic patterns and relationships
        • geospatial data analysis 1 -- spatial analysis techniques to map socio-ecological variables and inform interventions
        • spatial analytics 1 -- analytical approaches combining geography, economics and data science for policy insights
        • spatial analysis 1 -- geographic data analysis to understand spatial patterns and relationships
        • spatial clustering 1 -- grouping of geographic units based on similarity of characteristics
        • spatial prioritization 1 -- geographic targeting based on spatial analysis
    • Sustainable Agricultural Practices for Environment -- Agricultural practices with environmental sustainability outcomes
      • Sustainable Intensification and Diversification -- Intensifying production while maintaining environmental sustainability
        • sustainable intensification 5 -- increasing agricultural productivity while maintaining environmental sustainability
        • sustainable agriculture 7 -- farming practices that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
        • sustainable agricultural practices 6 -- farming methods that maintain productivity while conserving resources and enhancing resilience
        • crop diversification 21 -- agricultural strategy of growing multiple crop species to reduce risk and improve sustainability
        • intercropping 7 -- growing multiple crop species simultaneously in the same field
        • intercropping systems 4 -- agricultural practice of growing multiple crops simultaneously in the same field
        • crop rotation 1 -- sequential cultivation of different crops to improve soil health and productivity
        • integrated farming systems 12 -- agricultural approach combining multiple enterprises for synergistic benefits
        • integrated crop management 4 -- holistic farming approach combining pest, disease, and soil health management practices
        • nitrogen use efficiency 5 -- crop's ability to convert applied nitrogen into grain yield
        • nitrogen management 3 -- optimization of nitrogen fertilizer application rates and timing
        • biological nitrification inhibition 3 -- natural process that hinders soil nitrification to improve nitrogen use efficiency
        • organic farming 5 -- agricultural production system avoiding synthetic inputs and emphasizing natural processes
        • sustainable livestock practices 4 -- animal husbandry methods that balance productivity with environmental and social sustainability
        • sustainable cattle ranching 1 -- livestock production practices that minimize environmental impact while maintaining productivity
        • sustainable rice cultivation 2 -- rice production practices that balance environmental, economic and social considerations
        • sustainable production practices 1 -- farming methods that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
        • sustainable food systems 4 -- integrated agricultural systems that address climate, nutrition, and social challenges simultaneously
        • sustainable agricultural mechanization 2 -- use of machinery and equipment that reduces environmental impact while improving productivity
        • regenerative agricultural practices 1 -- farming methods that restore soil health and ecosystem function through practices like no-till, cover cropping, and crop rotation
        • nature-positive agriculture 3 -- farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem services while maintaining productivity
      • Composting and Organic Inputs -- Organic waste management and composting for soil health
        • composting 1 -- biological decomposition of organic matter into nutrient-rich soil amendment
        • systematic composting 2 -- structured method for producing compost using organic materials and training
        • aerobic composting 1 -- controlled decomposition of organic matter in the presence of oxygen to produce fertilizer
        • vermicompost production 1 -- composting process using earthworms to decompose organic materials
        • black soldier fly larvae composting 1 -- biotechnology using insect larvae to convert organic waste into protein-rich feed and organic fertilizers
        • green manuring 1 -- practice of growing and incorporating plants to improve soil fertility
        • straw management 1 -- practices for handling crop residues to optimize benefits and reduce emissions
        • rice straw transformation 1 -- converting rice straw waste into valuable products for circular economy applications
  • Food Systems, Nutrition, and Food Security -- Covers the transformation of food systems, nutrition outcomes, food safety, and food security across populations
    • Food Systems Transformation -- Sustainable food systems, agrifood system transformation, food sovereignty, and systems analytics
      • Food System Frameworks and Approaches -- Conceptual and analytical frameworks for food system transformation
        • food systems transformation 26 -- fundamental changes in how food is produced, distributed, and consumed
        • food system transformation 9 -- comprehensive change in how food is produced, processed, distributed, and consumed
        • sustainable food systems 4 -- integrated agricultural systems that address climate, nutrition, and social challenges simultaneously
        • food systems 5 -- interconnected processes from food production through consumption and disposal
        • food system analytics 3 -- data-driven analysis of food production, distribution, and consumption systems
        • agrifood system transformation 2 -- comprehensive changes to make food systems more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive
        • food sovereignty 1 -- community control over food production systems and nutritional self-determination
        • food systems resilience 4 -- capacity of food systems to withstand and recover from shocks while maintaining functionality
        • food systems leadership 2 -- leadership development and behaviors within agricultural and food system contexts
        • food systems foresight 1 -- strategic planning methodology for anticipating future food system changes
        • aquatic food systems 7 -- integrated networks of fisheries and aquaculture production, processing, and distribution
        • island food systems 2 -- food production and distribution systems specific to island environments
        • indigenous food systems 1 -- traditional food production and consumption practices of local communities
        • frontier food systems 1 -- food systems at the edges of current research and investment priorities
      • Value Chain Development -- Value chain analysis, upgrading, and market systems for food
        • value chain development 38 -- systematic improvement of market linkages and business relationships
        • value chain analysis 20 -- examination of production to consumption linkages and market dynamics
        • value chain upgrading 5 -- improving quality, coordination, and market linkages across agricultural supply chains
        • value chain mapping 2 -- systematic analysis of actors, relationships, and flows in agricultural commodity chains
        • value chain coordination 1 -- systematic organization of production, processing, and distribution activities
        • value chain governance 1 -- coordination mechanisms and power relationships among actors in agricultural value chains
        • nutrition-sensitive value chain 2 -- approach to analyze and improve nutritional outcomes along food value chains
        • livestock value chains 5 -- sequence of activities from livestock production to final consumption
        • market systems development 2 -- intervention strategy that addresses systemic barriers in market functioning
        • market systems analysis 4 -- systematic examination of market structures, actors, and dynamics
        • supply chain mapping 1 -- systematic identification and analysis of food system bottlenecks and flows
        • supply chain analysis 1 -- examination of production and trade networks and their impacts
      • Food Market Systems -- Market monitoring, intelligence, and food market interventions
        • market monitoring 9 -- systematic tracking and analysis of commodity prices and trade flows
        • market intelligence 11 -- systematic collection and analysis of market data to inform strategic decisions
        • market price monitoring 5 -- systematic tracking of commodity prices and market conditions
        • market assessment 3 -- systematic evaluation of supply, demand, and market dynamics for specific products or sectors
        • market information systems 1 -- platforms for collecting and disseminating agricultural price and market data
        • market linkages 1 -- connections between producers and buyers to facilitate trade
        • market linkage facilitation 1 -- connecting producers with buyers and markets to improve commercialization
        • nutrition-sensitive markets 1 -- market systems that prioritize and reward nutritional quality in agricultural products
        • food subsidies 1 -- government support to reduce food costs for targeted populations
        • institutional procurement 2 -- structured purchasing by organizations like schools or governments
        • home-grown school feeding 4 -- programs linking local food production with school nutrition
        • school feeding programs 6 -- institutional programs providing nutritious meals to school children
    • Nutrition Security and Dietary Quality -- Nutrition-sensitive agriculture, dietary diversity, biofortification, and malnutrition reduction
      • Nutrition-Sensitive Approaches -- Agriculture and food system approaches to improve nutrition outcomes
        • nutrition-sensitive agriculture 9 -- agricultural practices designed to improve dietary diversity and nutritional outcomes
        • nutrition-sensitive interventions 2 -- programs addressing underlying causes of malnutrition
        • gender-climate-nutrition integration 3 -- holistic approach linking gender equity, climate resilience, and nutrition outcomes
        • nutrition security 2 -- reliable access to adequate, safe, and nutritious food for healthy living
        • nutritional security 2 -- access to adequate nutrition and essential micronutrients
        • nutritional status 2 -- condition of health determined by nutrient intake and utilization
        • nutritional enhancement 5 -- improving the nutritional content of foods through ingredient modification or fortification
        • nutritional quality assessment 2 -- evaluation of nutrient content and bioavailability in crop products
        • micronutrient fortification 1 -- addition of essential vitamins and minerals to food products to address nutritional deficiencies
        • biofortification 41 -- enhancing nutritional content of crops through breeding
        • hidden hunger 1 -- micronutrient deficiencies despite adequate caloric intake
        • double burden of malnutrition 3 -- coexistence of under- and overnutrition within individuals, households, and populations
        • child malnutrition 2 -- inadequate nutrition in children leading to stunting, wasting, or underweight conditions
        • malnutrition burden 1 -- coexistence of undernutrition, micronutrient deficiencies, and overweight/obesity
        • malnutrition forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling to anticipate acute child malnutrition for early warning systems
      • Dietary Assessment and Quality -- Methods and tools for assessing dietary diversity and quality
        • dietary diversity 3 -- variety of foods consumed across different food groups
        • dietary assessment 7 -- measurement and evaluation of food and nutrient intake patterns
        • diet quality assessment 6 -- systematic evaluation of nutritional adequacy in food consumption
        • dietary diversity assessment 2 -- measurement of variety in food consumption patterns
        • diet quality monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking and assessment of nutritional quality in food consumption patterns
        • food diversity 3 -- variety of food types available within a given area or system
        • minimum dietary diversity 1 -- indicator measuring consumption across different food groups as proxy for nutrient adequacy
        • nutrition education 2 -- structured learning programs to improve dietary knowledge and behavior
        • protein consumption adequacy 1 -- meeting recommended dietary protein intake levels
        • nutritional adequacy ratio 1 -- measure of household food security based on nutritional requirements
        • micronutrient contribution modeling 1 -- analytical approach to calculate and visualize nutrient composition of food sources
        • micronutrient adequacy projection 1 -- modeling future nutritional status and micronutrient availability
        • cost of healthy diet analysis 1 -- economic assessment of affordability and access to nutritious food
        • planetary health diet 1 -- sustainable eating pattern that promotes human health within planetary boundaries
        • eat-lancet diet 1 -- planetary health diet framework balancing human nutrition and environmental sustainability
        • glycemic index modification 1 -- breeding strategy to alter blood sugar response of food crops
        • protein digestibility 1 -- measure of how well proteins can be broken down and absorbed by the body
      • Dietary Behavior and Interventions -- Behavior change and interventions to improve dietary practices
        • social behavior change communication 4 -- strategic communication approach to influence behaviors and social norms
        • behavioral change communication 2 -- interventions designed to influence individual decision-making and behavior
        • behavior change communication 1 -- strategic communication approach to influence individual and community behaviors
        • behavioral interventions 1 -- psychological approaches to influence farmer decision-making
        • behavioral nudges 1 -- interventions that guide behavior through choice architecture without restricting options
        • fruit and vegetable promotion 6 -- interventions designed to increase consumption of fruits and vegetables
        • school meal diversification 2 -- integration of diverse local crops into institutional feeding programs
        • home gardening 1 -- small-scale food production at household level for nutrition and food security
        • kitchen gardening 1 -- small-scale vegetable production for household nutrition and income
        • home garden intensification 1 -- systematic enhancement of household food production through integrated crop-livestock systems
        • ultra-processed foods 2 -- industrially processed foods contributing to poor diet quality and health outcomes
        • gluten-free products 1 -- food products free from gluten proteins for celiac disease management
        • alternative flour blending 1 -- combining different flour types to improve nutritional and functional properties of food products
    • Food Safety and Quality -- Food safety monitoring, quality assurance, traceability, and food safety management systems
      • Food Safety Monitoring and Management -- Systems and approaches for monitoring and managing food safety
        • food safety 6 -- assurance that food will not cause harm when prepared or consumed
        • food safety monitoring 2 -- systematic surveillance of contaminants and hazards in food systems
        • food safety management 2 -- systematic approach to ensuring safe food production and handling
        • food safety training 1 -- education programs to improve food handling and safety practices
        • quality assurance 2 -- systematic processes to ensure products or services meet specified standards
        • quality control 2 -- systematic processes to ensure product standards
        • quality management systems 1 -- structured framework for ensuring consistent product quality and safety standards
        • good agricultural practices 9 -- science-backed farming techniques including timely planting, optimal fertilizer rates, and improved varieties
        • good agronomic practices 2 -- evidence-based crop management techniques for optimal production
        • biosecurity 7 -- measures to prevent introduction and spread of diseases in livestock
        • biosecurity practices 2 -- preventive measures to reduce disease transmission in livestock production
        • biosecurity measures 1 -- practices to prevent introduction and spread of diseases in livestock systems
        • biosecurity assessment 1 -- evaluation of measures to prevent pathogen introduction and spread
        • phytosanitary measures 2 -- regulations and practices to prevent spread of plant pests and diseases
        • phytosanitary regulation 1 -- policies and standards for preventing plant disease spread through seed systems
        • phytosanitary testing 1 -- laboratory procedures to detect plant pathogens and ensure plant health
        • hygiene oversight 1 -- systematic monitoring and enforcement of sanitation standards
      • Contaminant and Residue Management -- Management of food contaminants, residues, and pathogens
        • aflatoxin contamination 1 -- presence of toxic compounds produced by fungi that cause liver cancer and stunting
        • pesticide residue detection 1 -- analytical techniques to identify and quantify chemical residues in crops
        • antimicrobial resistance 5 -- ability of microorganisms to survive exposure to antimicrobial drugs
        • antimicrobial resistance surveillance 11 -- systematic monitoring of antimicrobial resistance patterns in pathogens to inform public health responses
        • antimicrobial stewardship 6 -- coordinated interventions to optimize antimicrobial use and minimize resistance development
        • antibiotic resistance monitoring 3 -- surveillance and measurement of antimicrobial resistance in bacterial populations
      • Traceability and Quality Assessment -- Traceability systems and food quality assessment methods
        • traceability 2 -- ability to track products through supply chain stages
        • traceability systems 1 -- systems for tracking products through supply chains
        • food quality assessment 1 -- evaluation of nutritional, biochemical, and sensory attributes of food products
        • sensory evaluation 1 -- systematic assessment of food products using human senses to determine consumer acceptance
        • grain quality 2 -- nutritional and processing characteristics of cereal grains
        • grain quality assessment 1 -- evaluation of physical and chemical properties affecting grain value
        • quality testing 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop grain quality parameters
        • near-infrared spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid quality assessment using light absorption
        • iso/iec 17025 1 -- international standard for testing and calibration laboratory competence
        • score-on-doors rating 1 -- public disclosure system for food safety inspection results
    • Post-Harvest Management and Loss Reduction -- Post-harvest technology, storage, processing, food loss and waste reduction, and value addition
      • Post-Harvest Technology and Storage -- Technologies and practices for post-harvest handling and storage
        • post-harvest technology 2 -- methods and equipment for processing, preserving, and storing agricultural products
        • post-harvest management 1 -- practices and technologies for handling, processing and storing crops after harvest
        • post-harvest processing 3 -- handling and treatment of crops after harvest
        • post-harvest losses 5 -- reduction in crop quantity or quality after harvest
        • post-harvest loss reduction 1 -- minimizing food waste and quality degradation after crop harvest through improved handling
        • postharvest loss reduction 2 -- strategies to minimize food waste after harvest
        • post-harvest food loss 1 -- reduction in food quantity or quality that occurs after harvest but before consumption
        • hermetic storage 1 -- airtight storage systems that prevent pest infestation and maintain grain quality
        • cold chain management 1 -- temperature-controlled supply chain for preserving vaccine potency
        • cold chain transportation 1 -- temperature-controlled logistics system for preserving product quality during transport
        • on-farm seed storage 1 -- farmer-managed preservation of seeds between harvest and planting
        • two-step drying 1 -- seed processing technique using initial warm drying followed by cooler drying to improve storage longevity
        • parboiling 1 -- rice processing technique involving partial boiling in the husk to improve nutritional quality
        • gem parboiling technique 2 -- improved rice processing method for enhanced quality and efficiency
        • rice straw transformation 1 -- converting rice straw waste into valuable products for circular economy applications
      • Food Loss and Waste Reduction -- Approaches to reduce food loss and waste across the food system
        • food loss and waste reduction 1 -- strategies to minimize food losses along value chains and consumer waste
        • food rescue 2 -- recovery and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve food security
        • food recovery 2 -- systematic collection and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve access
        • waste-to-value technologies 1 -- methods to convert organic waste into useful products like compost, biogas, or protein
        • waste-to-feed conversion 1 -- process of converting agricultural by-products into livestock feed
        • circular economy 7 -- economic model emphasizing resource reuse and waste reduction
        • circular economy solutions 2 -- waste-to-value systems that convert agricultural residues into useful products
        • circular economy approaches 1 -- resource utilization strategies that minimize waste through reuse and recycling
        • circular economy principles 1 -- resource management strategies that minimize waste and maximize nutrient retention within production systems
        • waste hierarchy 1 -- prioritization framework emphasizing prevention, reduction, reuse, and recycling for waste management
        • resource recovery and reuse 1 -- circular economy approach to extract value from waste materials including biomass and wastewater
      • Food Processing and Value Addition -- Processing technologies and value addition for food products
        • fish value addition 1 -- processing and enhancement techniques to increase economic value of fish products
        • feed processing 2 -- techniques to improve nutritional quality and preservation of animal feeds
        • feed formulation 1 -- process of combining ingredients to create nutritionally balanced animal feed
        • feed pellet production 1 -- process of converting organic waste into nutritious animal feed through composting
        • geographical indications 1 -- certification system linking product quality and characteristics to specific geographic origins
        • organic certification 4 -- formal verification process ensuring agricultural products meet organic production standards
        • quality declared seed 1 -- seed certification system ensuring genetic purity and quality standards
    • Food Environment and Consumer Behavior -- Food environment assessment, consumer preferences, dietary behavior change, and food access interventions
      • Food Environment Assessment -- Assessment and analysis of food environments
        • food environment assessment 2 -- evaluation of food availability, accessibility, and quality in communities
        • food environment analysis 4 -- study of factors affecting food accessibility, availability, and affordability
        • food environment intervention 2 -- strategies to modify food availability, accessibility, and affordability
        • food environment 1 -- physical and social surroundings that influence food access and choices
        • built food environment 2 -- physical infrastructure and retail outlets that determine food access and availability
        • informal food vendors 1 -- unregistered small-scale food sellers operating in urban environments
        • vendor neighborhood analysis 1 -- spatial assessment of food diversity within defined radii around food vendors
        • urban food security 2 -- reliable access to adequate food in urban environments
        • urban farming 2 -- agricultural production within urban environments to enhance local food security
        • urban agriculture 2 -- food production activities within urban and peri-urban areas
      • Consumer Preferences and Behavior -- Consumer preference research and behavior change approaches
        • consumer preference mapping 2 -- systematic assessment of consumer preferences for product traits and characteristics
        • willingness to pay 3 -- economic measure of maximum amount consumers will pay for a good or service
        • willingness to pay assessment 1 -- economic evaluation of farmer demand for agricultural technologies
        • discrete choice experiments 3 -- statistical method to elicit preferences by presenting respondents with choice scenarios
        • contingent valuation 2 -- survey-based method to estimate economic value of non-market goods
        • experimental auctions 1 -- controlled market experiments to determine economic preferences
        • vickrey auction 1 -- sealed-bid auction mechanism for economic valuation where highest bidder pays second-highest price
        • choice experiment 1 -- research method using hypothetical scenarios to elicit preferences
        • shop-along methodology 1 -- observational research method following people during food shopping experiences
        • sensory evaluation 1 -- systematic assessment of food products using human senses to determine consumer acceptance
        • product concept testing 1 -- evaluation of new product ideas with target users
        • video-based product concept testing 1 -- market research method using videos to test farmer preferences for hypothetical crop products
        • behaviour change wheel 1 -- framework for understanding drivers of behavior change in agricultural adoption
        • behavior change model 1 -- framework for understanding and facilitating adoption of new practices through capability, opportunity, and motivation
        • social norms 2 -- shared expectations and rules that guide behavior within groups
      • Food Access and Social Protection -- Interventions to improve food access and social protection for food security
        • social protection 12 -- policies and programs designed to reduce poverty and vulnerability
        • cash transfers 2 -- direct monetary payments to individuals or households as social assistance
        • cash transfer programs 2 -- direct monetary assistance provided to households to improve welfare outcomes
        • digital cash transfers 1 -- electronic delivery of monetary assistance to beneficiaries
        • digital transfers 2 -- electronic delivery of cash assistance through mobile money and digital platforms
        • tapered cash transfers 1 -- gradually diminishing cash payments designed to support transition to self-reliance
        • shock-responsive social protection 1 -- social safety net programs that can rapidly scale up during emergencies
        • adaptive social protection 1 -- social safety nets that adjust to climate and economic shocks
        • social protection systems 1 -- government programs providing support to vulnerable populations during crises
        • social protection policy 1 -- government frameworks for providing assistance to vulnerable populations
        • social transfer programs 1 -- government programs providing financial or in-kind support to households
        • ultra-poor graduation model 3 -- integrated intervention combining cash transfers, assets, training, and coaching for extreme poverty
        • lean season assistance 1 -- humanitarian support during periods when food stocks are depleted and prices peak
        • economic inclusion 1 -- expanding access to economic opportunities for marginalized populations
    • Food Security Monitoring and Early Warning -- Food security monitoring, food crisis prediction, early warning systems, and food insecurity measurement
      • Food Security Measurement and Monitoring -- Tools and methods for measuring and monitoring food security
        • food security 91 -- reliable access to sufficient nutritious food
        • food security monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of food access, availability, and utilization indicators
        • food insecurity 3 -- lack of reliable access to sufficient quantities of affordable, nutritious food
        • food insecurity experience scale 1 -- standardized tool for measuring household food access and security
        • vulnerability assessment 6 -- systematic evaluation of exposure and susceptibility to risks
        • vulnerability analysis 2 -- assessment of susceptibility to climate and environmental stresses
        • vulnerability indexing 1 -- quantitative assessment of susceptibility to adverse conditions
        • computable general equilibrium modeling 5 -- economic modeling approach for policy impact analysis
        • social accounting matrix 2 -- comprehensive economic accounting framework linking production, income and expenditure flows
        • economywide modeling 1 -- comprehensive economic analysis across all sectors of an economy
        • equilibrium displacement modeling 1 -- economic analysis method examining market interactions and price effects
        • economic surplus modeling 1 -- analytical approach to quantify economic welfare impacts and benefits of interventions
      • Early Warning and Crisis Prediction -- Systems for early warning and prediction of food crises
        • early warning systems 35 -- monitoring and forecasting systems to predict and prepare for emergencies
        • food crisis prediction 3 -- analytical approaches for forecasting food security emergencies
        • food crisis early warning 2 -- systems to predict and alert about impending food security emergencies
        • anticipatory action 9 -- proactive interventions based on early warning signals to prevent crises
        • artificial intelligence famine forecasting 1 -- machine learning approaches to predict famine conditions and food crises
        • malnutrition forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling to anticipate acute child malnutrition for early warning systems
        • livestock early warning systems 1 -- integrated systems providing seasonal forecasts and decision support for pastoral livestock management
        • impact-based forecasting 1 -- weather prediction approach that focuses on potential impacts rather than just meteorological conditions
      • Food Security in Fragile Contexts -- Food security approaches in conflict-affected and fragile settings
        • fragile and conflict-affected settings 6 -- contexts characterized by violence, weak institutions and humanitarian crises
        • fragile and conflict-affected systems 2 -- food systems operating in contexts of institutional fragility and conflict
        • conflict-sensitive programming 6 -- development approach that considers potential conflict risks and promotes peace-building
        • conflict-affected settings 2 -- regions experiencing or recovering from armed conflict or civil unrest
        • humanitarian response 2 -- coordinated assistance to populations affected by crises and disasters
        • humanitarian-development-peace nexus 4 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian aid, development programming, and peacebuilding
        • humanitarian-development nexus 1 -- integration of scientific research into humanitarian efforts for sustainable solutions
        • forced displacement 4 -- involuntary movement of people due to conflict, persecution, or environmental factors
        • refugee policy impact 2 -- political and social effects of policies governing refugee populations
        • displacement 2 -- forced movement of people from their homes due to conflict, climate, or economic factors
    • Miscellaneous Food Systems Concepts -- Additional food systems concepts appearing less frequently
      • Food System Analytics and Modeling -- Analytical and modeling approaches for food systems
        • true cost accounting 1 -- comprehensive assessment including environmental and social costs of food production
        • life cycle assessment 1 -- evaluation of environmental impacts throughout a product's entire life cycle
        • bio-economic modeling 1 -- analytical framework integrating biological and economic factors
        • spatial production allocation modeling 1 -- geographic modeling of crop distribution and productivity patterns
        • price transmission 1 -- how price changes propagate through market chains
        • intra-household allocation 1 -- distribution of resources among household members
        • intrahousehold decision-making 1 -- distribution of decision-making power and preferences within households
        • food subsidies 1 -- government support to reduce food costs for targeted populations
      • Specific Food System Interventions -- Specific interventions and approaches in food systems
        • floating agriculture 2 -- cultivation on floating beds in waterlogged areas
        • guerrilla gardening 3 -- informal cultivation of plants in public or underutilized spaces
        • perennial vegetable production 1 -- cultivation of long-lived vegetable crops that produce over multiple seasons
        • vegetable production 1 -- cultivation and management of vegetable crops
        • home-grown school feeding 4 -- programs linking local food production with school nutrition
        • positive deviance approach 3 -- identifying and learning from individuals or groups with better outcomes despite similar constraints
        • pioneer positive deviance 8 -- approach that identifies and supports farmers already developing innovative local solutions
        • community conversations 4 -- structured dialogue approach for community engagement and behavior change
        • community health worker model 1 -- healthcare delivery system using trained community members to provide basic health services
        • knowledge attitudes practices survey 1 -- assessment tool to evaluate understanding, perceptions, and behaviors
  • Digital Innovation and Data Science -- Covers digital technologies, data-driven tools, remote sensing, and artificial intelligence applied to agriculture
    • Digital Advisory and Extension Platforms -- Digital extension services, mobile-based advisory tools, digital agro-advisory platforms, and AI-driven extension
      • Core Digital Advisory Services -- Primary digital advisory and extension concepts
        • digital advisory services 22 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital extension 13 -- delivery of agricultural advisory services through digital platforms and mobile applications
        • digital extension platforms 5 -- technology-enabled systems for delivering agricultural advisory services to farmers
        • digital advisory platforms 5 -- technology-based systems for delivering agricultural and climate advisories
        • digital agro-advisory services 4 -- technology-enabled platforms delivering agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital extension services 2 -- technology-enabled platforms for delivering agricultural advisory and information services to farmers
        • digital agricultural extension 3 -- technology-enabled delivery of agricultural advisory services and information to farmers
        • digital agricultural advisory 2 -- technology-enabled extension services delivering agricultural recommendations
        • digital agricultural advisory services 1 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural information and support to farmers
        • digital advisories 3 -- technology-based platforms delivering agricultural guidance to farmers
        • ai-driven agricultural extension 4 -- use of artificial intelligence to deliver agricultural advice and information to farmers
      • Mobile and Interactive Advisory Tools -- Mobile-based and interactive tools for agricultural advisory
        • mobile application 1 -- software platform designed for mobile devices to deliver services or information
        • mobile-based extension 1 -- delivering agricultural advisory services through mobile phone platforms
        • interactive voice response 1 -- technology that allows interaction with computer systems through voice commands and responses
        • automatic speech recognition 3 -- technology that converts spoken language into text for digital applications
        • radio-based extension 1 -- using radio broadcasts to deliver agricultural information and climate advisories
        • multimodal advisory services 1 -- agricultural extension using multiple communication channels and formats
      • Climate and Agro-Advisory Services -- Digital climate and agroclimatic advisory services
        • digital climate advisory services 3 -- technology-enabled platforms providing climate information and agricultural guidance to farmers
        • agroclimatic information services 7 -- platforms providing weather and climate data for agricultural decision-making
        • agroclimatic forecasting 3 -- prediction of weather and climate conditions relevant to agricultural planning
        • agroclimatic intelligence 1 -- integration of climate data, ai and agricultural knowledge for decision support
        • weather-informed agro-advisories 1 -- agricultural guidance services that incorporate weather forecast data for farming decisions
        • forecast-based advisory 1 -- decision support system using weather forecasts to guide agricultural practices
        • agro-climatic bulletin 5 -- weather-based agricultural advisory system providing farmers with climate information for decision-making
        • agro-advisories 1 -- agricultural advice services based on weather and climate information
        • site-specific fertilizer advisory 3 -- precision nutrient management recommendations based on landscape and soil conditions
        • site-specific fertilizer recommendations 15 -- customized nutrient management based on local soil and climate conditions
        • site-specific recommendations 5 -- tailored agricultural advice based on local conditions and farmer circumstances
        • bundled advisory services 3 -- integrated delivery of multiple agricultural services including advisories, inputs, credit, and insurance
        • digital climate advisory services 3 -- technology-enabled platforms providing climate information and agricultural guidance to farmers
      • Digital Extension Integration and Literacy -- Integration of digital tools in extension and digital literacy
        • digital extension integration 2 -- combining digital tools with traditional extension services for farmer outreach
        • digital literacy 5 -- capacity to effectively use digital technologies for information and communication
        • digital agriculture 15 -- use of digital technologies to enhance agricultural productivity and sustainability
        • digital transformation 8 -- integration of digital technologies to reshape agricultural and food systems
        • digital agriculture assessment 1 -- frameworks for measuring and benchmarking digital technology adoption in agriculture
        • digital agriculture ecosystem 1 -- interconnected digital tools and platforms supporting agricultural decision-making and operations
        • digital inclusiveness 1 -- ensuring equitable access and participation in digital technologies and innovations
    • Remote Sensing and Earth Observation -- Satellite imagery, drone-based monitoring, hyperspectral imaging, and earth observation for agricultural applications
      • Satellite-Based Monitoring -- Satellite remote sensing and monitoring applications
        • satellite remote sensing 8 -- use of satellite imagery and radar data to monitor agricultural systems
        • satellite-based crop monitoring 4 -- use of satellite imagery and vegetation indices to monitor crop development and phenology
        • satellite-based monitoring 3 -- use of satellite imagery and remote sensing for crop and weather monitoring
        • satellite imagery analysis 2 -- use of satellite data to monitor crop conditions and agricultural landscapes
        • satellite monitoring 2 -- use of satellite imagery and data for agricultural and environmental monitoring
        • satellite-based crop insurance 1 -- risk management mechanism using remote sensing data to assess crop losses and trigger payouts
        • earth observation 6 -- use of satellite and remote sensing technologies to monitor environmental and agricultural parameters
        • earth observation integration 2 -- combining satellite data with ground-based information for agricultural monitoring
        • synthetic aperture radar 3 -- remote sensing technique using radar signals to monitor surface conditions and vegetation
        • crop type mapping 2 -- remote sensing identification of agricultural land use patterns
        • deforestation monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of forest loss over time using remote sensing and ground data
        • uav-based remote sensing 1 -- collection of agricultural data using unmanned aerial vehicles equipped with sensors
      • Drone and UAV Applications -- Unmanned aerial vehicle applications in agriculture
        • drone-based monitoring 2 -- use of unmanned aerial vehicles for data collection and measurement
        • drone-based crop monitoring 1 -- use of unmanned aerial vehicles with sensors to assess crop health and growth
        • unmanned aerial vehicles 1 -- drone technology for agricultural applications including spraying and monitoring
        • unmanned aerial vehicle surveillance 2 -- drone-based monitoring and data collection for agriculture
        • unmanned aerial vehicle application 1 -- use of drones for agricultural input delivery and field operations
      • Spectral and Imaging Technologies -- Hyperspectral, multispectral, and other imaging technologies
        • hyperspectral remote sensing 1 -- imaging technology using multiple spectral bands to detect crop conditions
        • hyperspectral imaging 1 -- advanced sensing technique analyzing light interaction across multiple wavelengths
        • multispectral image analysis 1 -- automated analysis using multiple wavelengths of light for species identification
        • remote sensing 5 -- use of satellite or aerial imagery for agricultural monitoring
        • remote sensing pest detection 1 -- use of satellite imagery to identify and map crop pest infestations
        • image analysis 1 -- automated processing and interpretation of visual data
        • canopy height modeling 1 -- estimation of crop structural parameters using remote sensing or other technologies
      • Geospatial Analysis and Mapping -- Geospatial data analysis and spatial mapping tools
        • geospatial analysis 2 -- examination of spatial data to understand geographic patterns and relationships
