        • geospatial data analysis 1 -- spatial analysis techniques to map socio-ecological variables and inform interventions
        • spatial analysis 1 -- geographic data analysis to understand spatial patterns and relationships
        • spatial analytics 1 -- analytical approaches combining geography, economics and data science for policy insights
        • spatial clustering 1 -- grouping of geographic units based on similarity of characteristics
        • spatial targeting 2 -- geographic prioritization of interventions based on location-specific factors
        • spatial prioritization 1 -- geographic targeting based on spatial analysis
        • spatial effects 1 -- influence of geographic proximity on social and economic outcomes
        • spatiotemporal climate analysis 1 -- statistical analysis of climate patterns across space and time dimensions
        • spatiotemporal data cataloging 1 -- systematic organization and metadata management of spatial and temporal datasets
        • spatiotemporal asset catalog 1 -- standardized metadata specification for spatiotemporal data assets
        • web-gis platform 1 -- web-based geographic information system for spatial data analysis
        • participatory geographic information systems 1 -- community-based mapping approach integrating local knowledge with GIS technology
        • open data cube architecture 1 -- structured system for organizing and accessing large spatial datasets
        • digital soil mapping 5 -- use of digital tools and algorithms to map soil properties spatially
        • soil organic carbon mapping 1 -- spatial assessment of carbon content in soils
        • climate suitability mapping 2 -- spatial analysis to identify optimal locations for crops under current and future climate conditions
        • flood risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas vulnerable to flooding and quantify risk levels
        • soil erosion risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas prone to soil loss and degradation
        • aquifer recharge zone mapping 1 -- identification and mapping of areas where groundwater can be naturally replenished
        • water consumption mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of water use patterns in agricultural systems
        • vulnerability hotspot mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas of high climate and social risk
        • climate hazard mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of current and projected climate risks
        • climate risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis and visualization of climate-related hazards and vulnerabilities
    • Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning -- Machine learning algorithms, deep learning, computer vision, large language models, and AI applications in agriculture
      • Core AI and ML Methods -- Fundamental AI and machine learning approaches
        • artificial intelligence 11 -- computer systems performing tasks that typically require human intelligence
        • machine learning 8 -- computational methods that learn patterns from data for prediction
        • machine learning algorithms 2 -- computational methods that learn patterns from data for prediction or classification
        • machine learning modeling 7 -- use of algorithms that learn from data to make predictions or classifications
        • machine learning forecasting 6 -- artificial intelligence techniques applied to predict agricultural outcomes and resource needs
        • machine learning workflows 1 -- automated processes for data analysis and model deployment using artificial intelligence
        • machine learning integration 1 -- combining machine learning algorithms with process-based models to improve agricultural predictions
        • machine learning classification 1 -- automated pattern recognition for categorizing biological materials
        • deep learning 2 -- machine learning approach using neural networks for pattern recognition
        • deep learning detection 3 -- artificial intelligence approach for automated pattern recognition
        • transfer learning 1 -- machine learning approach applying knowledge from one domain to another
        • random forest classification 1 -- machine learning algorithm for image classification and pattern recognition
        • generative artificial intelligence 2 -- ai systems capable of creating new content including text, images, or recommendations based on training data
        • artificial intelligence modeling 1 -- computational approach using AI algorithms for agricultural applications and data analysis
        • artificial intelligence integration 1 -- incorporation of AI technologies to enhance service delivery and decision-making
        • artificial intelligence famine forecasting 1 -- machine learning approaches to predict famine conditions and food crises
      • Computer Vision and Image Processing -- Computer vision and image-based AI applications
        • computer vision 4 -- artificial intelligence technique that enables machines to interpret and analyze visual information
        • computer vision phenotyping 1 -- automated plant trait measurement using image analysis algorithms
        • machine learning phenotyping 1 -- use of computational algorithms to automatically assess plant traits from sensor data
        • digital phenotyping 2 -- automated collection of plant trait data using digital technologies
        • vision-language models 1 -- AI systems that process both visual and textual information
      • Natural Language Processing -- Language models and NLP applications
        • large language models 4 -- AI systems for processing and generating human-like text responses

      • AI Applications and Ethics -- Applied AI in agriculture and ethical considerations
        • artificial intelligence ethics 1 -- principles and practices for responsible development and use of AI technologies
        • gender-responsive artificial intelligence 1 -- ai systems designed to recognize and mitigate gender biases in service delivery
        • bias mitigation in ai 2 -- techniques to identify and reduce unfair discrimination in artificial intelligence systems
        • bias detection and mitigation 1 -- systematic identification and correction of algorithmic biases in ai systems
      • Predictive Modeling and Forecasting -- AI-driven prediction and forecasting tools
        • yield prediction modeling 1 -- use of mathematical models to forecast agricultural crop yields
        • drought prediction 2 -- forecasting techniques to anticipate drought conditions using meteorological and satellite data
        • flood prediction 1 -- forecasting approach to anticipate inundation extent and timing for agricultural risk management
        • food crisis prediction 3 -- analytical approaches for forecasting food security emergencies
        • malnutrition forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling to anticipate acute child malnutrition for early warning systems
        • irrigation demand forecasting 6 -- prediction of crop water requirements to optimize irrigation scheduling and water allocation
        • rainfall forecasting 1 -- prediction of precipitation patterns to support agricultural decision-making
        • seasonal rainfall forecasting 1 -- prediction of precipitation patterns over seasonal timescales for agricultural planning
        • reservoir forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling of future water storage levels using multiple data sources
        • rapid response modeling 1 -- streamlined analytical approach to quickly assess impacts of global crises
        • impact-based forecasting 1 -- weather prediction approach that focuses on potential impacts rather than just meteorological conditions
    • Decision Support and Modeling Tools -- Crop modeling, decision support systems, simulation modeling, and data-driven decision-making tools
      • Crop and Agricultural Modeling -- Crop simulation and agricultural modeling tools
        • crop modeling 7 -- mathematical simulation of crop growth and yield under different environmental and management conditions
        • crop simulation modeling 3 -- computational tools for predicting crop growth, yield, and environmental impacts under different scenarios
        • crop growth modeling 3 -- computational simulation of plant development and yield prediction
        • process-based modeling 1 -- simulation approach using mechanistic understanding of system processes
        • process-based simulation 1 -- modeling approach using mechanistic understanding of biological and physical processes
        • biophysical simulation modeling 1 -- computational approach to model water and nutrient flows in agricultural systems
        • crop suitability modeling 1 -- analytical framework to assess geographic areas' suitability for growing different crops based on environmental conditions
        • hydrological modeling 3 -- mathematical simulation of water cycle processes for water resource planning and management
        • hydrological modelling 1 -- mathematical simulation of water cycle processes for water resource management
        • flood modeling 1 -- computational simulation of water flow patterns and flood behavior
        • watershed modeling 1 -- simulation of water flow and management at landscape scale
        • water balance model 2 -- computational framework quantifying water inputs, outputs, and storage changes in agricultural systems
        • hydro-economic modeling 1 -- integrated analysis combining hydrological and economic data for water resource management
        • system dynamics modeling 1 -- computational approach to understand complex systems through feedback loops, delays, and trade-offs
        • atmospheric dispersion modeling 1 -- mathematical simulation of how gases spread through the atmosphere
        • spatial production allocation modeling 1 -- geographic modeling of crop distribution and productivity patterns
        • bio-economic modeling 1 -- analytical framework integrating biological and economic factors
        • global agricultural modeling 1 -- mathematical simulation of agricultural systems at global scale to analyze market and production dynamics
        • agricultural integrated assessment models 1 -- quantitative models combining economic, crop growth, and hydrology components for food security analysis

        • evidence-based decision support 3 -- agricultural advisory systems using scientific data to guide farmer decisions
        • aquaculture decision support tool 3 -- multi-criteria analysis platform for aquaculture site selection and planning

        • computable general equilibrium modeling 5 -- economic modeling approach for policy impact analysis

        • equilibrium displacement modeling 1 -- economic analysis method examining market interactions and price effects
        • economic surplus modeling 1 -- analytical approach to quantify economic welfare impacts and benefits of interventions
        • economywide modeling 1 -- comprehensive economic analysis across all sectors of an economy
        • ex ante economic analysis 2 -- forward-looking assessment of investment returns before implementation
        • micronutrient contribution modeling 1 -- analytical approach to calculate and visualize nutrient composition of food sources
        • micronutrient adequacy projection 1 -- modeling future nutritional status and micronutrient availability

        • early warning systems 35 -- monitoring and forecasting systems to predict and prepare for emergencies
        • food crisis early warning 2 -- systems to predict and alert about impending food security emergencies
        • livestock early warning systems 1 -- integrated systems providing seasonal forecasts and decision support for pastoral livestock management
        • drought monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of drought conditions using indicators
        • agroclimatic monitoring 3 -- systematic observation and tracking of climate conditions affecting agricultural production
        • food security monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of food access, availability, and utilization indicators
        • integrated monitoring systems 2 -- comprehensive surveillance platforms combining multiple data sources

        • emission monitoring systems 3 -- tools and frameworks for measuring and reporting greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural activities
        • digital monitoring platform 3 -- technology-based system for tracking and verification of activities
        • digital monitoring systems 2 -- electronic platforms for tracking and reporting agricultural activities and outcomes
        • digital mrv platform 1 -- technology system for monitoring, reporting and verifying environmental restoration activities
        • water monitoring system 1 -- integrated platform for tracking and analyzing water resource availability and use
        • hydrological monitoring 1 -- systematic measurement and tracking of water levels and flow in water bodies
        • soil health monitoring 2 -- systematic assessment of soil biological, chemical, and physical properties for management decisions
        • soil moisture monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking of soil water content using remote sensing or field measurements
        • soil organic carbon monitoring 2 -- measurement and tracking of carbon content in agricultural soils
        • heat stress monitoring 1 -- assessment of temperature impacts on livestock behavior and productivity
        • zero deforestation monitoring 1 -- tracking and verification of forest-free agricultural production
        • environmental dna monitoring 1 -- biodiversity assessment using genetic material extracted from environmental samples
        • socio-environmental monitoring 1 -- integrated assessment of social and environmental outcomes in landscapes
        • agrobiodiversity monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking of crop diversity and genetic resources in food systems
        • systematic agrobiodiversity monitoring 2 -- standardized tracking of crop diversity conservation status over time and space
        • diet quality monitoring 2 -- systematic tracking and assessment of nutritional quality in food consumption patterns
        • food safety monitoring 2 -- systematic surveillance of contaminants and hazards in food systems
        • antibiotic resistance monitoring 3 -- surveillance and measurement of antimicrobial resistance in bacterial populations
        • migration monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking and analysis of population movement patterns
        • climate disinformation monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking and response to climate-related misinformation
    • Data Management and Digital Infrastructure -- Data standardization, interoperability, digital public infrastructure, data platforms, and knowledge management systems

        • data standardization 2 -- process of converting diverse datasets into consistent, comparable formats

        • federated data sharing 1 -- collaborative data sharing framework that maintains data sovereignty while enabling analysis
        • data integration platform 1 -- unified system combining multiple data sources

        • data digitization 2 -- process of converting analog information into digital formats for improved management

        • digital water body inventory 1 -- interactive platform mapping and managing water infrastructure using satellite data

        • data-driven agriculture 3 -- agricultural decision-making based on systematic analysis of climatic, soil, and agronomic data using computational methods
        • food system analytics 3 -- data-driven analysis of food production, distribution, and consumption systems
        • breeding data management 2 -- systematic organization and tracking of plant breeding information and materials
        • data-driven breeding 2 -- use of analytical tools and databases to guide breeding decisions
        • digital sequence information 3 -- genomic data used for genetic resource characterization and analysis
        • data gap analysis 3 -- systematic assessment to identify missing or incomplete information in databases
        • evidence gap mapping 4 -- systematic methodology to identify and visualize gaps in research evidence
    • Precision Agriculture and Smart Farming -- Precision farming, site-specific recommendations, IoT sensors, digital twins, and data-driven farm management
      • Precision Farming Technologies -- Core precision agriculture and smart farming tools
        • precision agriculture 13 -- data-driven farming approach using technology for site-specific crop management

        • digital twin 8 -- real-time digital replica of physical systems for management
        • digital twin technology 3 -- digital replica of physical systems used for modeling and decision support

        • soil spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid, non-destructive assessment of soil properties
        • digital soil sensing 1 -- portable field-based technology for rapid soil property assessment
        • near-infrared spectroscopy 1 -- analytical technique for rapid quality assessment using light absorption
        • acoustic sensing 1 -- detection and analysis of sound patterns for monitoring and identification purposes
        • precision sowing 2 -- accurate placement of seeds using mechanized equipment for optimal crop establishment
        • precision seeding 1 -- accurate placement of seeds at optimal spacing and depth using specialized equipment
        • precision nitrogen management 1 -- optimized fertilizer application matching nitrogen rates to specific field conditions
        • field-specific nutrient management 3 -- customized fertilizer recommendations based on individual field characteristics and conditions

        • digital farming tools 3 -- technology platforms that support agricultural decision-making and farmer participation in sustainable practices
        • smart valleys approach 5 -- integrated water control system for inland valley rice production

        • high-throughput phenotyping 4 -- automated measurement of plant traits using advanced technologies
        • plant phenotyping 1 -- measurement and analysis of plant physical and biochemical traits
        • sparse phenotyping 1 -- strategy to reduce phenotyping costs while maintaining selection accuracy
        • image analysis 1 -- automated processing and interpretation of visual data
        • crop monitoring 2 -- systematic observation and assessment of crop growth and conditions
      • Sensor and IoT Applications -- Sensor networks and IoT applications in agriculture
        • soil health assessment 7 -- evaluation of soil physical, chemical, and biological properties for agricultural sustainability
        • soil health diagnostics 1 -- systematic assessment of soil physical, chemical and biological properties
        • soil diagnostics 1 -- field-based assessment of soil physical, chemical, and biological characteristics
        • water quality monitoring 1 -- systematic assessment of water parameters to ensure suitability for aquaculture and other uses
        • integrated water storage assessment 1 -- comprehensive evaluation of multiple water storage types and their temporal dynamics for resource management
        • heat stress analysis 4 -- quantitative assessment of temperature impacts on crops, livestock, and human populations
      • Crowdsourcing and Citizen Science -- Participatory and crowdsourced data collection
        • citizen science 12 -- scientific research conducted by members of the general public in collaboration with professional scientists
        • crowdsourcing 1 -- engaging large groups to collect data or perform tasks using technology
        • community-based climate observation 3 -- local monitoring of climate variables by community members to support decision-making and early warning
        • tricot approach 3 -- triadic comparison method for on-farm testing where farmers rank three technologies
        • triadic comparisons of technology options 15 -- research approach to decentralize technology evaluation using large numbers of participants for higher external validity
    • E-Learning and Virtual Training -- Digital learning platforms and virtual training tools
      • e-learning 1 -- digital educational delivery method using online platforms and resources

      • game-based learning 1 -- structured experiential learning using games to develop understanding and problem-solving skills
      • mobile phone-delivered training 1 -- educational content delivered through automated phone calls or mobile platforms

  • Markets, Value Chains, and Rural Livelihoods -- Covers market systems, agricultural value chains, business development, and rural economic development
    • Value Chain Development and Analysis -- Value chain mapping, upgrading, coordination, governance, and market systems development
      • Core Value Chain Concepts -- Foundational value chain development and analysis concepts
        • value chain development 38 -- systematic improvement of market linkages and business relationships
        • value chain analysis 20 -- examination of production to consumption linkages and market dynamics
        • value chain upgrading 5 -- improving quality, coordination, and market linkages across agricultural supply chains
        • value chain mapping 2 -- systematic analysis of actors, relationships, and flows in agricultural commodity chains
        • value chain governance 1 -- coordination mechanisms and power relationships among actors in agricultural value chains
        • value chain coordination 1 -- systematic organization of production, processing, and distribution activities
        • livestock value chains 5 -- sequence of activities from livestock production to final consumption
        • nutrition-sensitive value chain 2 -- approach to analyze and improve nutritional outcomes along food value chains
      • Supply Chain and Post-Harvest -- Supply chain management and post-harvest value addition
        • supply chain mapping 1 -- systematic identification and analysis of food system bottlenecks and flows
        • supply chain analysis 1 -- examination of production and trade networks and their impacts
        • post-harvest losses 5 -- reduction in crop quantity or quality after harvest
        • post-harvest processing 3 -- handling and treatment of crops after harvest
        • post-harvest technology 2 -- methods and equipment for processing, preserving, and storing agricultural products
        • post-harvest loss reduction 1 -- minimizing food waste and quality degradation after crop harvest through improved handling
        • postharvest loss reduction 2 -- strategies to minimize food waste after harvest
        • post-harvest management 1 -- practices and technologies for handling, processing and storing crops after harvest
        • traceability 2 -- ability to track products through supply chain stages
        • traceability systems 1 -- systems for tracking products through supply chains
        • food safety 6 -- assurance that food will not cause harm when prepared or consumed
        • food safety management 2 -- systematic approach to ensuring safe food production and handling
        • food safety monitoring 2 -- systematic surveillance of contaminants and hazards in food systems
        • food safety training 1 -- education programs to improve food handling and safety practices
        • quality assurance 2 -- systematic processes to ensure products or services meet specified standards
        • quality control 2 -- systematic processes to ensure product standards
        • cold chain management 1 -- temperature-controlled supply chain for preserving vaccine potency
        • cold chain transportation 1 -- temperature-controlled logistics system for preserving product quality during transport
      • Agricultural Commercialization -- Commercialization clusters and contract farming
        • agricultural commercialization clusters 3 -- geographic zones organized for coordinated agricultural development and market access
        • contract farming 2 -- agricultural production under forward contracts between farmers and buyers
        • market linkages 1 -- connections between producers and buyers to facilitate trade
        • market linkage facilitation 1 -- connecting producers with buyers and markets to improve commercialization
        • last-mile delivery 2 -- reaching end users through local agent networks and service providers
        • last-mile distribution 1 -- final stage of product delivery from businesses to end-users in rural areas
    • Market Systems and Market Intelligence -- Market assessment, price monitoring, market information systems, and market segmentation
      • Market Analysis and Assessment -- Tools and methods for understanding market systems
        • market intelligence 11 -- systematic collection and analysis of market data to inform strategic decisions
        • market assessment 3 -- systematic evaluation of supply, demand, and market dynamics for specific products or sectors
        • market systems analysis 4 -- systematic examination of market structures, actors, and dynamics
        • market systems development 2 -- intervention strategy that addresses systemic barriers in market functioning
        • market segmentation 11 -- division of markets based on consumer preferences and end-use requirements
        • seed product market segmentation 2 -- systematic categorization of seed markets based on standardized criteria
        • consumer preference mapping 2 -- systematic assessment of consumer preferences for product traits and characteristics
        • willingness to pay 3 -- economic measure of maximum amount consumers will pay for a good or service
        • willingness to pay assessment 1 -- economic evaluation of farmer demand for agricultural technologies
        • discrete choice experiments 3 -- statistical method to elicit preferences by presenting respondents with choice scenarios
        • contingent valuation 2 -- survey-based method to estimate economic value of non-market goods
        • demand signaling 1 -- systematic approach to understanding stakeholder needs and priorities for scaling innovations
        • demand signalling 1 -- process of identifying and articulating stakeholder needs and priorities for innovation adoption
      • Price and Market Monitoring -- Price monitoring, market information, and market intelligence systems
        • market monitoring 9 -- systematic tracking and analysis of commodity prices and trade flows
        • market price monitoring 5 -- systematic tracking of commodity prices and market conditions
        • market information systems 1 -- platforms for collecting and disseminating agricultural price and market data
        • price transmission 1 -- how price changes propagate through market chains
        • pricing strategy 1 -- method of setting product prices to influence consumer behavior and adoption
      • Market-Oriented Seed Systems -- Market-driven approaches to seed delivery and production
        • market-responsive breeding 6 -- crop breeding aligned with specific market demands and consumer preferences
        • market-driven seed production 1 -- seed multiplication based on trader and consumer demand
        • market-driven seed delivery 1 -- seed distribution system responsive to farmer and trader market demands rather than supply-driven
        • direct seed marketing 1 -- policy approach allowing seed producers to sell directly to farmers bypassing state distribution
        • seed delivery network 1 -- coordinated system of organizations that multiply and distribute improved crop varieties to farmers
        • trader-centered seed systems 1 -- seed distribution networks that place traders at the center of connecting producers with farmers
    • Agricultural Mechanization and Services -- Mechanized agriculture, small-scale mechanization, mechanization service providers, and precision seeding
      • Mechanization Technologies -- Technologies and approaches for agricultural mechanization
        • mechanized agriculture 5 -- use of machinery to perform agricultural operations
        • agricultural mechanization 4 -- introduction of machinery to replace manual labor in farming operations
        • small-scale mechanization 3 -- use of small machinery like power tillers and mowers in agricultural production
        • mechanized direct seeding 4 -- agricultural practice using machinery to plant seeds directly into soil
        • mechanized direct-seeded rice 4 -- climate-smart rice cultivation technology using machines for precision sowing instead of transplanting
        • mechanical transplanting 2 -- use of machines to plant seedlings instead of manual labor
        • precision seeding 1 -- accurate placement of seeds at optimal spacing and depth using specialized equipment
        • precision sowing 2 -- accurate placement of seeds using mechanized equipment for optimal crop establishment
        • scale-appropriate mechanization 3 -- agricultural machinery and tools sized and designed for smallholder farming systems
        • sustainable agricultural mechanization 2 -- use of machinery and equipment that reduces environmental impact while improving productivity
      • Mechanization Service Models -- Service delivery models for agricultural mechanization
        • mechanization service provider model 2 -- business model where farmers provide mechanization services to other farmers for a fee
        • mechanization services 2 -- provision of agricultural machinery and equipment services to farmers
        • mechanized agriculture services 2 -- provision of machinery and equipment for agricultural operations
        • mechanization hire services 1 -- business model providing agricultural machinery access to farmers through rental arrangements
        • irrigation-as-a-service 2 -- business model where irrigation services are provided by third parties rather than owned by farmers
        • water-as-a-service 1 -- business model providing irrigation services rather than selling equipment
        • shared services 2 -- centralized provision of common resources and capabilities across organizations
    • Business Development and Private Sector Engagement -- Business model development, agribusiness training, private sector investment, and inclusive business models
      • Business Models and Development -- Development of business models and plans for agribusiness
        • business model development 8 -- creation of sustainable commercial frameworks for technology adoption
        • inclusive business models 3 -- commercial strategies ensuring access to services for smallholder farmers and marginalized groups
        • sustainable business model 1 -- commercial framework balancing economic viability with environmental and social benefits
        • agroecological business modeling 1 -- development of sustainable business models based on ecological principles
        • business plan development 1 -- structured process of creating comprehensive business strategies and financial projections
        • business incubation 4 -- support system for developing and accelerating new enterprises
        • business development services 2 -- support services to strengthen enterprise capacity and growth
        • business-to-business forum 2 -- platform for learning, networking, and joint problem-solving to catalyze business growth and competitiveness
        • business-to-business discussion tools 1 -- structured dialogue mechanisms to strengthen coordination and collaboration among value chain actors
        • business investment decision tools 1 -- digital platforms supporting enterprise planning and investment analysis
        • accelerator programs 1 -- structured support programs for scaling agricultural innovations and businesses
        • hub model 3 -- territorial multistakeholder platforms integrating research, innovation and extension
        • innovation hubs 2 -- territorial platforms that connect actors, capacities, and knowledge for technology validation and scaling
      • Agribusiness Training and Capacity -- Training and capacity building for agribusiness
        • agribusiness training 3 -- education combining agricultural production with business management skills
        • agribusiness acceleration 3 -- structured support to help agricultural enterprises scale and access markets
        • financial literacy training 5 -- educational programs teaching budgeting, savings, credit management, and investment decision-making skills
        • financial management training 1 -- educational programs to develop business financial skills and practices
        • women's business skills training 1 -- educational programs designed to develop entrepreneurial and business management capabilities among women
        • investment readiness 2 -- preparation of enterprises for attracting investment funding
        • investment planning 3 -- systematic process for identifying and prioritizing climate-resilient investments
        • investment action plan 2 -- strategic framework outlining funding requirements and implementation pathways
        • bankable investment projects 1 -- development projects structured to meet financial institution lending criteria
        • bankable project development 1 -- preparation of investment-ready proposals that meet funding institution requirements
      • Private Sector and Partnerships -- Private sector investment and public-private partnerships
        • private sector investment 4 -- mobilization of commercial capital for development and sustainability outcomes
        • private sector engagement 2 -- involving commercial entities in development initiatives
        • public-private partnerships 20 -- collaborative arrangements between government and private sector entities
        • micro small medium enterprises 4 -- small-scale business entities that form the backbone of many developing economies
        • cooperative development 3 -- building organizational capacity and governance structures for farmer collective action
        • innovation networks 7 -- collaborative platforms connecting multiple actors to facilitate knowledge exchange and scaling
        • innovation platforms 5 -- multi-stakeholder forums for collaborative problem-solving and knowledge sharing
        • territorial innovation networks 2 -- collaborative networks linking research, extension and farmers across geographic territories
      • Geographical Indications and Certification -- Product differentiation through certification and geographical indications
        • geographical indications 1 -- certification system linking product quality and characteristics to specific geographic origins
        • organic certification 4 -- formal verification process ensuring agricultural products meet organic production standards
        • quality management systems 1 -- structured framework for ensuring consistent product quality and safety standards
    • Finance, Insurance, and Risk Management -- Index insurance, blended finance, digital financial services, climate finance, and risk contingent credit
      • Agricultural Insurance -- Insurance products for agricultural risk management
        • index insurance 3 -- insurance products that use objective indices to trigger payouts rather than individual loss assessments
        • bundled livestock insurance 1 -- integrated insurance products combining multiple risk protections for pastoral communities
        • area-based yield insurance 1 -- insurance coverage based on regional average yields rather than individual farm losses
        • picture-based insurance 1 -- digital crop insurance using smartphone images to assess and indemnify crop damage
        • weather index-based insurance 1 -- agricultural insurance that pays based on weather parameters rather than actual crop losses
        • satellite-based crop insurance 1 -- risk management mechanism using remote sensing data to assess crop losses and trigger payouts
        • climate-smart credit scoring 2 -- financial assessment tool incorporating climate resilience factors for agricultural lending
        • climate-linked credit scoring 1 -- financial risk assessment integrating climate data with borrower profiles
      • Finance and Investment Mechanisms -- Blended finance, digital financial services, and innovative financing
        • blended finance 8 -- combination of public and private funding to reduce investment risks
        • blended financing 1 -- financial strategy combining public and private funding sources to mobilize resources for development
        • digital financial services 2 -- technology-enabled credit, insurance, and banking solutions for agricultural communities
        • digital transfers 2 -- electronic delivery of cash assistance through mobile money and digital platforms
        • digital cash transfers 1 -- electronic delivery of monetary assistance to beneficiaries
        • financial innovation 1 -- development of new financial products and services for agricultural value chains
        • risk contingent credit 3 -- financial mechanism that links agricultural credit with insurance coverage to manage production risks
        • livestock-based lending 1 -- financial model using livestock as collateral to provide credit access to smallholder farmers
        • savings and lending groups 1 -- community-based financial institutions that provide members with savings and credit services
        • pay-as-you-go system 1 -- payment model allowing gradual ownership transfer through installment payments
        • bundled financial services 1 -- integrated packages combining credit, insurance, and agricultural services
        • bundled advisory services 3 -- integrated delivery of multiple agricultural services including advisories, inputs, credit, and insurance
      • Climate Finance -- Climate finance mechanisms, tracking, and mobilization
        • climate finance 11 -- financial mechanisms including carbon credits and international funds to support climate solutions
        • climate finance mechanisms 4 -- financial instruments and structures designed to fund climate mitigation and adaptation projects
        • climate finance tracking 6 -- systems for monitoring flow and use of climate-related funding
        • climate finance mobilization 1 -- strategies to attract and deploy financial resources for climate adaptation and mitigation
        • climate finance taxonomy 1 -- classification framework for directing climate investments toward specific sectors and activities
        • adaptation finance 3 -- financial resources dedicated to climate change adaptation activities
        • carbon trading 3 -- market-based mechanism for trading carbon credits to offset greenhouse gas emissions
        • carbon credit certification 2 -- formal verification process that enables farmers to monetize emission reductions through carbon markets
        • carbon market readiness 1 -- capacity building to enable participation in carbon trading mechanisms
        • payment for ecosystem services 2 -- economic mechanism compensating land managers for environmental benefits
        • payments for ecosystem services 1 -- economic incentives for conservation and environmental stewardship
        • payment for environmental services 2 -- economic mechanism that compensates land users for providing ecosystem services
        • payments for environmental services 1 -- economic incentive schemes that reward farmers for environmental stewardship
        • eco-compensation 2 -- payment mechanism to compensate for environmental services and conservation efforts
        • biodiversity-positive financing 1 -- investment mechanisms that channel finance toward biodiversity conservation and sustainable livelihoods
      • Social Protection and Transfers -- Social protection programs and cash transfer mechanisms
        • social protection 12 -- policies and programs designed to reduce poverty and vulnerability
        • cash transfers 2 -- direct monetary payments to individuals or households as social assistance
        • cash transfer programs 2 -- direct monetary assistance provided to households to improve welfare outcomes
        • tapered cash transfers 1 -- gradually diminishing cash payments designed to support transition to self-reliance
        • shock-responsive social protection 1 -- social safety net programs that can rapidly scale up during emergencies
        • adaptive social protection 1 -- social safety nets that adjust to climate and economic shocks
        • social protection systems 1 -- government programs providing support to vulnerable populations during crises
        • social protection policy 1 -- government frameworks for providing assistance to vulnerable populations
        • social transfer programs 1 -- government programs providing financial or in-kind support to households
        • lean season assistance 1 -- humanitarian support during periods when food stocks are depleted and prices peak
        • conditional subsidies 1 -- financial incentives tied to specific agricultural practices or outcomes
        • food subsidies 1 -- government support to reduce food costs for targeted populations
        • subsidy repurposing 1 -- redirecting government subsidy spending toward more effective development investments
        • fertilizer subsidy reform 1 -- policy changes to agricultural input support programs
        • agricultural support repurposing 1 -- reforming agricultural policies and financial instruments for sustainable food system transformation
    • Livelihoods and Economic Inclusion -- Livelihood improvement, economic inclusion, youth entrepreneurship, and ultra-poor graduation approaches
      • Livelihood Approaches -- Frameworks and approaches for livelihood improvement
        • livelihood improvement 3 -- enhancement of income, employment, and economic opportunities for communities
        • livelihood capitals 1 -- assets and resources that individuals and households use to build their livelihoods
        • economic inclusion 1 -- expanding access to economic opportunities for marginalized populations
        • ultra-poor graduation model 3 -- integrated intervention combining cash transfers, assets, training, and coaching for extreme poverty
        • saving for change 1 -- community-based savings and lending mechanism for rural financial inclusion
        • dignified work 1 -- employment that provides income, respect, safety and social recognition
        • rural revitalization 1 -- strategies to improve economic and social conditions in rural areas
        • livelihood cooperation 1 -- collaborative strategies between communities to enhance economic resilience
      • Youth and Women's Economic Empowerment -- Youth entrepreneurship and women's economic empowerment
        • youth entrepreneurship 2 -- support for business development and job creation among young people
        • youth empowerment 2 -- strategies to engage and support young people in agricultural development
        • women's empowerment 15 -- process of increasing women's access to resources, decision-making power, and opportunities
        • women's land ownership 1 -- legal and social recognition of women's rights to own and control land resources
        • women's leadership development 3 -- capacity building initiatives focused on enhancing women's leadership skills and confidence
        • women's political participation 4 -- involvement of women in political processes and decision-making
        • women's empowerment in agriculture index 2 -- standardized tool for measuring women's empowerment in agricultural contexts
        • gender empowerment 4 -- process of increasing women's capacity to make strategic life choices
        • empowerment 3 -- process of increasing capacity to make choices and transform those choices into actions
        • gender equality 3 -- equal rights, opportunities and treatment regardless of gender
        • gender equity 3 -- fair treatment and opportunities for all genders in agricultural systems
        • gender inclusion 5 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits for all genders in development processes
        • social inclusion 2 -- ensuring marginalized groups have access to opportunities and services
        • gender and social inclusion 7 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits across social groups
        • gender equality social inclusion 3 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits for all groups
        • gender equality and social inclusion 2 -- strategy to ensure equitable participation of marginalized groups
        • intersectionality 2 -- analysis considering multiple overlapping social identities and systems of oppression
      • Labor and Migration -- Labor migration and related livelihood dynamics
        • labor migration 5 -- movement of people for employment opportunities, often from rural to urban areas or abroad
        • migration policy 1 -- government strategies and frameworks for managing human mobility
        • time poverty 1 -- insufficient time to meet basic needs due to excessive work burdens
        • gendered labor response 2 -- differential impacts of external shocks on men's and women's work patterns
      • Economic Analysis and Impact -- Economic analysis tools for livelihood and market assessment
        • cost-benefit analysis 6 -- economic evaluation method using financial indicators like NPV and IRR
        • cost-effectiveness analysis 2 -- economic evaluation comparing costs and outcomes of different interventions
        • economic impact assessment 2 -- quantitative evaluation of economic benefits and costs of interventions
        • ex ante economic analysis 2 -- forward-looking assessment of investment returns before implementation
        • computable general equilibrium modeling 5 -- economic modeling approach for policy impact analysis
        • social accounting matrix 2 -- comprehensive economic accounting framework linking production, income and expenditure flows
        • economic surplus modeling 1 -- analytical approach to quantify economic welfare impacts and benefits of interventions
        • equilibrium displacement modeling 1 -- economic analysis method examining market interactions and price effects
        • technical efficiency analysis 2 -- measurement of how well inputs are converted to outputs relative to best practice
        • stochastic frontier analysis 1 -- econometric method to estimate production efficiency and separate inefficiency from random variation
        • true cost accounting 1 -- comprehensive assessment including environmental and social costs of food production
        • cost of healthy diet analysis 1 -- economic assessment of affordability and access to nutritious food
        • economic return analysis 1 -- calculation of financial benefits and costs of agricultural interventions or investments
    • Smallholder Farming and Technology Adoption -- Smallholder farming systems, technology adoption, and scaling approaches
      • Smallholder Farming Systems -- Approaches and systems for smallholder farmers
        • smallholder farming 12 -- small-scale agricultural production by resource-limited farmers
        • farmer segmentation 1 -- classification of farmers into distinct groups based on characteristics and preferences
        • farmer group model 1 -- organizational structure for collective farmer action and resource sharing
        • model farms 2 -- demonstration sites showcasing integrated agricultural technologies and practices
        • innovation bundling 5 -- integrating technical, digital and institutional solutions to address interconnected challenges
        • bundled innovations 2 -- integrated packages combining multiple complementary technologies and practices
        • bundled csa practices 1 -- integrated packages of climate-smart agricultural interventions implemented at scale
        • integrated production bundle 4 -- coherent package of agronomic practices, inputs, and management recommendations for specific crops
        • whole-farm innovation bundles 3 -- integrated packages of complementary technologies and practices for entire farming systems
        • socio-technical innovation bundles 4 -- integrated packages combining technological innovations with social interventions
        • innovation package 2 -- bundled set of technologies, practices and services designed to achieve specific outcomes
        • technology bundling 1 -- integrated package of complementary technologies and services for scaling
      • Technology Adoption and Diffusion -- Dynamics of technology adoption and diffusion in agricultural contexts
        • technology adoption 9 -- process by which farmers accept and implement new agricultural technologies or practices
        • technology diffusion 3 -- spread and adoption of new technologies across markets and users
        • technology dissemination 2 -- process of spreading improved agricultural technologies to farmers
        • technology transfer 6 -- process of moving innovations from research to practical application
        • technology adoption dynamics 2 -- patterns of farmer uptake and abandonment of agricultural innovations
        • adoption dynamics 1 -- patterns of technology uptake including initial use, consistent adoption, dis-adoption, and non-adoption
        • varietal adoption 8 -- process by which farmers select and use new crop varieties
        • improved seed adoption 2 -- process by which farmers take up and use enhanced crop varieties
        • fertilizer adoption 1 -- farmer uptake and use of mineral fertilizers
        • forage adoption 1 -- uptake of improved grass varieties by livestock farmers
      • Scaling Approaches -- Frameworks and strategies for scaling agricultural innovations
        • scaling for impact 18 -- systematic approach to expanding successful innovations to achieve broader development outcomes
        • scaling pathways 12 -- systematic approaches to expand adoption of innovations
        • scaling framework 7 -- systematic approach for expanding successful interventions to broader contexts
        • scaling strategies 5 -- systematic methods for expanding successful interventions to broader populations
        • scaling readiness 5 -- systematic preparation of innovations for widespread adoption and implementation
        • scaling readiness assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of factors affecting the scaling of innovations
        • scaling assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of factors affecting innovation adoption and diffusion
        • innovation scaling 6 -- process of expanding successful agricultural innovations to broader adoption
        • inclusive scaling 4 -- strategy for expanding improved varieties that ensures broad access across different farm types and socioeconomic levels
        • adaptive scaling 2 -- approach to scaling innovations that adapts to local contexts and market conditions
        • responsible scaling 2 -- expansion of innovations while anticipating and mitigating social and environmental risks
        • producer-led scaling 4 -- farmer-driven expansion of successful practices through peer-to-peer learning
        • participatory scaling 1 -- collaborative approach to expanding successful interventions through stakeholder engagement
        • gender-responsive scaling 1 -- scaling strategies that integrate gender considerations and address gendered barriers
        • selective scaling 1 -- targeted expansion strategy focusing on specific contexts or beneficiary groups for improved varieties
        • scaling champions 2 -- trusted individuals who coordinate actors and support innovation adoption
        • scaling hubs 1 -- regional platforms that coordinate partnerships and knowledge sharing for agricultural innovation adoption
        • scaling fund 1 -- financial mechanism supporting innovation expansion and market penetration
        • scaling scan tool 1 -- assessment framework to evaluate innovation scalability potential
        • science of scaling 1 -- evidence-based understanding of how innovations achieve widespread adoption and impact
        • scaling 4 -- process of expanding successful innovations or interventions to reach broader populations or geographic areas
        • impact accelerator 1 -- coordinated system design to enhance adoption of agricultural innovations
    • Digital Agriculture and Advisory Services -- Digital tools, platforms, and advisory services for agricultural markets and value chains
      • Digital Advisory and Extension Platforms -- Digital platforms for agricultural advisory and extension services
        • digital advisory services 22 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital advisory platforms 5 -- technology-based systems for delivering agricultural and climate advisories
        • digital extension 13 -- delivery of agricultural advisory services through digital platforms and mobile applications
        • digital extension platforms 5 -- technology-enabled systems for delivering agricultural advisory services to farmers
        • digital extension services 2 -- technology-enabled platforms for delivering agricultural advisory and information services to farmers
        • digital extension integration 2 -- combining digital tools with traditional extension services for farmer outreach
        • digital agricultural extension 3 -- technology-enabled delivery of agricultural advisory services and information to farmers
        • digital agro-advisory services 4 -- technology-enabled platforms delivering agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital agricultural advisory 2 -- technology-enabled extension services delivering agricultural recommendations
        • digital agricultural advisory services 1 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural information and support to farmers
        • digital advisories 3 -- technology-based platforms delivering agricultural guidance to farmers
        • digital platforms 6 -- technology-based systems for information sharing and service delivery
        • digital farming tools 3 -- technology platforms that support agricultural decision-making and farmer participation in sustainable practices
        • mobile application 1 -- software platform designed for mobile devices to deliver services or information
        • interactive voice response 1 -- technology that allows interaction with computer systems through voice commands and responses
        • radio-based extension 1 -- using radio broadcasts to deliver agricultural information and climate advisories
        • mobile-based extension 1 -- delivering agricultural advisory services through mobile phone platforms
        • mobile phone-delivered training 1 -- educational content delivered through automated phone calls or mobile platforms
        • last-mile distribution 1 -- final stage of product delivery from businesses to end-users in rural areas
      • Digital Agriculture Systems -- Broader digital agriculture systems and transformation
        • digital agriculture 15 -- use of digital technologies to enhance agricultural productivity and sustainability
        • digital transformation 8 -- integration of digital technologies to reshape agricultural and food systems
        • digital literacy 5 -- capacity to effectively use digital technologies for information and communication
        • digital inclusiveness 1 -- ensuring equitable access and participation in digital technologies and innovations
        • digital public infrastructure 1 -- shared digital platforms and protocols that enable scalable service delivery
        • digital agriculture ecosystem 1 -- interconnected digital tools and platforms supporting agricultural decision-making and operations
        • digital agriculture assessment 1 -- frameworks for measuring and benchmarking digital technology adoption in agriculture
        • agricultural data hubs 2 -- centralized platforms for organizing and sharing agricultural information
        • knowledge platform 2 -- digital system for organizing, sharing and accessing information resources
        • digital methods 1 -- computational approaches for analyzing online data and digital content
    • Institutional and Governance Frameworks -- Multi-stakeholder platforms, institutional coordination, and governance for market and value chain development
      • Multi-Stakeholder Engagement -- Platforms and approaches for multi-stakeholder collaboration
        • multi-stakeholder platforms 33 -- collaborative spaces bringing together diverse actors for innovation and change
        • multistakeholder platforms 5 -- collaborative mechanisms bringing together diverse actors to address common challenges
        • multi-stakeholder engagement 9 -- collaborative processes involving diverse actors including farmers, government, researchers, and private sector
        • multi-stakeholder collaboration 5 -- coordinated engagement among diverse organizations and sectors toward common goals
        • multi-stakeholder partnership 4 -- collaborative arrangements involving diverse actors from different sectors
        • multistakeholder partnerships 2 -- collaborative arrangements involving diverse actors across agricultural value chains
        • multistakeholder networks 2 -- collaborative structures involving diverse actors to coordinate knowledge sharing and scaling
        • stakeholder engagement 16 -- systematic involvement of relevant parties in decision-making processes
        • stakeholder consultation 4 -- structured engagement with relevant parties to gather input and build consensus
        • stakeholder dialogue 3 -- structured multi-party discussions to address complex resource management challenges
        • stakeholder mapping 1 -- systematic identification and categorization of relevant actors and their relationships
        • civil society engagement 2 -- involvement of non-governmental organizations and community groups in policy and development processes
        • institutional coordination 4 -- alignment and collaboration among organizations and agencies
        • inter-institutional coordination 3 -- collaborative arrangements between different organizations for common goals
        • district working groups 2 -- collaborative governance structures for coordinated natural resource management
      • Policy and Enabling Environment -- Policy frameworks and enabling environments for market development
        • enabling environment 3 -- policy and regulatory conditions that support business development
        • policy dialogue 2 -- structured discussions among stakeholders to inform policy development and implementation
        • policy advocacy 2 -- strategic efforts to influence policy decisions using evidence
        • policy analysis 2 -- systematic evaluation of policy options, impacts, and implementation strategies
        • policy assessment 4 -- evaluation of regulatory frameworks and their effectiveness
        • policy coherence 8 -- alignment and coordination of policies across different levels and sectors of governance
        • policy coherence analysis 1 -- systematic assessment of alignment and consistency across multiple policies
        • policy alignment 2 -- coordination of policies and institutional frameworks for coherent implementation
        • policy harmonization 1 -- aligning policies across jurisdictions for consistency
        • policy uptake 3 -- adoption and implementation of research findings in policy frameworks
        • evidence-based policy 6 -- policy development informed by systematic research evidence
        • policy integration 7 -- incorporating innovations into official government frameworks
        • fertilizer policy 2 -- government regulations and subsidies governing fertilizer availability, pricing, and use
        • land tenure security 1 -- legal and institutional frameworks that provide secure rights to land ownership or use
        • fisheries subsidies 1 -- government financial support to fishing industry operations
        • agricultural distortions 1 -- policy-induced inefficiencies that affect agricultural productivity and resource allocation
        • regulatory oversight 1 -- systematic monitoring and enforcement of standards and regulations
        • phytosanitary regulation 1 -- policies and standards for preventing plant disease spread through seed systems
        • digital marketing regulation 1 -- policies controlling online promotion of products to specific populations
      • Collective Action and Governance -- Collective action and governance mechanisms for rural development
        • collective action 2 -- coordinated community efforts to address shared challenges
        • participatory governance 10 -- inclusive decision-making processes involving multiple stakeholders
        • community-based governance 2 -- local management systems where communities control and manage shared resources
        • village-level governance 3 -- local institutional arrangements for decision-making and resource management
        • polycentric governance 1 -- multi-level decision-making involving multiple centers of authority
        • community-led governance 1 -- local management systems where communities control decision-making processes
        • rangeland governance 2 -- institutional arrangements for managing communal grazing lands and resources
        • inclusive water governance 2 -- water management frameworks that ensure equitable participation and benefit-sharing
        • community-based management 6 -- natural resource management approach led by local communities
        • community-based resource management 2 -- participatory strategy where local communities manage their natural resources
        • community-led initiatives 2 -- locally driven programs managed by community members
        • community engagement 2 -- participatory processes involving local stakeholders in development initiatives
  • Gender, Social Inclusion, and Human Wellbeing -- Covers gender equity, social inclusion, empowerment, and human wellbeing dimensions of agricultural development
    • Gender Equality and Women's Empowerment -- Gender-transformative approaches, women's empowerment, gender mainstreaming, and gender-responsive programming
      • Gender-Transformative Approaches -- Approaches that address root causes of gender inequality
        • gender transformative approaches 29 -- interventions that address root causes of gender inequality and transform power relations
        • gender transformative research 7 -- research approaches that challenge and transform underlying causes of gender inequalities
        • gender-transformative intervention 3 -- development strategies that address structural gender inequalities and power dynamics
      • Women's Empowerment -- Concepts related to empowering women in agriculture and society
        • women's empowerment 15 -- process of increasing women's access to resources, decision-making power, and opportunities
        • women's empowerment in agriculture index 2 -- standardized tool for measuring women's empowerment in agricultural contexts
        • women's leadership development 3 -- capacity building initiatives focused on enhancing women's leadership skills and confidence
        • women's political participation 4 -- involvement of women in political processes and decision-making
        • women's business skills training 1 -- educational programs designed to develop entrepreneurial and business management capabilities among women
        • women's land ownership 1 -- legal and social recognition of women's rights to own and control land resources
        • empowerment 3 -- process of increasing capacity to make choices and transform those choices into actions
      • Gender Mainstreaming and Programming -- Integrating gender into programs, policies, and research
        • gender mainstreaming 8 -- systematic integration of gender perspectives into policies and programs
        • gender-responsive approaches 7 -- interventions that acknowledge and address different needs and constraints of men and women
        • gender-responsive programming 6 -- development interventions addressing gender inequalities and promoting inclusion
        • gender and social inclusion 7 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits across social groups
        • gender integration 4 -- systematic incorporation of gender considerations into policies, programs, and research
        • gender inclusion 5 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits for all genders in development processes
        • gender empowerment 4 -- process of increasing women's capacity to make strategic life choices
        • gender equity 3 -- fair treatment and opportunities for all genders in agricultural systems
        • gender equality 3 -- equal rights, opportunities and treatment regardless of gender
        • gender equality social inclusion 3 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits for all groups
        • gender equality and social inclusion 2 -- strategy to ensure equitable participation of marginalized groups
        • gender-inclusive approaches 4 -- methods that ensure equitable participation and benefits for all genders
        • gender-responsive agriculture 5 -- farming interventions designed to address differential impacts on men and women
        • gender-responsive interventions 2 -- programs designed to address differential impacts across gender lines
        • gender-responsive research 2 -- research design that considers differential impacts and needs of men and women
        • gender-responsive policy 2 -- policy framework that integrates gender equality considerations into planning and implementation
        • gender-responsive services 2 -- services designed to address differential needs and barriers faced by different genders
        • gender-responsive solutions 3 -- interventions designed to address differential impacts and needs by gender
        • gender-responsive extension services 2 -- agricultural advisory services designed to address specific needs and constraints of different genders
        • gender-responsive agricultural advisories 3 -- agricultural guidance systems designed to address different needs and constraints of men and women farmers
        • gender-responsive breeding 3 -- breeding programs that consider differentiated needs of men and women farmers
        • gender-responsive livestock breeding 1 -- breeding programs that consider gender dynamics in livestock management and benefits
        • gender-responsive budgeting 2 -- budget analysis and allocation considering differential gender impacts
        • gender-responsive scaling 1 -- scaling strategies that integrate gender considerations and address gendered barriers
        • gender-responsive delivery 1 -- agricultural services designed to address gender-specific needs
        • gender-responsive adaptation 1 -- climate adaptation strategies that address differential impacts and needs by gender
        • gender-responsive technical assistance 1 -- support services designed to address specific needs and barriers faced by different genders
        • gender-responsive business planning 1 -- business strategy development incorporating gender equity and inclusion principles
        • gender-responsive artificial intelligence 1 -- ai systems designed to recognize and mitigate gender biases in service delivery
        • gender-sensitive interventions 3 -- development interventions designed to address gender-specific needs and constraints
        • gender-sensitive agricultural interventions 3 -- agricultural programs that explicitly address gender roles and women's participation in farming activities
        • gender-sensitive measurement 1 -- evaluation framework incorporating gender equality considerations
        • gender-sensitive programming 1 -- design and delivery of programs that consider and address gender-specific needs and impacts
        • gender-inclusive strategies 1 -- interventions designed to address gender-based barriers and promote equal participation
        • gender-inclusive planning 1 -- incorporating gender equality and social inclusion considerations into development planning
        • gender-inclusive water management 1 -- water governance approaches that ensure equitable participation of women in decision-making
        • gender inclusivity 1 -- ensuring equal participation and empowerment of all genders in development processes
        • gender equality integration 1 -- systematic inclusion of gender considerations in development programs
        • gender dynamics 2 -- patterns of interaction and power relations between genders
        • gender norms analysis 1 -- systematic examination of social expectations and roles that limit participation and decision-making
        • gender norms measurement 1 -- quantitative tools for assessing and tracking changes in gender-related social norms
        • gender productivity gap 1 -- difference in agricultural productivity between male and female farmers
        • gendered labor response 2 -- differential impacts of external shocks on men's and women's work patterns
        • gender action learning system 1 -- participatory empowerment tool addressing gender inequities in development
        • gender-climate-nutrition integration 3 -- holistic approach linking gender equity, climate resilience, and nutrition outcomes
    • Social Inclusion and Equity -- Social inclusion, intersectionality, youth empowerment, and marginalized group engagement
      • Social Inclusion Frameworks -- Frameworks and approaches for inclusive development
        • social inclusion 2 -- ensuring marginalized groups have access to opportunities and services
        • intersectionality 2 -- analysis considering multiple overlapping social identities and systems of oppression
        • social equity integration 3 -- ensuring marginalized groups have equal access to resources and decision-making in development programs
        • inclusive scaling 4 -- strategy for expanding improved varieties that ensures broad access across different farm types and socioeconomic levels
        • inclusive business models 3 -- commercial strategies ensuring access to services for smallholder farmers and marginalized groups
        • economic inclusion 1 -- expanding access to economic opportunities for marginalized populations
      • Youth and Marginalized Groups -- Empowerment of youth and other marginalized populations
        • youth empowerment 2 -- strategies to engage and support young people in agricultural development
        • youth entrepreneurship 2 -- support for business development and job creation among young people
      • Intra-household and Community Dynamics -- Decision-making and resource allocation within households and communities
        • intra-household decision-making
        • intra-household allocation 1 -- distribution of resources among household members
        • intra-household information sharing 1 -- flow of knowledge and data between members within farming households
        • wife-husband model 1 -- agricultural mechanization approach targeting gender-inclusive technology adoption
        • time poverty 1 -- insufficient time to meet basic needs due to excessive work burdens
        • dignified work 1 -- employment that provides income, respect, safety and social recognition
      • Justice and Rights -- Environmental justice, climate justice, and rights-based approaches
        • environmental justice 2 -- equitable distribution of environmental benefits and burdens across social groups
        • climate justice 2 -- fair treatment of all people regarding climate change impacts and solutions
        • just transition 3 -- equitable shift to sustainable economy that leaves no one behind and creates decent work opportunities
        • water justice 1 -- equitable access to water resources and inclusive decision-making in water management
        • distributive justice 1 -- fair allocation of resources ensuring equitable access to quantity, quality, and affordability
        • procedural justice 1 -- inclusive and participatory decision-making processes involving affected stakeholders
        • land tenure security 1 -- legal and institutional frameworks that provide secure rights to land ownership or use
        • food sovereignty 1 -- community control over food production systems and nutritional self-determination
    • Social Norms and Behavior Change -- Social norms change, behavior change communication, community conversations, and gender norms analysis
      • Behavior Change Approaches -- Methods and tools for changing behaviors and social norms
        • social behavior change communication 4 -- strategic communication approach to influence behaviors and social norms
        • behavioral change communication 2 -- interventions designed to influence individual decision-making and behavior
        • behavior change communication 1 -- strategic communication approach to influence individual and community behaviors
        • behavior change model 1 -- framework for understanding and facilitating adoption of new practices through capability, opportunity, and motivation
        • behaviour change wheel 1 -- framework for understanding drivers of behavior change in agricultural adoption
        • behavioral interventions 1 -- psychological approaches to influence farmer decision-making
        • behavioral nudges 1 -- interventions that guide behavior through choice architecture without restricting options
        • low-carbon behaviors 3 -- individual and household actions that reduce greenhouse gas emissions
      • Social Norms -- Analysis and transformation of social norms
        • social norms 2 -- shared expectations and rules that guide behavior within groups
        • positive deviance approach 3 -- identifying and learning from individuals or groups with better outcomes despite similar constraints
        • pioneer positive deviance 8 -- approach that identifies and supports farmers already developing innovative local solutions
        • positive deviance 1 -- approach identifying successful practices among those facing similar challenges
      • Community Engagement Methods -- Participatory and community-based approaches to social change
        • community conversations 4 -- structured dialogue approach for community engagement and behavior change
        • theatre of the oppressed 2 -- participatory theatre methodology for addressing social issues and power dynamics
        • participatory theatre 1 -- interactive drama approach used for community education and social change
        • community theater 1 -- use of theatrical performances and storytelling to raise awareness and promote social change in communities
        • forum theatre 1 -- interactive performance method to explore social issues and solutions
        • edutainment 1 -- educational content delivery through entertainment media to engage and inform audiences
        • decolonial pedagogies 2 -- educational frameworks that challenge colonial knowledge systems and power structures
        • feminist political ecology 1 -- analytical framework examining gender, power, and environmental resource access
    • Social Protection and Humanitarian Response -- Social protection systems, cash transfers, shock-responsive social protection, and humanitarian-development nexus
      • Social Protection Systems -- Programs and systems providing social protection to vulnerable populations
        • social protection 12 -- policies and programs designed to reduce poverty and vulnerability
        • social protection systems 1 -- government programs providing support to vulnerable populations during crises
        • social protection policy 1 -- government frameworks for providing assistance to vulnerable populations
        • shock-responsive social protection 1 -- social safety net programs that can rapidly scale up during emergencies
        • adaptive social protection 1 -- social safety nets that adjust to climate and economic shocks
        • ultra-poor graduation model 3 -- integrated intervention combining cash transfers, assets, training, and coaching for extreme poverty
        • social transfer programs 1 -- government programs providing financial or in-kind support to households
        • lean season assistance 1 -- humanitarian support during periods when food stocks are depleted and prices peak
      • Cash and Financial Transfers -- Cash transfer programs and digital financial support
        • cash transfers 2 -- direct monetary payments to individuals or households as social assistance
        • cash transfer programs 2 -- direct monetary assistance provided to households to improve welfare outcomes
        • digital transfers 2 -- electronic delivery of cash assistance through mobile money and digital platforms
        • digital cash transfers 1 -- electronic delivery of monetary assistance to beneficiaries
        • tapered cash transfers 1 -- gradually diminishing cash payments designed to support transition to self-reliance
        • conditional subsidies 1 -- financial incentives tied to specific agricultural practices or outcomes
        • savings and lending groups 1 -- community-based financial institutions that provide members with savings and credit services
        • saving for change 1 -- community-based savings and lending mechanism for rural financial inclusion
        • financial literacy training 5 -- educational programs teaching budgeting, savings, credit management, and investment decision-making skills
        • financial management training 1 -- educational programs to develop business financial skills and practices
      • Humanitarian Response -- Humanitarian programming and development-humanitarian linkages
        • humanitarian response 2 -- coordinated assistance to populations affected by crises and disasters
        • humanitarian-development nexus 1 -- integration of scientific research into humanitarian efforts for sustainable solutions
        • humanitarian-development-peace nexus 4 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian aid, development programming, and peacebuilding
        • hdp nexus 1 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian, development, and peace interventions
        • anticipatory action 9 -- proactive interventions based on early warning signals to prevent crises
        • refugee policy impact 2 -- political and social effects of policies governing refugee populations
    • Conflict, Fragility, and Displacement -- Conflict-sensitive programming, fragile settings, forced displacement, climate-conflict nexus, and peacebuilding
      • Conflict-Sensitive Programming -- Approaches for working in conflict-affected and fragile settings
        • conflict-sensitive programming 6 -- development approach that considers potential conflict risks and promotes peace-building
        • conflict sensitivity 5 -- considering peace and conflict dynamics in development interventions
        • fragile and conflict-affected settings 6 -- contexts characterized by violence, weak institutions and humanitarian crises
        • fragile and conflict-affected systems 2 -- food systems operating in contexts of institutional fragility and conflict
