        • soil bulk density measurement 1 -- techniques for measuring soil compaction to assess soil health and carbon stocks
        • soil quality index 1 -- composite measure of soil health based on multiple indicators
        • soil fertility assessment 1 -- evaluation of soil nutrient content and quality for agricultural decision-making
        • soil health assessment 7 -- evaluation of soil physical, chemical, and biological properties for agricultural sustainability
        • soil diagnostics 1 -- field-based assessment of soil physical, chemical, and biological characteristics
        • genetic diversity assessment 2 -- evaluation of genetic variation within crop populations
        • genetic parameter estimation 1 -- statistical methods to quantify heritable variation in traits
        • heritability estimation 2 -- quantification of genetic contribution to trait variation
        • heritability metrics 2 -- statistical measures quantifying genetic versus environmental contribution to trait variation
        • combining ability analysis 2 -- assessment of parental lines' ability to produce superior hybrid offspring
        • combining ability 1 -- genetic capacity of parents to produce superior hybrid offspring
        • morphological characterization 4 -- systematic description of physical traits in crop varieties or landraces
        • phenotyping 2 -- measurement and analysis of observable plant characteristics
        • digital phenotyping 2 -- automated collection of plant trait data using digital technologies
        • high-throughput phenotyping 4 -- automated measurement of plant traits using advanced technologies
        • sparse phenotyping 1 -- strategy to reduce phenotyping costs while maintaining selection accuracy
        • multi-stress phenotyping 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop performance under multiple environmental stresses
        • plant phenotyping 1 -- measurement and analysis of plant physical and biochemical traits
        • acoustic sensing 1 -- detection and analysis of sound patterns for monitoring and identification purposes
        • flow cytometry 1 -- laboratory technique for determining chromosome numbers and analyzing cellular characteristics
        • microbial diversity assessment 1 -- analysis of bacterial community composition and structure in environmental samples
        • rhizosphere microbiome analysis 1 -- study of microbial communities in plant root zones
        • metatranscriptomics 1 -- analysis of total RNA from environmental samples to detect pathogens
        • genomic diversity analysis 1 -- use of DNA sequencing data to understand genetic variation patterns in crops
        • genomic profiling 1 -- comprehensive analysis of genetic material to identify specific traits or pathogens
        • population genomics 2 -- study of genetic variation and evolution within and between populations
        • whole-genome sequencing 1 -- complete DNA analysis technique for pathogen identification and characterization
        • targeted sequencing 1 -- focused DNA sequencing approach to analyze specific genomic regions of interest
        • skim-sequencing 1 -- low-coverage DNA sequencing approach for cost-effective genotyping
        • dna fingerprinting 1 -- genetic technique for identifying crop varieties using molecular markers
        • genetic fingerprinting 1 -- molecular technique for identifying unique genetic profiles of varieties
        • molecular marker analysis 2 -- use of DNA markers to identify genes or traits in breeding programs
        • haplotype analysis 1 -- study of linked genetic variants inherited together as blocks
        • chromatin accessibility mapping 1 -- technique to identify regions of genome accessible for transcription factor binding
        • comparative genomic analysis 1 -- comparison of genome structure and function between different organisms or varieties
        • transcriptome analysis 1 -- comprehensive study of RNA transcripts produced by genome under specific conditions
        • multiplex pcr 1 -- molecular technique enabling simultaneous detection of multiple pathogens in one assay
        • loop-mediated isothermal amplification 1 -- DNA amplification technique that operates at constant temperature for rapid detection
        • pangenome construction 1 -- assembling comprehensive genomic landscape capturing complete genetic diversity within species
        • pan-genomics 1 -- study of entire gene sets across multiple individuals or varieties of a species
        • single nucleotide polymorphisms 1 -- DNA sequence variations where single nucleotides differ between individuals
        • polymorphism information content 1 -- measure of genetic marker informativeness based on allele frequencies
        • quantitative trait loci mapping 9 -- identification of genomic regions controlling quantitative traits
        • quantitative trait locus mapping 4 -- genetic technique to identify chromosomal regions controlling quantitative traits
        • quantitative trait nucleotides 1 -- DNA sequence variants that contribute to quantitative trait variation
        • meta-qtl analysis 1 -- statistical method combining qtl data from multiple studies to identify consensus regions
        • qtl pyramiding 1 -- breeding strategy that stacks multiple quantitative trait loci to combine beneficial traits
        • genome-wide association mapping 4 -- genetic analysis technique to identify genes associated with specific traits
        • genome-wide association analysis 1 -- statistical approach to identify genetic variants associated with traits across the genome
        • single-step genome-wide association study 1 -- genomic analysis method combining pedigree and marker information to identify trait-associated genetic variants
        • multi-omics integration 3 -- combining genomics, transcriptomics, and metabolomics data for analysis
        • multi-omics approaches 2 -- integration of genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics and metabolomics data
        • metabolomics-assisted breeding 1 -- integration of metabolite profiling with breeding programs to enhance crop improvement
        • line × tester analysis 1 -- breeding design to evaluate combining ability and hybrid performance
        • testcross evaluation 1 -- crossing experimental lines with standard testers to evaluate performance
        • multi-trait selection index 1 -- breeding tool that combines multiple traits into single selection criterion
        • multi-trait genomic selection 1 -- genomic selection approach considering multiple traits simultaneously
        • optimal contribution selection 1 -- breeding strategy to optimize genetic contributions while managing inbreeding
        • realized genetic gain 1 -- actual improvement in crop traits achieved through breeding over time
        • genetic gain assessment 1 -- standardized measurement of breeding progress across crops and traits
        • genetic variance partitioning 1 -- statistical decomposition of trait variation into additive and non-additive components
        • heterotic pattern integration 1 -- breeding approach to combine complementary genetic groups for hybrid vigor
        • heterotic group development 1 -- classification of breeding lines based on combining ability for hybrid development
        • rapid-cycle genomic selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach using genomic data to reduce crop development time
        • rapid cycle recurrent selection 1 -- accelerated breeding approach that reduces cycle time from three years to one year
        • family-based selection 2 -- breeding strategy using family performance data to select superior genetic lines
        • population improvement 1 -- genetic enhancement of breeding populations for desired traits
        • pollen tube growth analysis 1 -- study of pollen tube development to understand fertilization barriers
        • anthocyanin content evaluation 1 -- measurement of pigment compounds in plant tissues
        • phenolic compound extraction 1 -- process of isolating and purifying phenolic compounds from plant materials
        • protein digestibility 1 -- measure of how well proteins can be broken down and absorbed by the body
        • grain quality assessment 1 -- evaluation of physical and chemical properties affecting grain value
        • nutritional adequacy ratio 1 -- measure of household food security based on nutritional requirements
        • micronutrient adequacy projection 1 -- modeling future nutritional status and micronutrient availability
        • data fusion 3 -- integration of multiple data sources or sensor types to improve analytical outcomes
        • lean data collection 1 -- efficient data gathering focusing on essential information
        • data digitization 2 -- process of converting analog information into digital formats for improved management
        • fairification 1 -- process of making data findable, accessible, interoperable and reusable
        • rotated empirical orthogonal functions 1 -- statistical technique for analyzing spatial patterns in climate data
        • spatiotemporal climate analysis 1 -- statistical analysis of climate patterns across space and time dimensions
        • climate attribution 2 -- scientific methods to quantify human influence on extreme weather events
        • climate impact projections 2 -- quantitative assessment of expected climate change effects on agricultural systems and crop yields
        • climate suitability mapping 2 -- spatial analysis to identify optimal locations for crops under current and future climate conditions
        • climate hazard mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of current and projected climate risks
        • climate risk profiling 4 -- systematic assessment of climate hazards and vulnerabilities for agricultural planning
        • flood risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas vulnerable to flooding and quantify risk levels
        • soil erosion risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas prone to soil loss and degradation
        • vulnerability hotspot mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas of high climate and social risk
        • risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of disease or hazard probability
        • landscape performance indicators 1 -- metrics used to assess sustainability and functionality of agricultural landscapes
        • indicator repository 1 -- curated knowledge base of metrics for tracking adaptation progress
        • recommendation domains 1 -- geographic areas with similar conditions suitable for specific technology adoption
        • spatial production allocation modeling 1 -- geographic modeling of crop distribution and productivity patterns
        • equilibrium displacement modeling 1 -- economic analysis method examining market interactions and price effects
        • economic surplus modeling 1 -- analytical approach to quantify economic welfare impacts and benefits of interventions
        • stochastic dynamic population modeling 1 -- probabilistic modeling approach for livestock population health and economics
        • biophysical simulation modeling 1 -- computational approach to model water and nutrient flows in agricultural systems
        • process-based simulation 1 -- modeling approach using mechanistic understanding of biological and physical processes
        • rapid response modeling 1 -- streamlined analytical approach to quickly assess impacts of global crises
        • global agricultural modeling 1 -- mathematical simulation of agricultural systems at global scale to analyze market and production dynamics
        • agricultural integrated assessment models 1 -- quantitative models combining economic, crop growth, and hydrology components for food security analysis
        • hydro-economic modeling 1 -- integrated analysis combining hydrological and economic data for water resource management
        • water balance model 2 -- computational framework quantifying water inputs, outputs, and storage changes in agricultural systems
        • flood modeling 1 -- computational simulation of water flow patterns and flood behavior
        • watershed modeling 1 -- simulation of water flow and management at landscape scale
        • atmospheric dispersion modeling 1 -- mathematical simulation of how gases spread through the atmosphere
        • crop suitability modeling 1 -- analytical framework to assess geographic areas' suitability for growing different crops based on environmental conditions
        • micronutrient contribution modeling 1 -- analytical approach to calculate and visualize nutrient composition of food sources
        • yield prediction modeling 1 -- use of mathematical models to forecast agricultural crop yields
        • yield projection 2 -- forecasting future crop production based on various biophysical and socioeconomic factors
        • irrigation demand forecasting 6 -- prediction of crop water requirements to optimize irrigation scheduling and water allocation
        • machine learning forecasting 6 -- artificial intelligence techniques applied to predict agricultural outcomes and resource needs
        • rainfall forecasting 1 -- prediction of precipitation patterns to support agricultural decision-making
        • seasonal rainfall forecasting 1 -- prediction of precipitation patterns over seasonal timescales for agricultural planning
        • sub-seasonal forecasting 1 -- weather and climate prediction for timescales of 2 weeks to 2 months
        • reservoir forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling of future water storage levels using multiple data sources
        • drought prediction 2 -- forecasting techniques to anticipate drought conditions using meteorological and satellite data
        • flood prediction 1 -- forecasting approach to anticipate inundation extent and timing for agricultural risk management
        • malnutrition forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling to anticipate acute child malnutrition for early warning systems
        • food crisis prediction 3 -- analytical approaches for forecasting food security emergencies
        • artificial intelligence famine forecasting 1 -- machine learning approaches to predict famine conditions and food crises
        • impact-based forecasting 1 -- weather prediction approach that focuses on potential impacts rather than just meteorological conditions
        • forecast-based advisory 1 -- decision support system using weather forecasts to guide agricultural practices
        • seasonal climate forecast 1 -- long-range weather prediction providing probabilistic outlooks for upcoming growing seasons
        • agroclimatic forecasting 3 -- prediction of weather and climate conditions relevant to agricultural planning
        • climate forecasting 4 -- prediction of future climate conditions to support agricultural and disaster preparedness planning
        • heat stress analysis 4 -- quantitative assessment of temperature impacts on crops, livestock, and human populations
        • drought stress management 2 -- strategies to mitigate water deficit impacts on crop production
        • drought tolerance screening 2 -- systematic evaluation of crop varieties for resistance to water stress
        • drought experiments 1 -- controlled research trials to evaluate crop performance under water stress conditions
        • pathotype screening 1 -- testing plant materials against different strains of pathogens to assess resistance
        • disease resistance screening 4 -- systematic evaluation of genetic materials for pathogen tolerance
        • germplasm screening 1 -- systematic evaluation of genetic material for desired traits
        • anti-methanogenic screening 1 -- laboratory testing to identify compounds that reduce methane production
        • integrated pathogen screening 1 -- comprehensive testing approach combining multiple methods to identify disease resistance
        • pest risk identification 2 -- systematic surveillance and early warning systems for agricultural pest and disease threats
        • serosurvey 1 -- epidemiological study measuring antibodies in blood samples
        • environmental sampling 2 -- collection of environmental samples for laboratory analysis
        • biosecurity assessment 1 -- evaluation of measures to prevent pathogen introduction and spread
        • animal welfare assessment 1 -- evaluation of animal well-being in production systems
        • breed evaluation 1 -- systematic assessment of animal genetic resources for performance traits
        • forage evaluation 2 -- assessment of feed crops for biomass production and nutritional quality
        • stress tolerance indices 2 -- mathematical measures used to quantify plant performance under stress conditions
        • trait preferences 3 -- farmer and stakeholder preferences for specific crop characteristics
        • trait preference ranking 1 -- systematic evaluation and ordering of livestock or crop characteristics based on stakeholder priorities
        • consumer preference mapping 2 -- systematic assessment of consumer preferences for product traits and characteristics
        • demand signaling 1 -- systematic approach to understanding stakeholder needs and priorities for scaling innovations
        • demand signalling 1 -- process of identifying and articulating stakeholder needs and priorities for innovation adoption
        • market information systems 1 -- platforms for collecting and disseminating agricultural price and market data
        • price transmission 1 -- how price changes propagate through market chains
        • food environment intervention 2 -- strategies to modify food availability, accessibility, and affordability
        • food quality assessment 1 -- evaluation of nutritional, biochemical, and sensory attributes of food products
        • nutritional status 2 -- condition of health determined by nutrient intake and utilization
        • minimum dietary diversity 1 -- indicator measuring consumption across different food groups as proxy for nutrient adequacy
        • protein consumption adequacy 1 -- meeting recommended dietary protein intake levels
        • malnutrition burden 1 -- coexistence of undernutrition, micronutrient deficiencies, and overweight/obesity
        • double burden of malnutrition 3 -- coexistence of under- and overnutrition within individuals, households, and populations
        • hidden hunger 1 -- micronutrient deficiencies despite adequate caloric intake
        • quality testing 1 -- systematic evaluation of crop grain quality parameters
        • quality assurance 2 -- systematic processes to ensure products or services meet specified standards
        • quality control 2 -- systematic processes to ensure product standards
        • iso/iec 17025 1 -- international standard for testing and calibration laboratory competence
        • standard operating procedures 1 -- documented processes that ensure consistent quality and compliance in service delivery
        • seed quality testing 1 -- assessment of seed viability, purity, and health for conservation and distribution
        • seed health testing 2 -- protocols for detecting pathogens and ensuring seed quality
        • phytosanitary testing 1 -- laboratory procedures to detect plant pathogens and ensure plant health
        • virus indexing 1 -- systematic testing to detect and identify viral pathogens in plant materials
        • rapid diagnostic testing 1 -- quick field-based methods for disease identification
        • disease diagnosis 3 -- systematic identification and characterization of plant diseases
        • pathogen identification 3 -- laboratory techniques for diagnosing disease-causing organisms
        • pesticide residue detection 1 -- analytical techniques to identify and quantify chemical residues in crops
        • antibiotic resistance monitoring 3 -- surveillance and measurement of antimicrobial resistance in bacterial populations
        • antimicrobial resistance surveillance 11 -- systematic monitoring of antimicrobial resistance patterns in pathogens to inform public health responses
        • zoonotic disease surveillance 6 -- monitoring and detection of diseases transmissible between animals and humans
        • viral disease surveillance 1 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant viral pathogens
        • disease surveillance 4 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant diseases across geographic areas
        • food safety monitoring 2 -- systematic surveillance of contaminants and hazards in food systems
        • traceability 2 -- ability to track products through supply chain stages
        • traceability systems 1 -- systems for tracking products through supply chains
        • carbon credit certification 2 -- formal verification process that enables farmers to monetize emission reductions through carbon markets
        • carbon market readiness 1 -- capacity building to enable participation in carbon trading mechanisms
        • climate finance tracking 6 -- systems for monitoring flow and use of climate-related funding
        • climate finance taxonomy 1 -- classification framework for directing climate investments toward specific sectors and activities
        • adaptation finance 3 -- financial resources dedicated to climate change adaptation activities
        • climate adaptation indicators 2 -- metrics used to measure progress in building resilience to climate change
        • global goal on adaptation 1 -- international climate policy framework for adaptation planning and implementation
        • nationally determined contributions 20 -- country-specific climate action plans under the Paris Agreement
        • national adaptation plans 5 -- medium to long-term national planning processes for climate adaptation
        • policy uptake 3 -- adoption and implementation of research findings in policy frameworks
        • policy coherence 8 -- alignment and coordination of policies across different levels and sectors of governance
        • policy alignment 2 -- coordination of policies and institutional frameworks for coherent implementation
        • policy integration 7 -- incorporating innovations into official government frameworks
        • policy harmonization 1 -- aligning policies across jurisdictions for consistency
        • policy dialogue 2 -- structured discussions among stakeholders to inform policy development and implementation
        • policy advocacy 2 -- strategic efforts to influence policy decisions using evidence
        • science-policy interface 1 -- mechanisms connecting scientific research with policy development and implementation
        • evidence-based water resource management 1 -- water planning and allocation using data-driven analysis and modeling
        • water accounting 9 -- systematic assessment and monitoring of water resources and their utilization
        • water productivity 10 -- efficiency of water use in producing agricultural output per unit of water consumed
        • water productivity optimization 1 -- maximizing agricultural output per unit of water used
        • irrigation water productivity 2 -- measure of crop yield per unit of irrigation water applied
        • water consumption mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of water use patterns in agricultural systems
        • aquifer recharge zone mapping 1 -- identification and mapping of areas where groundwater can be naturally replenished
        • integrated water storage assessment 1 -- comprehensive evaluation of multiple water storage types and their temporal dynamics for resource management
        • inland valley suitability assessment 1 -- evaluation of wetland areas for rice cultivation potential
        • multicrop land suitability 1 -- spatial analysis to identify optimal locations for integrated cropping systems
        • soil organic carbon mapping 1 -- spatial assessment of carbon content in soils
        • carbon uptake 1 -- process by which ecosystems absorb atmospheric carbon dioxide through photosynthesis
        • greenhouse gas emission reduction 2 -- mitigation of climate-warming gases from agricultural practices
        • low-emission agriculture 3 -- farming practices designed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • emission intensity 1 -- greenhouse gas emissions per unit of agricultural output
        • emission intensity per weight gain 1 -- ratio of greenhouse gas emissions to livestock weight gain
        • enteric fermentation 2 -- digestive process in ruminants that produces methane as a byproduct
        • methane emissions 1 -- release of methane gas from livestock digestion processes
        • methane emission reduction 2 -- practices to decrease methane gas release from agricultural systems
        • enteric methane reduction 1 -- strategies to reduce methane emissions from livestock digestion
        • greenhouse gas emission reduction 2 -- mitigation of climate-warming gases from agricultural practices
        • carbon neutrality 1 -- achieving net-zero carbon dioxide emissions
        • blue carbon opportunities 1 -- carbon sequestration potential in marine and coastal ecosystems
        • bamboo carbon sink 1 -- carbon storage capacity of bamboo forests and plantations
        • mineral-associated organic carbon 1 -- soil carbon fraction bound to mineral particles that provides long-term carbon storage
        • soil organic carbon sequestration 2 -- process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon in soil through organic matter accumulation
        • ecosystem carbon sequestration 3 -- natural processes by which ecosystems capture and store atmospheric carbon
        • carbon trading 3 -- market-based mechanism for trading carbon credits to offset greenhouse gas emissions
        • eco-compensation 2 -- payment mechanism to compensate for environmental services and conservation efforts
        • biodiversity-positive financing 1 -- investment mechanisms that channel finance toward biodiversity conservation and sustainable livelihoods
        • planetary boundaries 1 -- safe operating limits for Earth system processes that regulate planet stability
        • resilience science 1 -- interdisciplinary field studying systems' capacity to cope, adapt, and transform under stress
        • social-ecological systems 1 -- integrated systems of humans and natural components
        • socio-ecological resilience 3 -- capacity of social-ecological systems to adapt and maintain function under stress
        • socio-ecological gains 1 -- joint improvements in productivity, quality, environmental sustainability, and equitable benefit distribution
        • adaptive capacity 3 -- ability of systems to adjust to climate change and reduce vulnerability
        • coping strategies 3 -- actions taken by households to manage economic or environmental stress
        • transformational adaptation 4 -- fundamental changes in agricultural systems to address climate change impacts
        • transformative adaptation 3 -- fundamental changes in systems to address climate impacts
        • maladaptation 2 -- adaptation efforts that unintentionally create new risks or vulnerabilities
        • climate risk perception 1 -- farmers' subjective assessment and interpretation of climate-related threats
        • climate risk information 2 -- data and analysis about climate-related hazards used for decision-making
        • climate-informed decision-making 2 -- incorporating climate data and projections into planning and management
        • climate risk management 11 -- strategies and practices for identifying, assessing, and mitigating climate-related risks
        • climate risk assessment 16 -- evaluation of climate hazard exposure and vulnerability
        • climate vulnerability 9 -- increased susceptibility to climate-related risks and impacts
        • climate fragility 2 -- vulnerability of systems to climate-related stresses and shocks
        • climate-conflict analysis 1 -- analytical approach to measure connections between climate change and conflict occurrence
        • climate-peace-security nexus 5 -- integrated framework addressing interconnected climate, peace and security challenges
        • climate-displacement nexus 4 -- relationship between climate change impacts and human displacement
        • climate-conflict nexus 12 -- the interconnected relationship between climate change impacts and violent conflict emergence
        • humanitarian-development-peace nexus 4 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian aid, development programming, and peacebuilding
        • humanitarian-development nexus 1 -- integration of scientific research into humanitarian efforts for sustainable solutions
        • hdp nexus 1 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian, development, and peace interventions
        • human security 3 -- protection of individuals from threats to their safety and wellbeing
        • conflict-affected settings 2 -- regions experiencing or recovering from armed conflict or civil unrest
        • fragile and conflict-affected settings 6 -- contexts characterized by violence, weak institutions and humanitarian crises
        • fragile and conflict-affected systems 2 -- food systems operating in contexts of institutional fragility and conflict
        • refugee policy impact 2 -- political and social effects of policies governing refugee populations
        • forced displacement 4 -- involuntary movement of people due to conflict, persecution, or environmental factors
        • climate-induced displacement 4 -- forced migration resulting from climate change impacts and extreme weather events
        • managed resettlement 1 -- planned relocation of communities away from climate hazards
        • displacement 2 -- forced movement of people from their homes due to conflict, climate, or economic factors
        • migration policy 1 -- government strategies and frameworks for managing human mobility
        • mental health impacts 2 -- psychological consequences of climate change and displacement on affected populations
        • just transition 3 -- equitable shift to sustainable economy that leaves no one behind and creates decent work opportunities
        • environmental justice 2 -- equitable distribution of environmental benefits and burdens across social groups
        • climate justice 2 -- fair treatment of all people regarding climate change impacts and solutions
        • water justice 1 -- equitable access to water resources and inclusive decision-making in water management
        • distributive justice 1 -- fair allocation of resources ensuring equitable access to quantity, quality, and affordability
        • procedural justice 1 -- inclusive and participatory decision-making processes involving affected stakeholders
        • environmental peacebuilding 2 -- use of environmental cooperation to promote peace and social cohesion in post-conflict areas
        • social protection 12 -- policies and programs designed to reduce poverty and vulnerability
        • social protection systems 1 -- government programs providing support to vulnerable populations during crises
        • social protection policy 1 -- government frameworks for providing assistance to vulnerable populations
        • adaptive social protection 1 -- social safety nets that adjust to climate and economic shocks
        • shock-responsive social protection 1 -- social safety net programs that can rapidly scale up during emergencies
        • cash transfers 2 -- direct monetary payments to individuals or households as social assistance
        • cash transfer programs 2 -- direct monetary assistance provided to households to improve welfare outcomes
        • tapered cash transfers 1 -- gradually diminishing cash payments designed to support transition to self-reliance
        • digital cash transfers 1 -- electronic delivery of monetary assistance to beneficiaries
        • digital transfers 2 -- electronic delivery of cash assistance through mobile money and digital platforms
        • social transfer programs 1 -- government programs providing financial or in-kind support to households
        • ultra-poor graduation model 3 -- integrated intervention combining cash transfers, assets, training, and coaching for extreme poverty
        • lean season assistance 1 -- humanitarian support during periods when food stocks are depleted and prices peak
        • conditional subsidies 1 -- financial incentives tied to specific agricultural practices or outcomes
        • food subsidies 1 -- government support to reduce food costs for targeted populations
        • subsidy repurposing 1 -- redirecting government subsidy spending toward more effective development investments
        • agricultural support repurposing 1 -- reforming agricultural policies and financial instruments for sustainable food system transformation
        • fertilizer subsidy reform 1 -- policy changes to agricultural input support programs
        • fisheries subsidies 1 -- government financial support to fishing industry operations
        • agricultural distortions 1 -- policy-induced inefficiencies that affect agricultural productivity and resource allocation
        • index insurance 3 -- insurance products that use objective indices to trigger payouts rather than individual loss assessments
        • risk contingent credit 3 -- financial mechanism that links agricultural credit with insurance coverage to manage production risks
        • area-based yield insurance 1 -- insurance coverage based on regional average yields rather than individual farm losses
        • satellite-based crop insurance 1 -- risk management mechanism using remote sensing data to assess crop losses and trigger payouts
        • picture-based insurance 1 -- digital crop insurance using smartphone images to assess and indemnify crop damage
        • weather index-based insurance 1 -- agricultural insurance that pays based on weather parameters rather than actual crop losses
        • bundled livestock insurance 1 -- integrated insurance products combining multiple risk protections for pastoral communities
        • climate-smart credit scoring 2 -- financial assessment tool incorporating climate resilience factors for agricultural lending
        • climate-linked credit scoring 1 -- financial risk assessment integrating climate data with borrower profiles
        • blended finance 8 -- combination of public and private funding to reduce investment risks
        • blended financing 1 -- financial strategy combining public and private funding sources to mobilize resources for development
        • climate finance mechanisms 4 -- financial instruments and structures designed to fund climate mitigation and adaptation projects
        • climate finance mobilization 1 -- strategies to attract and deploy financial resources for climate adaptation and mitigation
        • investment planning 3 -- systematic process for identifying and prioritizing climate-resilient investments
        • investment action plan 2 -- strategic framework outlining funding requirements and implementation pathways
        • investment readiness 2 -- preparation of enterprises for attracting investment funding
        • bankable investment projects 1 -- development projects structured to meet financial institution lending criteria
        • bankable project development 1 -- preparation of investment-ready proposals that meet funding institution requirements
        • business model development 8 -- creation of sustainable commercial frameworks for technology adoption
        • inclusive business models 3 -- commercial strategies ensuring access to services for smallholder farmers and marginalized groups
        • agroecological business modeling 1 -- development of sustainable business models based on ecological principles
        • sustainable business model 1 -- commercial framework balancing economic viability with environmental and social benefits
        • business incubation 4 -- support system for developing and accelerating new enterprises
        • accelerator programs 1 -- structured support programs for scaling agricultural innovations and businesses
        • impact accelerator 1 -- coordinated system design to enhance adoption of agricultural innovations
        • scaling fund 1 -- financial mechanism supporting innovation expansion and market penetration
        • scaling hubs 1 -- regional platforms that coordinate partnerships and knowledge sharing for agricultural innovation adoption
        • scaling champions 2 -- trusted individuals who coordinate actors and support innovation adoption
        • hub model 3 -- territorial multistakeholder platforms integrating research, innovation and extension
        • three-legged stool approach 1 -- innovative framework for improving meat safety in informal sectors
        • end-to-end approach 2 -- comprehensive intervention strategy addressing multiple points in a system
        • responsible innovation 2 -- innovation processes that consider ethical, social, and environmental implications
        • agricultural research for development 1 -- research programs designed to generate agricultural innovations for development outcomes
        • technology diffusion 3 -- spread and adoption of new technologies across markets and users
        • technology transfer 6 -- process of moving innovations from research to practical application
        • technology dissemination 2 -- process of spreading improved agricultural technologies to farmers
        • last-mile delivery 2 -- reaching end users through local agent networks and service providers

        • decentralized service delivery 1 -- distribution of services through local providers rather than centralized institutions

        • shared services 2 -- centralized provision of common resources and capabilities across organizations
        • localization 1 -- shifting leadership and decision-making to local actors in development interventions

        • multi-stakeholder collaboration 5 -- coordinated engagement among diverse organizations and sectors toward common goals
        • multi-stakeholder engagement 9 -- collaborative processes involving diverse actors including farmers, government, researchers, and private sector

        • private sector engagement 2 -- involving commercial entities in development initiatives

        • collective action 2 -- coordinated community efforts to address shared challenges
        • polycentric governance 1 -- multi-level decision-making involving multiple centers of authority

        • village-level governance 3 -- local institutional arrangements for decision-making and resource management
        • community-based governance 2 -- local management systems where communities control and manage shared resources

        • community-based management 6 -- natural resource management approach led by local communities
        • community-based resource management 2 -- participatory strategy where local communities manage their natural resources
        • community-led environmental management 2 -- local community-driven approaches to natural resource management and environmental conservation
        • community-led initiatives 2 -- locally driven programs managed by community members
        • community-driven extension 1 -- extension services led and owned by local communities rather than external agencies
        • community-based extension 1 -- extension service delivery model using local community members as agents
        • farmer-led extension 2 -- agricultural advisory systems driven by peer-to-peer knowledge sharing among farmers
        • farmer-led innovation 2 -- innovation processes initiated and driven by farmers themselves
        • community-led innovation 1 -- innovation processes initiated and controlled by local communities
        • innovation validation 1 -- systematic testing of prototypes and assumptions with real users before full implementation
        • crowdsourcing 1 -- engaging large groups to collect data or perform tasks using technology
        • crowdsourcing 1 -- engaging large groups to collect data or perform tasks using technology
        • interactive voice response 1 -- technology that allows interaction with computer systems through voice commands and responses
        • radio-based extension 1 -- using radio broadcasts to deliver agricultural information and climate advisories
        • mobile-based extension 1 -- delivering agricultural advisory services through mobile phone platforms

        • participatory extension 2 -- collaborative method of delivering agricultural knowledge involving farmer participation

        • ai-driven agricultural extension 4 -- use of artificial intelligence to deliver agricultural advice and information to farmers
        • digital agricultural advisory 2 -- technology-enabled extension services delivering agricultural recommendations
        • digital agricultural advisory services 1 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural information and support to farmers
        • digital agro-advisory services 4 -- technology-enabled platforms delivering agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital advisory services 22 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital advisory platforms 5 -- technology-based systems for delivering agricultural and climate advisories
        • digital advisories 3 -- technology-based platforms delivering agricultural guidance to farmers
        • multimodal advisory services 1 -- agricultural extension using multiple communication channels and formats
        • bundled advisory services 3 -- integrated delivery of multiple agricultural services including advisories, inputs, credit, and insurance
        • bundled climate-smart solutions 3 -- integrated package of climate adaptation and mitigation practices for agriculture
        • bundled cis and csa 5 -- integrated packages combining climate information with smart agriculture practices
        • bundled csa practices 1 -- integrated packages of climate-smart agricultural interventions implemented at scale
        • bundled financial services 1 -- integrated packages combining credit, insurance, and agricultural services

        • innovation package 2 -- bundled set of technologies, practices and services designed to achieve specific outcomes
        • digital platforms 6 -- technology-based systems for information sharing and service delivery

        • digital agriculture ecosystem 1 -- interconnected digital tools and platforms supporting agricultural decision-making and operations
        • digital agriculture assessment 1 -- frameworks for measuring and benchmarking digital technology adoption in agriculture

        • digital financial services 2 -- technology-enabled credit, insurance, and banking solutions for agricultural communities

        • digital seed inventory management 1 -- systematic approach to tracking and managing seed stocks using digital platforms

  • Other / Cross-cutting -- Concepts that span multiple domains, are broadly cross-cutting, or do not fit neatly into a single domain
    • Systems Thinking and Nexus Approaches -- Integrated systems approaches, nexus thinking, and transdisciplinary research
      • Nexus Frameworks -- Water-energy-food and related nexus approaches
        • water-energy-food nexus 8 -- integrated management of water, energy and food systems
        • water-energy-food-environment nexus 3 -- integrated framework for managing interconnected water, energy, food and environmental systems
        • water-energy-food-ecosystems nexus 3 -- integrated approach addressing interconnections between water, energy, food, and ecosystem management
        • water-energy-food-ecosystem nexus 2 -- integrated management of interconnected resource systems
        • nexus approach 3 -- integrated analysis of water-energy-food-environment interactions and trade-offs
        • biodiversity nexus 1 -- interconnected relationships between biodiversity and multiple sectors
        • climate-food nexus 1 -- interconnected relationship between climate change and food systems
      • Systems and Transdisciplinary Research -- Systems thinking, transdisciplinary and interdisciplinary approaches
        • systems thinking 2 -- holistic approach to understanding complex problems by examining interconnections and leverage points
        • transdisciplinary research 1 -- problem-focused research approach that integrates knowledge from multiple disciplines and stakeholders
        • transdisciplinary approaches 1 -- research and intervention methods that integrate multiple disciplines and stakeholder perspectives
        • interdisciplinary research 3 -- research approach combining multiple scientific disciplines to address complex problems
        • systems approach 3 -- holistic methodology analyzing interconnected components and feedback loops
        • systems science 1 -- interdisciplinary study of complex systems and their emergent properties
        • social-ecological systems 1 -- integrated systems of humans and natural components
        • causal loop diagrams 1 -- systems thinking tool to map interconnected causes and effects
        • system dynamics modeling 1 -- computational approach to understand complex systems through feedback loops, delays, and trade-offs
      • Food Systems Transformation -- Broad food systems change and transformation approaches
        • food systems transformation 26 -- fundamental changes in how food is produced, distributed, and consumed
        • food system transformation 9 -- comprehensive change in how food is produced, processed, distributed, and consumed
        • sustainable food systems 4 -- integrated agricultural systems that address climate, nutrition, and social challenges simultaneously
        • food systems resilience 4 -- capacity of food systems to withstand and recover from shocks while maintaining functionality
        • food systems 5 -- interconnected processes from food production through consumption and disposal
        • agrifood system transformation 2 -- comprehensive changes to make food systems more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive
        • food systems foresight 1 -- strategic planning methodology for anticipating future food system changes
        • food systems leadership 2 -- leadership development and behaviors within agricultural and food system contexts
        • food system analytics 3 -- data-driven analysis of food production, distribution, and consumption systems
        • island food systems 2 -- food production and distribution systems specific to island environments
        • frontier food systems 1 -- food systems at the edges of current research and investment priorities
        • indigenous food systems 1 -- traditional food production and consumption practices of local communities
      • Integrated and Adaptive Management -- Integrated approaches and adaptive management frameworks
        • adaptive management 4 -- iterative management approach that adjusts based on learning and feedback
        • theory of change 4 -- comprehensive description of how and why desired change is expected to happen
        • monitoring and evaluation 8 -- systematic assessment of intervention implementation and outcomes
        • monitoring and evaluation frameworks 3 -- structured approaches to measure and assess intervention outcomes
        • impact evaluation 4 -- systematic assessment of changes caused by development interventions
        • evidence-based policy 6 -- policy development informed by systematic research evidence
        • policy coherence 8 -- alignment and coordination of policies across different levels and sectors of governance
        • policy coherence analysis 1 -- systematic assessment of alignment and consistency across multiple policies
        • policy integration 7 -- incorporating innovations into official government frameworks
        • policy alignment 2 -- coordination of policies and institutional frameworks for coherent implementation
        • policy uptake 3 -- adoption and implementation of research findings in policy frameworks
        • policy dialogue 2 -- structured discussions among stakeholders to inform policy development and implementation
        • policy advocacy 2 -- strategic efforts to influence policy decisions using evidence
        • policy analysis 2 -- systematic evaluation of policy options, impacts, and implementation strategies
        • policy assessment 4 -- evaluation of regulatory frameworks and their effectiveness
        • science-policy interface 1 -- mechanisms connecting scientific research with policy development and implementation
        • strategic foresight 2 -- systematic exploration of future scenarios to inform current decisions
        • foresight analysis 1 -- systematic exploration of future scenarios for strategic planning
        • participatory foresight 1 -- collaborative approach to exploring and planning for future scenarios
        • institutional coordination 4 -- alignment and collaboration among organizations and agencies
        • inter-institutional coordination 3 -- collaborative arrangements between different organizations for common goals
        • multisectoral collaboration 3 -- coordination across different sectors and disciplines
    • One Health and Zoonotic Disease -- One health approach, zoonotic disease surveillance, antimicrobial resistance, and human-animal-environment health linkages
      • One Health Approach -- Integrated human-animal-environment health frameworks
        • one health approach 30 -- collaborative framework integrating human, animal, and environmental health
        • one health 6 -- integrated approach addressing interconnected human, animal, and environmental health
      • Zoonotic Disease Surveillance and Management -- Surveillance and management of diseases crossing animal-human boundaries
        • zoonotic disease surveillance 6 -- monitoring and detection of diseases transmissible between animals and humans
        • zoonotic disease epidemiology 2 -- study of disease patterns and transmission dynamics between animals and humans
        • zoonotic disease transmission 1 -- spread of infectious diseases between animals and humans
        • zoonotic disease management 2 -- prevention and control of diseases transmissible between animals and humans
        • disease surveillance 4 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant diseases across geographic areas
        • viral disease surveillance 1 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant viral pathogens
      • Antimicrobial Resistance -- Surveillance and stewardship of antimicrobial resistance

        • antimicrobial resistance 5 -- ability of microorganisms to survive exposure to antimicrobial drugs
        • antimicrobial stewardship 6 -- coordinated interventions to optimize antimicrobial use and minimize resistance development
        • antibiotic resistance monitoring 3 -- surveillance and measurement of antimicrobial resistance in bacterial populations
      • Biosecurity and Disease Prevention -- Biosecurity practices and disease prevention frameworks
        • biosecurity 7 -- measures to prevent introduction and spread of diseases in livestock
        • biosecurity practices 2 -- preventive measures to reduce disease transmission in livestock production
        • biosecurity assessment 1 -- evaluation of measures to prevent pathogen introduction and spread
        • biosecurity measures 1 -- practices to prevent introduction and spread of diseases in livestock systems
        • biosafety protocols 2 -- standardized procedures to prevent biological contamination and exposure
        • biosafety regulatory compliance 1 -- adherence to safety protocols and regulations for genetically modified organisms
    • Circular Economy and Waste Management -- Circular economy principles, waste-to-value technologies, resource recovery, and bioeconomy approaches
      • Circular Economy Principles -- Core circular economy frameworks and approaches
        • circular economy 7 -- economic model emphasizing resource reuse and waste reduction
        • circular bioeconomy 11 -- economic model using biological resources in circular value chains
        • circular economy principles 1 -- resource management strategies that minimize waste and maximize nutrient retention within production systems
        • circular economy solutions 2 -- waste-to-value systems that convert agricultural residues into useful products
        • circular economy approaches 1 -- resource utilization strategies that minimize waste through reuse and recycling
        • waste hierarchy 1 -- prioritization framework emphasizing prevention, reduction, reuse, and recycling for waste management
      • Waste-to-Value and Resource Recovery -- Technologies and approaches for converting waste to value
        • waste-to-value technologies 1 -- methods to convert organic waste into useful products like compost, biogas, or protein
        • resource recovery and reuse 1 -- circular economy approach to extract value from waste materials including biomass and wastewater
        • food loss and waste reduction 1 -- strategies to minimize food losses along value chains and consumer waste
        • food rescue 2 -- recovery and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve food security
        • food recovery 2 -- systematic collection and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve access
        • waste-to-feed conversion 1 -- process of converting agricultural by-products into livestock feed
        • livestock waste recovery 1 -- conversion of animal manure into useful products like biogas or compost
        • black soldier fly larvae composting 1 -- biotechnology using insect larvae to convert organic waste into protein-rich feed and organic fertilizers
        • black soldier fly farming 1 -- insect-based agricultural production system for protein and waste management
        • bioconversion 1 -- biological transformation of organic materials into useful products through living organisms
        • vermicompost production 1 -- composting process using earthworms to decompose organic materials
        • systematic composting 2 -- structured method for producing compost using organic materials and training
        • aerobic composting 1 -- controlled decomposition of organic matter in the presence of oxygen to produce fertilizer
        • composting 1 -- biological decomposition of organic matter into nutrient-rich soil amendment
      • Biogas and Bioenergy -- Biogas production and bioenergy from agricultural waste
        • biogas production 4 -- anaerobic digestion process converting organic matter to methane-rich gas
        • biogas digester performance 3 -- operational efficiency and gas production capacity of small-scale anaerobic digestion systems
        • biogas digester technology 1 -- anaerobic digestion system converting organic waste to energy
        • bioslurry application 1 -- use of organic waste slurry as fertilizer to enhance crop production and reduce emissions
        • anaerobic digestion 1 -- biological process that breaks down organic matter in oxygen-free environment to produce biogas
        • biomethane potential 1 -- measure of maximum methane yield from organic feedstock under anaerobic conditions
        • co-digestion 1 -- anaerobic digestion of multiple organic feedstocks together to optimize biogas production
        • biofuel production 1 -- industrial processes for converting biological materials into renewable transportation fuels
      • Payment for Ecosystem Services -- Mechanisms for valuing and paying for ecosystem services
        • payment for ecosystem services 2 -- economic mechanism compensating land managers for environmental benefits
        • payments for ecosystem services 1 -- economic incentives for conservation and environmental stewardship
        • payment for environmental services 2 -- economic mechanism that compensates land users for providing ecosystem services
        • payments for environmental services 1 -- economic incentive schemes that reward farmers for environmental stewardship
        • eco-compensation 2 -- payment mechanism to compensate for environmental services and conservation efforts
        • ecosystem services valuation 2 -- quantification and monetization of environmental benefits
        • ecosystem service valuation 1 -- economic quantification of benefits provided by natural systems
        • true cost accounting 1 -- comprehensive assessment including environmental and social costs of food production

    • Ethics, Justice, and Responsible Innovation -- Environmental justice, climate justice, responsible innovation, AI ethics, and decolonial approaches in agriculture
      • Justice and Equity Frameworks -- Environmental, climate, and social justice approaches
        • environmental justice 2 -- equitable distribution of environmental benefits and burdens across social groups
        • climate justice 2 -- fair treatment of all people regarding climate change impacts and solutions
        • just transition 3 -- equitable shift to sustainable economy that leaves no one behind and creates decent work opportunities
        • water justice 1 -- equitable access to water resources and inclusive decision-making in water management
        • distributive justice 1 -- fair allocation of resources ensuring equitable access to quantity, quality, and affordability
        • procedural justice 1 -- inclusive and participatory decision-making processes involving affected stakeholders
        • social equity integration 3 -- ensuring marginalized groups have equal access to resources and decision-making in development programs
        • food sovereignty 1 -- community control over food production systems and nutritional self-determination
        • dignified work 1 -- employment that provides income, respect, safety and social recognition
      • Responsible and Ethical Innovation -- Responsible innovation, AI ethics, and decolonial approaches
        • responsible innovation 2 -- innovation processes that consider ethical, social, and environmental implications

        • bias detection and mitigation 1 -- systematic identification and correction of algorithmic biases in ai systems
        • gender-responsive artificial intelligence 1 -- ai systems designed to recognize and mitigate gender biases in service delivery
        • decolonial pedagogies 2 -- educational frameworks that challenge colonial knowledge systems and power structures
        • feminist political ecology 1 -- analytical framework examining gender, power, and environmental resource access
        • fair data principles 1 -- data management standards ensuring findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable datasets
        • fairification 1 -- process of making data findable, accessible, interoperable and reusable
        • digital sequence information 3 -- genomic data used for genetic resource characterization and analysis

        • social norms 2 -- shared expectations and rules that guide behavior within groups
        • time poverty 1 -- insufficient time to meet basic needs due to excessive work burdens
        • intra-household allocation 1 -- distribution of resources among household members
        • intrahousehold decision-making 1 -- distribution of decision-making power and preferences within households
        • intra-household information sharing 1 -- flow of knowledge and data between members within farming households

      • Urban Farming Systems -- Urban and peri-urban agricultural production systems

        • guerrilla gardening 3 -- informal cultivation of plants in public or underutilized spaces

        • floating agriculture 2 -- cultivation on floating beds in waterlogged areas

    • Capacity Building and Learning -- Capacity building, training, and learning approaches across agricultural development
      • Capacity Building Approaches -- Core capacity building and development frameworks
        • capacity building 262 -- systematic development of skills and knowledge among target beneficiaries

        • participatory learning 8 -- educational methodology where stakeholders actively engage in knowledge creation and sharing through collaborative activities
        • participatory learning methodology 2 -- educational approach that engages participants in dialogue and reflection to foster learning and behavior change
        • participatory action research 1 -- collaborative research approach involving stakeholders in problem-solving
        • participatory action learning 1 -- collaborative approach integrating local knowledge with scientific methods
        • participatory research 32 -- research approach involving stakeholders as active participants in knowledge generation
        • participatory approach 13 -- research and development methods involving stakeholder participation
        • participatory methods 5 -- research approaches involving active participation of study subjects
        • participatory methodology 2 -- research approach that actively involves community members in the design and implementation process
        • participatory design 2 -- collaborative approach involving end-users in the design and development process
        • participatory co-design 3 -- collaborative approach involving stakeholders in designing research and interventions
        • participatory evaluation 5 -- research approach involving farmers and stakeholders in assessment of technologies
        • participatory monitoring and evaluation 3 -- community-led approach to tracking progress and assessing outcomes using locally relevant indicators
        • participatory mapping 7 -- collaborative approach involving farmers in adaptation planning and field-level solutions
        • participatory diagnosis 5 -- collaborative assessment of needs and priorities involving stakeholders

        • participatory validation 1 -- research approach involving stakeholders in confirming and refining findings
        • participatory scenario development 1 -- collaborative process for creating future scenarios with stakeholder input
        • participatory rural appraisal 1 -- community-based research method using local knowledge and participation
        • participatory geographic information systems 1 -- community-based mapping approach integrating local knowledge with GIS technology
        • participatory risk mapping 1 -- collaborative approach involving experts to identify climate-related risks and develop adaptation plans
        • participatory indicator selection 1 -- collaborative process for developing context-specific performance measures
        • participatory technology development 1 -- collaborative approach involving stakeholders in designing and developing technologies
        • participatory framework 1 -- structured approach involving multiple stakeholders in decision-making processes
        • action research 1 -- research approach combining investigation with direct action for social change

        • peer learning 3 -- knowledge exchange and skill development among individuals with similar experiences
        • peer learning networks 2 -- farmer-to-farmer knowledge sharing platforms for scaling innovations
        • farmer-to-farmer learning 1 -- peer-to-peer knowledge exchange methods that validate farmers as sources of innovation

        • virtual training 4 -- remote delivery of educational content and skills development
