        • mobile phone-delivered training 1 -- educational content delivered through automated phone calls or mobile platforms
        • game-based learning 1 -- structured experiential learning using games to develop understanding and problem-solving skills
        • gamification 2 -- applying game design elements to non-game contexts to enhance engagement
        • edutainment 1 -- educational content delivery through entertainment media to engage and inform audiences
        • community conversations 4 -- structured dialogue approach for community engagement and behavior change
        • participatory theatre 1 -- interactive drama approach used for community education and social change
        • theatre of the oppressed 2 -- participatory theatre methodology for addressing social issues and power dynamics
        • forum theatre 1 -- interactive performance method to explore social issues and solutions
        • community theater 1 -- use of theatrical performances and storytelling to raise awareness and promote social change in communities
        • experimental gaming 1 -- use of games to study decision-making and behavior in controlled settings
        • field excursion 2 -- educational approach using hands-on outdoor learning experiences
        • validation workshops 3 -- participatory sessions to verify and confirm research findings
        • diagnostic bootcamp 2 -- interactive workshop format for identifying business constraints and solutions
        • advocacy training 4 -- educational programs to develop skills for promoting causes and influencing policy
        • stakeholder training 7 -- educational activities designed to improve stakeholder knowledge and skills
        • extension training 4 -- capacity building programs for agricultural extension workers to improve farmer outreach
        • agribusiness training 3 -- education combining agricultural production with business management skills
        • financial literacy training 5 -- educational programs teaching budgeting, savings, credit management, and investment decision-making skills
        • financial management training 1 -- educational programs to develop business financial skills and practices
        • curriculum development 3 -- systematic design and creation of educational programs and materials
        • curriculum integration 6 -- incorporation of new knowledge and practices into educational programs
        • curriculum institutionalization 1 -- formal integration of interdisciplinary courses into university academic programs
        • mentorship program 1 -- structured learning relationship between experienced professionals and developing practitioners
      • Communities of Practice and Networks -- Learning networks, communities of practice, and innovation platforms
        • community of practice 24 -- collaborative network for sharing knowledge and best practices among practitioners
        • innovation networks 7 -- collaborative platforms connecting multiple actors to facilitate knowledge exchange and scaling
        • innovation platforms 5 -- multi-stakeholder forums for collaborative problem-solving and knowledge sharing
        • innovation hubs 2 -- territorial platforms that connect actors, capacities, and knowledge for technology validation and scaling
        • living lab 6 -- collaborative innovation platform where stakeholders co-create and test solutions in real-world settings
        • agroecosystem living labs 2 -- collaborative research approach engaging farmers and communities to test climate-smart practices in real farming systems
        • hub model 3 -- territorial multistakeholder platforms integrating research, innovation and extension
        • territorial innovation networks 2 -- collaborative networks linking research, extension and farmers across geographic territories
        • agricultural innovation systems 2 -- networks of organizations, policies, and processes that generate, adapt, and diffuse agricultural technologies
        • agricultural innovation 1 -- development and adoption of new technologies and practices in farming systems
    • Gender and Social Dimensions -- Gender transformative approaches, women's empowerment, and social dimensions of agricultural development
      • Gender Transformative Approaches -- Approaches that transform gender norms and relations
        • gender transformative approaches 29 -- interventions that address root causes of gender inequality and transform power relations
        • gender transformative research 7 -- research approaches that challenge and transform underlying causes of gender inequalities
        • gender-transformative intervention 3 -- development strategies that address structural gender inequalities and power dynamics
        • gender mainstreaming 8 -- systematic integration of gender perspectives into policies and programs
        • gender and social inclusion 7 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits across social groups
        • gender equality social inclusion 3 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits for all groups
        • gender equality and social inclusion 2 -- strategy to ensure equitable participation of marginalized groups
        • gender equality 3 -- equal rights, opportunities and treatment regardless of gender
        • gender equality integration 1 -- systematic inclusion of gender considerations in development programs
        • gender equity 3 -- fair treatment and opportunities for all genders in agricultural systems
        • gender inclusion 5 -- ensuring equitable participation and benefits for all genders in development processes
        • gender integration 4 -- systematic incorporation of gender considerations into policies, programs, and research
        • gender inclusivity 1 -- ensuring equal participation and empowerment of all genders in development processes
        • gender-inclusive approaches 4 -- methods that ensure equitable participation and benefits for all genders
        • gender-inclusive strategies 1 -- interventions designed to address gender-based barriers and promote equal participation
        • gender-inclusive planning 1 -- incorporating gender equality and social inclusion considerations into development planning
      • Women's Empowerment -- Women's empowerment, leadership, and participation
        • women's empowerment 15 -- process of increasing women's access to resources, decision-making power, and opportunities
        • women's empowerment in agriculture index 2 -- standardized tool for measuring women's empowerment in agricultural contexts
        • women's political participation 4 -- involvement of women in political processes and decision-making
        • women's leadership development 3 -- capacity building initiatives focused on enhancing women's leadership skills and confidence
        • women's land ownership 1 -- legal and social recognition of women's rights to own and control land resources
        • women's business skills training 1 -- educational programs designed to develop entrepreneurial and business management capabilities among women
        • empowerment 3 -- process of increasing capacity to make choices and transform those choices into actions
        • youth empowerment 2 -- strategies to engage and support young people in agricultural development
        • youth entrepreneurship 2 -- support for business development and job creation among young people
      • Gender-Responsive Programming -- Gender-responsive approaches in agricultural programming
        • gender-responsive approaches 7 -- interventions that acknowledge and address different needs and constraints of men and women
        • gender-responsive programming 6 -- development interventions addressing gender inequalities and promoting inclusion
        • gender-responsive research 2 -- research design that considers differential impacts and needs of men and women
        • gender-responsive agriculture 5 -- farming interventions designed to address differential impacts on men and women
        • gender-responsive interventions 2 -- programs designed to address differential impacts across gender lines
        • gender-responsive services 2 -- services designed to address differential needs and barriers faced by different genders
        • gender-responsive solutions 3 -- interventions designed to address differential impacts and needs by gender
        • gender-responsive policy 2 -- policy framework that integrates gender equality considerations into planning and implementation
        • gender-responsive budgeting 2 -- budget analysis and allocation considering differential gender impacts
        • gender-responsive extension services 2 -- agricultural advisory services designed to address specific needs and constraints of different genders
        • gender-responsive agricultural advisories 3 -- agricultural guidance systems designed to address different needs and constraints of men and women farmers
        • gender-responsive technical assistance 1 -- support services designed to address specific needs and barriers faced by different genders
        • gender-responsive delivery 1 -- agricultural services designed to address gender-specific needs
        • gender-responsive scaling 1 -- scaling strategies that integrate gender considerations and address gendered barriers
        • gender-responsive adaptation 1 -- climate adaptation strategies that address differential impacts and needs by gender
        • gender-responsive business planning 1 -- business strategy development incorporating gender equity and inclusion principles
        • gender-responsive livestock breeding 1 -- breeding programs that consider gender dynamics in livestock management and benefits
        • gender-responsive breeding 3 -- breeding programs that consider differentiated needs of men and women farmers
        • gender-sensitive interventions 3 -- development interventions designed to address gender-specific needs and constraints
        • gender-sensitive agricultural interventions 3 -- agricultural programs that explicitly address gender roles and women's participation in farming activities
        • gender-sensitive programming 1 -- design and delivery of programs that consider and address gender-specific needs and impacts
        • gender-sensitive measurement 1 -- evaluation framework incorporating gender equality considerations
      • Gender Dynamics and Analysis -- Analysis of gender dynamics, norms, and relations
        • gender dynamics 2 -- patterns of interaction and power relations between genders
        • gender norms analysis 1 -- systematic examination of social expectations and roles that limit participation and decision-making
        • gender norms measurement 1 -- quantitative tools for assessing and tracking changes in gender-related social norms
        • gender empowerment 4 -- process of increasing women's capacity to make strategic life choices
        • gender productivity gap 1 -- difference in agricultural productivity between male and female farmers
        • gendered labor response 2 -- differential impacts of external shocks on men's and women's work patterns
        • gender action learning system 1 -- participatory empowerment tool addressing gender inequities in development
        • gender-climate-nutrition integration 3 -- holistic approach linking gender equity, climate resilience, and nutrition outcomes
        • gender-based violence 6 -- violence directed against individuals based on their gender
        • intimate partner violence 3 -- physical, sexual, or psychological harm by current or former intimate partners
        • gender-inclusive water management 1 -- water governance approaches that ensure equitable participation of women in decision-making
    • Multi-Stakeholder Engagement and Governance -- Multi-stakeholder platforms, participatory governance, and collaborative approaches
      • Multi-Stakeholder Platforms and Partnerships -- Platforms and partnerships for multi-stakeholder collaboration
        • multi-stakeholder platforms 33 -- collaborative spaces bringing together diverse actors for innovation and change
        • multistakeholder platforms 5 -- collaborative mechanisms bringing together diverse actors to address common challenges
        • multi-stakeholder engagement 9 -- collaborative processes involving diverse actors including farmers, government, researchers, and private sector
        • multi-stakeholder collaboration 5 -- coordinated engagement among diverse organizations and sectors toward common goals
        • multi-stakeholder partnership 4 -- collaborative arrangements involving diverse actors from different sectors
        • multi-stakeholder approaches 1 -- collaborative methods involving diverse actors in problem-solving
        • multistakeholder partnerships 2 -- collaborative arrangements involving diverse actors across agricultural value chains
        • multistakeholder networks 2 -- collaborative structures involving diverse actors to coordinate knowledge sharing and scaling
        • public-private partnerships 20 -- collaborative arrangements between government and private sector entities
        • private sector engagement 2 -- involving commercial entities in development initiatives
        • private sector investment 4 -- mobilization of commercial capital for development and sustainability outcomes
        • civil society engagement 2 -- involvement of non-governmental organizations and community groups in policy and development processes
      • Stakeholder Engagement Methods -- Methods for engaging stakeholders in research and development
        • stakeholder engagement 16 -- systematic involvement of relevant parties in decision-making processes
        • stakeholder consultation 4 -- structured engagement with relevant parties to gather input and build consensus
        • stakeholder dialogue 3 -- structured multi-party discussions to address complex resource management challenges
        • stakeholder mapping 1 -- systematic identification and categorization of relevant actors and their relationships
        • stakeholder co-design 2 -- collaborative design process involving all relevant parties in solution development
        • co-design 17 -- collaborative development process involving multiple stakeholders in innovation creation
        • co-creation 4 -- collaborative development process involving multiple stakeholders
        • co-production 2 -- collaborative development process involving multiple stakeholders
        • co-creation approach 1 -- collaborative development process involving community members as partners
        • co-production of knowledge 1 -- collaborative research approach involving communities in knowledge generation
        • human-centered design 19 -- participatory design approach that prioritizes user needs and experiences in technology development
        • user-centered design 3 -- design approach that prioritizes end-user needs and experiences throughout development
        • participatory product design 1 -- collaborative approach involving stakeholders in identifying market segments and prioritizing traits for product development
      • Governance Frameworks -- Governance structures and frameworks for agricultural development
        • participatory governance 10 -- inclusive decision-making processes involving multiple stakeholders
        • community-based governance 2 -- local management systems where communities control and manage shared resources
        • community-based management 6 -- natural resource management approach led by local communities
        • community-based resource management 2 -- participatory strategy where local communities manage their natural resources
        • polycentric governance 1 -- multi-level decision-making involving multiple centers of authority
        • village-level governance 3 -- local institutional arrangements for decision-making and resource management
        • collective action 2 -- coordinated community efforts to address shared challenges
        • community engagement 2 -- participatory processes involving local stakeholders in development initiatives
        • district working groups 2 -- collaborative governance structures for coordinated natural resource management
        • enabling environment 3 -- policy and regulatory conditions that support business development
        • institutional learning 1 -- systematic knowledge acquisition and application within organizations
    • Scaling and Innovation Diffusion -- Approaches for scaling innovations and ensuring broad adoption
      • Scaling Frameworks and Strategies -- Frameworks and strategies for scaling agricultural innovations
        • scaling for impact 18 -- systematic approach to expanding successful innovations to achieve broader development outcomes
        • scaling pathways 12 -- systematic approaches to expand adoption of innovations
        • scaling framework 7 -- systematic approach for expanding successful interventions to broader contexts
        • scaling strategies 5 -- systematic methods for expanding successful interventions to broader populations
        • scaling readiness 5 -- systematic preparation of innovations for widespread adoption and implementation
        • scaling readiness assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of factors affecting the scaling of innovations
        • scaling assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of factors affecting innovation adoption and diffusion
        • scaling 4 -- process of expanding successful innovations or interventions to reach broader populations or geographic areas
        • innovation scaling 6 -- process of expanding successful agricultural innovations to broader adoption
        • inclusive scaling 4 -- strategy for expanding improved varieties that ensures broad access across different farm types and socioeconomic levels
        • adaptive scaling 2 -- approach to scaling innovations that adapts to local contexts and market conditions
        • responsible scaling 2 -- expansion of innovations while anticipating and mitigating social and environmental risks
        • participatory scaling 1 -- collaborative approach to expanding successful interventions through stakeholder engagement
        • selective scaling 1 -- targeted expansion strategy focusing on specific contexts or beneficiary groups for improved varieties
        • science of scaling 1 -- evidence-based understanding of how innovations achieve widespread adoption and impact
        • scaling scan tool 1 -- assessment framework to evaluate innovation scalability potential
        • scaling hubs 1 -- regional platforms that coordinate partnerships and knowledge sharing for agricultural innovation adoption
        • scaling champions 2 -- trusted individuals who coordinate actors and support innovation adoption
        • scaling fund 1 -- financial mechanism supporting innovation expansion and market penetration
      • Innovation Bundling and Packages -- Bundling innovations for greater impact
        • innovation bundling 5 -- integrating technical, digital and institutional solutions to address interconnected challenges
        • socio-technical innovation bundles 4 -- integrated packages combining technological innovations with social interventions
        • bundled innovations 2 -- integrated packages combining multiple complementary technologies and practices
        • innovation package 2 -- bundled set of technologies, practices and services designed to achieve specific outcomes
        • whole-farm innovation bundles 3 -- integrated packages of complementary technologies and practices for entire farming systems
        • integrated production bundle 4 -- coherent package of agronomic practices, inputs, and management recommendations for specific crops
        • bundled csa practices 1 -- integrated packages of climate-smart agricultural interventions implemented at scale
        • bundled climate-smart solutions 3 -- integrated package of climate adaptation and mitigation practices for agriculture
        • bundled cis and csa 5 -- integrated packages combining climate information with smart agriculture practices
        • technology bundling 1 -- integrated package of complementary technologies and services for scaling
        • integrated technology package 1 -- bundled interventions combining multiple technologies to address interconnected challenges
      • Technology Adoption and Diffusion -- Processes and dynamics of technology adoption
        • technology adoption 9 -- process by which farmers accept and implement new agricultural technologies or practices
        • technology adoption dynamics 2 -- patterns of farmer uptake and abandonment of agricultural innovations
        • adoption dynamics 1 -- patterns of technology uptake including initial use, consistent adoption, dis-adoption, and non-adoption
        • innovation validation 1 -- systematic testing of prototypes and assumptions with real users before full implementation
        • last-mile delivery 2 -- reaching end users through local agent networks and service providers
        • last-mile distribution 1 -- final stage of product delivery from businesses to end-users in rural areas
        • decentralized service delivery 1 -- distribution of services through local providers rather than centralized institutions
        • service delivery integration 1 -- coordination of multiple services to provide holistic support to farmers
    • Research Methods and Evidence Generation -- Cross-cutting research methods and evidence generation approaches
      • Experimental and Evaluation Methods -- Experimental designs and evaluation methodologies
        • randomized controlled trial 12 -- experimental design with random assignment to treatment and control groups
        • randomized control trial 1 -- experimental design randomly assigning subjects to treatment and control groups
        • cluster randomized control trial 1 -- experimental design where groups rather than individuals are randomly assigned to treatments
        • cluster randomized controlled trial 1 -- experimental design where groups rather than individuals are randomly assigned to treatments
        • clustered randomized control trial 1 -- experimental design using randomized groups of clusters to test interventions
        • randomized evaluation 1 -- experimental research design using random assignment to measure intervention impacts
        • randomized survey methodology 1 -- research design using random assignment to reduce bias in data collection
        • difference-in-differences analysis 1 -- statistical method comparing treatment and control groups over time
        • impact assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of intervention effects on outcomes
        • longitudinal impact assessment 1 -- long-term tracking of intervention effects over multiple time periods
        • cost-benefit analysis 6 -- economic evaluation method using financial indicators like NPV and IRR
        • cost-effectiveness analysis 2 -- economic evaluation comparing costs and outcomes of different interventions
        • ex ante economic analysis 2 -- forward-looking assessment of investment returns before implementation
        • economic impact assessment 2 -- quantitative evaluation of economic benefits and costs of interventions
        • sustainability assessment 1 -- evaluation framework measuring environmental, social and economic impacts
        • life cycle assessment 1 -- evaluation of environmental impacts throughout a product's entire life cycle
        • environmental impact assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of environmental effects of agricultural practices
        • disaster impact assessment 1 -- evaluation of natural disaster effects on livelihoods and economic outcomes
      • Systematic Reviews and Evidence Synthesis -- Systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and evidence synthesis
        • systematic review 7 -- comprehensive review methodology using systematic search and analysis protocols
        • meta-analysis 3 -- statistical method for combining results from multiple studies to assess overall effects
        • scoping review 1 -- systematic research method to map existing literature and identify knowledge gaps
        • evidence gap mapping 4 -- systematic methodology to identify and visualize gaps in research evidence
        • data gap analysis 3 -- systematic assessment to identify missing or incomplete information in databases
      • Mixed Methods and Qualitative Approaches -- Mixed methods and qualitative research approaches
        • mixed-methods approach 4 -- research design combining quantitative and qualitative data collection techniques
        • social network analysis 1 -- analytical method to study information flow and relationships within social networks
        • fuzzy cognitive mapping 1 -- participatory method to model stakeholder perceptions of system relationships
        • causal chain analysis 1 -- approach linking climate shocks, adaptive actions, and outcomes through explicit causal pathways
        • multi-criteria analysis 1 -- decision-making framework evaluating multiple competing objectives
        • multi-criteria decision-making 1 -- analytical approach for evaluating options based on multiple, often conflicting criteria
        • discrete choice experiments 3 -- statistical method to elicit preferences by presenting respondents with choice scenarios
        • choice experiment 1 -- research method using hypothetical scenarios to elicit preferences
        • willingness to pay 3 -- economic measure of maximum amount consumers will pay for a good or service
        • willingness to pay assessment 1 -- economic evaluation of farmer demand for agricultural technologies
        • contingent valuation 2 -- survey-based method to estimate economic value of non-market goods
        • experimental auctions 1 -- controlled market experiments to determine economic preferences
        • vickrey auction 1 -- sealed-bid auction mechanism for economic valuation where highest bidder pays second-highest price
        • best-worst scaling 1 -- survey method for assessing preferences by comparing best and worst options
        • computable general equilibrium modeling 5 -- economic modeling approach for policy impact analysis
        • social accounting matrix 2 -- comprehensive economic accounting framework linking production, income and expenditure flows
        • stochastic frontier analysis 1 -- econometric method to estimate production efficiency and separate inefficiency from random variation
        • technical efficiency analysis 2 -- measurement of how well inputs are converted to outputs relative to best practice
        • econometric analysis
      • Citizen Science and Crowdsourcing -- Citizen science and crowdsourcing approaches
        • citizen science 12 -- scientific research conducted by members of the general public in collaboration with professional scientists
        • crowdsourcing 1 -- engaging large groups to collect data or perform tasks using technology
        • pioneer positive deviance 8 -- approach that identifies and supports farmers already developing innovative local solutions
        • positive deviance approach 3 -- identifying and learning from individuals or groups with better outcomes despite similar constraints
        • positive deviance 1 -- approach identifying successful practices among those facing similar challenges
        • tricot approach 3 -- triadic comparison method for on-farm testing where farmers rank three technologies
        • triadic comparisons of technology options 15 -- research approach to decentralize technology evaluation using large numbers of participants for higher external validity
        • triadic comparison of technologies 2 -- participatory evaluation method where farmers compare three technologies simultaneously
    • Climate Security and Conflict -- Climate-conflict nexus, fragile settings, and humanitarian-development-peace linkages
      • Climate-Conflict Nexus -- Linkages between climate change and conflict
        • climate-conflict nexus 12 -- the interconnected relationship between climate change impacts and violent conflict emergence
        • climate-peace-security nexus 5 -- integrated framework addressing interconnected climate, peace and security challenges
        • climate security 16 -- framework addressing security risks arising from climate change impacts
        • climate fragility 2 -- vulnerability of systems to climate-related stresses and shocks
        • conflict sensitivity 5 -- considering peace and conflict dynamics in development interventions
        • conflict-sensitive programming 6 -- development approach that considers potential conflict risks and promotes peace-building
        • environmental peacebuilding 2 -- use of environmental cooperation to promote peace and social cohesion in post-conflict areas
        • herder-farmer conflict 3 -- violent conflict between nomadic herders and settled agricultural communities over land and resources
        • farmer-herder conflict 1 -- disputes between agricultural and pastoral communities over land and resource use
        • conflict diffusion analysis 1 -- measurement of geographic spread of violence and conflict
        • conflict classification 1 -- systematic categorization of different types of violence and conflict
        • political violence analysis 1 -- systematic study of organized violence and its impacts on communities
        • climate-conflict analysis 1 -- analytical approach to measure connections between climate change and conflict occurrence
      • Fragile and Conflict-Affected Settings -- Agricultural development in fragile and conflict-affected contexts
        • fragile and conflict-affected settings 6 -- contexts characterized by violence, weak institutions and humanitarian crises
        • fragile and conflict-affected systems 2 -- food systems operating in contexts of institutional fragility and conflict
        • conflict-affected settings 2 -- regions experiencing or recovering from armed conflict or civil unrest
        • humanitarian response 2 -- coordinated assistance to populations affected by crises and disasters
        • humanitarian-development-peace nexus 4 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian aid, development programming, and peacebuilding
        • humanitarian-development nexus 1 -- integration of scientific research into humanitarian efforts for sustainable solutions
        • hdp nexus 1 -- integrated approach linking humanitarian, development, and peace interventions
        • human security 3 -- protection of individuals from threats to their safety and wellbeing
        • anticipatory action 9 -- proactive interventions based on early warning signals to prevent crises
        • disaster risk management 2 -- systematic approach to identifying, assessing, and reducing risks from natural hazards
      • Displacement and Migration -- Climate-induced displacement and migration
        • climate-displacement nexus 4 -- relationship between climate change impacts and human displacement
        • climate-induced displacement 4 -- forced migration resulting from climate change impacts and extreme weather events
        • forced displacement 4 -- involuntary movement of people due to conflict, persecution, or environmental factors
        • displacement 2 -- forced movement of people from their homes due to conflict, climate, or economic factors
        • managed resettlement 1 -- planned relocation of communities away from climate hazards
        • labor migration 5 -- movement of people for employment opportunities, often from rural to urban areas or abroad
        • migration policy 1 -- government strategies and frameworks for managing human mobility
        • migration monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking and analysis of population movement patterns
        • refugee policy impact 2 -- political and social effects of policies governing refugee populations
      • Social Protection in Fragile Contexts -- Social protection approaches in fragile and crisis contexts
        • social protection 12 -- policies and programs designed to reduce poverty and vulnerability
        • social protection systems 1 -- government programs providing support to vulnerable populations during crises
        • social protection policy 1 -- government frameworks for providing assistance to vulnerable populations
        • adaptive social protection 1 -- social safety nets that adjust to climate and economic shocks
        • shock-responsive social protection 1 -- social safety net programs that can rapidly scale up during emergencies
        • cash transfers 2 -- direct monetary payments to individuals or households as social assistance
        • cash transfer programs 2 -- direct monetary assistance provided to households to improve welfare outcomes
        • digital cash transfers 1 -- electronic delivery of monetary assistance to beneficiaries
        • tapered cash transfers 1 -- gradually diminishing cash payments designed to support transition to self-reliance
        • social transfer programs 1 -- government programs providing financial or in-kind support to households
        • lean season assistance 1 -- humanitarian support during periods when food stocks are depleted and prices peak
        • ultra-poor graduation model 3 -- integrated intervention combining cash transfers, assets, training, and coaching for extreme poverty
        • conditional subsidies 1 -- financial incentives tied to specific agricultural practices or outcomes
    • Finance and Investment for Development -- Climate finance, blended finance, and investment approaches for agricultural development
      • Climate Finance -- Climate finance mechanisms, tracking, and mobilization
        • climate finance 11 -- financial mechanisms including carbon credits and international funds to support climate solutions
        • climate finance tracking 6 -- systems for monitoring flow and use of climate-related funding
        • climate finance mechanisms 4 -- financial instruments and structures designed to fund climate mitigation and adaptation projects
        • climate finance mobilization 1 -- strategies to attract and deploy financial resources for climate adaptation and mitigation
        • climate finance taxonomy 1 -- classification framework for directing climate investments toward specific sectors and activities
        • adaptation finance 3 -- financial resources dedicated to climate change adaptation activities
        • carbon trading 3 -- market-based mechanism for trading carbon credits to offset greenhouse gas emissions
        • carbon credit certification 2 -- formal verification process that enables farmers to monetize emission reductions through carbon markets
        • carbon market readiness 1 -- capacity building to enable participation in carbon trading mechanisms
        • biodiversity-positive financing 1 -- investment mechanisms that channel finance toward biodiversity conservation and sustainable livelihoods
      • Blended Finance and Investment -- Blended finance, investment planning, and business development
        • blended finance 8 -- combination of public and private funding to reduce investment risks
        • blended financing 1 -- financial strategy combining public and private funding sources to mobilize resources for development
        • investment planning 3 -- systematic process for identifying and prioritizing climate-resilient investments
        • investment readiness 2 -- preparation of enterprises for attracting investment funding
        • investment action plan 2 -- strategic framework outlining funding requirements and implementation pathways
        • bankable investment projects 1 -- development projects structured to meet financial institution lending criteria
        • bankable project development 1 -- preparation of investment-ready proposals that meet funding institution requirements
        • business incubation 4 -- support system for developing and accelerating new enterprises
        • accelerator programs 1 -- structured support programs for scaling agricultural innovations and businesses
        • agribusiness acceleration 3 -- structured support to help agricultural enterprises scale and access markets
        • impact accelerator 1 -- coordinated system design to enhance adoption of agricultural innovations
        • financial innovation 1 -- development of new financial products and services for agricultural value chains
        • inclusive business models 3 -- commercial strategies ensuring access to services for smallholder farmers and marginalized groups
        • business model development 8 -- creation of sustainable commercial frameworks for technology adoption
        • sustainable business model 1 -- commercial framework balancing economic viability with environmental and social benefits
      • Insurance and Risk Finance -- Agricultural insurance and risk financing mechanisms
        • index insurance 3 -- insurance products that use objective indices to trigger payouts rather than individual loss assessments
        • risk contingent credit 3 -- financial mechanism that links agricultural credit with insurance coverage to manage production risks
        • weather index-based insurance 1 -- agricultural insurance that pays based on weather parameters rather than actual crop losses
        • satellite-based crop insurance 1 -- risk management mechanism using remote sensing data to assess crop losses and trigger payouts
        • picture-based insurance 1 -- digital crop insurance using smartphone images to assess and indemnify crop damage
        • bundled livestock insurance 1 -- integrated insurance products combining multiple risk protections for pastoral communities
        • area-based yield insurance 1 -- insurance coverage based on regional average yields rather than individual farm losses
        • climate-smart credit scoring 2 -- financial assessment tool incorporating climate resilience factors for agricultural lending
        • climate-linked credit scoring 1 -- financial risk assessment integrating climate data with borrower profiles
        • livestock-based lending 1 -- financial model using livestock as collateral to provide credit access to smallholder farmers
    • Resilience and Vulnerability -- Resilience assessment, vulnerability analysis, and adaptive capacity frameworks
      • Resilience Frameworks -- Frameworks for assessing and building resilience
        • resilience 4 -- ability of households or systems to withstand and recover from shocks and stresses
        • socio-ecological resilience 3 -- capacity of social-ecological systems to adapt and maintain function under stress
        • adaptive capacity 3 -- ability of systems to adjust to climate change and reduce vulnerability
        • resilience assessment 1 -- evaluation of system capacity to cope with and recover from shocks
        • climate resilience assessment 4 -- systematic evaluation of systems' ability to withstand climate impacts
        • resilience science 1 -- interdisciplinary field studying systems' capacity to cope, adapt, and transform under stress
        • multidimensional resilience 1 -- measurement of household capacity to withstand shocks across multiple well-being dimensions
        • subjective resilience 2 -- household-perceived capacity to anticipate, withstand, recover from, and adapt to hazards
        • resilience capacity index 1 -- composite measure of household or community ability to withstand and recover from shocks
        • hydrological resilience 1 -- capacity of water systems to withstand and recover from disturbances
        • socio-ecological gains 1 -- joint improvements in productivity, quality, environmental sustainability, and equitable benefit distribution
      • Vulnerability Assessment -- Vulnerability assessment and analysis approaches
        • vulnerability assessment 6 -- systematic evaluation of exposure and susceptibility to risks
        • vulnerability analysis 2 -- assessment of susceptibility to climate and environmental stresses
        • climate vulnerability 9 -- increased susceptibility to climate-related risks and impacts
        • climate vulnerability assessment 3 -- systematic evaluation of exposure, sensitivity, and adaptive capacity to climate risks
        • vulnerability indexing 1 -- quantitative assessment of susceptibility to adverse conditions
        • vulnerability hotspot mapping 1 -- spatial analysis to identify areas of high climate and social risk
        • compound risk assessment 1 -- analysis of interconnected and cascading risks across multiple systems
        • coping strategies 3 -- actions taken by households to manage economic or environmental stress
        • maladaptation 2 -- adaptation efforts that unintentionally create new risks or vulnerabilities
        • transformational adaptation 4 -- fundamental changes in agricultural systems to address climate change impacts
        • transformative adaptation 3 -- fundamental changes in systems to address climate impacts
      • Transformative Change -- Approaches for transformative and systemic change
        • agroecological transition 29 -- shift from conventional to sustainable farming practices that work with natural ecosystems
        • agroecology transition 2 -- systematic shift from conventional to ecological agricultural practices and systems
        • nature-positive agriculture 3 -- farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem services while maintaining productivity
        • planetary boundaries 1 -- safe operating limits for Earth system processes that regulate planet stability
        • planetary health diet 1 -- sustainable eating pattern that promotes human health within planetary boundaries
        • transformational agroecology 1 -- transition toward environmentally sustainable, productive, and socially just agri-food systems
        • agroecological stewardship 4 -- practices that promote environmental sustainability and ecological health in agricultural systems
        • agroecological business modeling 1 -- development of sustainable business models based on ecological principles
    • Digital Technologies for Agriculture -- Cross-cutting digital technologies and platforms for agricultural development
      • Digital Advisory and Extension -- Digital advisory services and extension platforms
        • digital advisory services 22 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital extension 13 -- delivery of agricultural advisory services through digital platforms and mobile applications
        • digital extension platforms 5 -- technology-enabled systems for delivering agricultural advisory services to farmers
        • digital extension services 2 -- technology-enabled platforms for delivering agricultural advisory and information services to farmers
        • digital extension integration 2 -- combining digital tools with traditional extension services for farmer outreach
        • digital agricultural extension 3 -- technology-enabled delivery of agricultural advisory services and information to farmers
        • digital advisory platforms 5 -- technology-based systems for delivering agricultural and climate advisories
        • digital agro-advisory services 4 -- technology-enabled platforms delivering agricultural guidance and recommendations to farmers
        • digital agricultural advisory 2 -- technology-enabled extension services delivering agricultural recommendations
        • digital agricultural advisory services 1 -- technology-enabled platforms providing agricultural information and support to farmers
        • digital advisories 3 -- technology-based platforms delivering agricultural guidance to farmers
        • digital climate advisory services 3 -- technology-enabled platforms providing climate information and agricultural guidance to farmers
        • digital agriculture assessment 1 -- frameworks for measuring and benchmarking digital technology adoption in agriculture
        • ai-driven agricultural extension 4 -- use of artificial intelligence to deliver agricultural advice and information to farmers
        • multimodal advisory services 1 -- agricultural extension using multiple communication channels and formats
        • radio-based extension 1 -- using radio broadcasts to deliver agricultural information and climate advisories
        • interactive voice response 1 -- technology that allows interaction with computer systems through voice commands and responses
        • mobile-based extension 1 -- delivering agricultural advisory services through mobile phone platforms
        • mobile application 1 -- software platform designed for mobile devices to deliver services or information
      • Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning -- AI and machine learning applications in agriculture
        • artificial intelligence 11 -- computer systems performing tasks that typically require human intelligence
        • machine learning 8 -- computational methods that learn patterns from data for prediction
        • machine learning modeling 7 -- use of algorithms that learn from data to make predictions or classifications
        • machine learning algorithms 2 -- computational methods that learn patterns from data for prediction or classification
        • machine learning forecasting 6 -- artificial intelligence techniques applied to predict agricultural outcomes and resource needs
        • machine learning integration 1 -- combining machine learning algorithms with process-based models to improve agricultural predictions
        • machine learning workflows 1 -- automated processes for data analysis and model deployment using artificial intelligence
        • machine learning classification 1 -- automated pattern recognition for categorizing biological materials
        • deep learning 2 -- machine learning approach using neural networks for pattern recognition
        • deep learning detection 3 -- artificial intelligence approach for automated pattern recognition
        • computer vision 4 -- artificial intelligence technique that enables machines to interpret and analyze visual information
        • large language models 4 -- AI systems for processing and generating human-like text responses
        • generative artificial intelligence 2 -- ai systems capable of creating new content including text, images, or recommendations based on training data
        • vision-language models 1 -- AI systems that process both visual and textual information
        • named entity recognition 1 -- natural language processing technique to identify and classify named entities in text
        • automatic speech recognition 3 -- technology that converts spoken language into text for digital applications
        • transfer learning 1 -- machine learning approach applying knowledge from one domain to another
        • random forest classification 1 -- machine learning algorithm for image classification and pattern recognition
        • artificial intelligence modeling 1 -- computational approach using AI algorithms for agricultural applications and data analysis
        • artificial intelligence integration 1 -- incorporation of AI technologies to enhance service delivery and decision-making
        • artificial intelligence famine forecasting 1 -- machine learning approaches to predict famine conditions and food crises
      • Digital Platforms and Infrastructure -- Digital platforms, infrastructure, and data systems
        • digital platforms 6 -- technology-based systems for information sharing and service delivery
        • digital transformation 8 -- integration of digital technologies to reshape agricultural and food systems
        • digital agriculture 15 -- use of digital technologies to enhance agricultural productivity and sustainability
        • digital farming tools 3 -- technology platforms that support agricultural decision-making and farmer participation in sustainable practices
        • digital data collection 7 -- electronic systems for gathering and analyzing information
        • digital data capture 1 -- electronic collection and recording of information using digital platforms and tools

        • evidence-based decision support 3 -- agricultural advisory systems using scientific data to guide farmer decisions
        • digital twin 8 -- real-time digital replica of physical systems for management
        • digital twin technology 3 -- digital replica of physical systems used for modeling and decision support
        • data-driven agriculture 3 -- agricultural decision-making based on systematic analysis of climatic, soil, and agronomic data using computational methods

    • Miscellaneous Cross-Cutting Concepts -- Additional cross-cutting concepts that span multiple domains
      • Planetary and Environmental Sustainability -- Broad environmental sustainability and planetary health concepts
        • environmental sustainability 3 -- management practices that maintain ecological integrity while meeting human needs
        • nature-based solutions 9 -- interventions that use natural processes to address environmental and development challenges
        • sustainable agriculture 7 -- farming practices that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
        • sustainable agricultural practices 6 -- farming methods that maintain productivity while conserving resources and enhancing resilience
        • agricultural sustainability 1 -- farming practices that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
        • sustainable intensification 5 -- increasing agricultural productivity while maintaining environmental sustainability
        • regenerative agriculture 12 -- farming practices aimed at rebuilding soil health and enhancing ecosystem function
        • regenerative agricultural practices 1 -- farming methods that restore soil health and ecosystem function through practices like no-till, cover cropping, and crop rotation
        • agroecological practices 12 -- farming methods that integrate ecological principles for sustainability
        • agroecological solutions 1 -- sustainable farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem functions
        • agroecological products 1 -- agricultural products produced using ecological principles and sustainable farming methods
        • agroecological performance 1 -- measurement of farming system sustainability across multiple dimensions
        • agroecological assessment 2 -- evaluation of agricultural systems using agroecological principles
        • agroecological change 1 -- transformation toward ecological farming practices and systems
        • nature-positive agriculture 3 -- farming practices that enhance biodiversity and ecosystem services while maintaining productivity
        • zero-carbon agriculture 3 -- farming systems designed to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions
        • low-carbon agriculture 2 -- agricultural systems designed to minimize greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • low-emission agriculture 3 -- farming practices designed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining productivity
        • zero-carbon villages 1 -- rural communities designed to achieve net-zero carbon emissions
        • carbon neutrality 1 -- achieving net-zero carbon dioxide emissions
        • stewardship 2 -- responsible management and oversight of biotechnology innovations
        • land tenure security 1 -- legal and institutional frameworks that provide secure rights to land ownership or use
        • land degradation 4 -- decline in land quality and productivity due to human activities and climate factors
        • resource scarcity 3 -- insufficient availability of natural resources like water, land, or pasture
      • Livelihoods and Social Welfare -- Livelihood improvement and social welfare approaches
        • livelihood improvement 3 -- enhancement of income, employment, and economic opportunities for communities
        • livelihood capitals 1 -- assets and resources that individuals and households use to build their livelihoods
        • livelihood cooperation 1 -- collaborative strategies between communities to enhance economic resilience
        • savings and lending groups 1 -- community-based financial institutions that provide members with savings and credit services
        • cooperative development 3 -- building organizational capacity and governance structures for farmer collective action
        • mental health impacts 2 -- psychological consequences of climate change and displacement on affected populations
        • human security 3 -- protection of individuals from threats to their safety and wellbeing
        • rural revitalization 1 -- strategies to improve economic and social conditions in rural areas
        • economic inclusion 1 -- expanding access to economic opportunities for marginalized populations

        • baseline assessment 2 -- initial measurement of conditions before intervention

        • stage-gate framework 1 -- structured decision-making process with defined milestones and evaluation criteria
        • three-legged stool approach 1 -- innovative framework for improving meat safety in informal sectors
        • end-to-end approach 2 -- comprehensive intervention strategy addressing multiple points in a system
        • vision-to-action process 2 -- structured community planning approach to translate environmental goals into actionable farming plans
        • usability testing 2 -- evaluation of user interface design through task-based user feedback

        • lean data collection 1 -- efficient data gathering focusing on essential information

        • quality control 2 -- systematic processes to ensure product standards

        • traceability systems 1 -- systems for tracking products through supply chains

        • food safety management 2 -- systematic approach to ensuring safe food production and handling
        • food safety training 1 -- education programs to improve food handling and safety practices
        • agricultural research for development 1 -- research programs designed to generate agricultural innovations for development outcomes

        • national adaptation plans 5 -- medium to long-term national planning processes for climate adaptation
        • global goal on adaptation 1 -- international climate policy framework for adaptation planning and implementation
        • climate loss and damage 1 -- economic and non-economic losses from climate change impacts that cannot be avoided through adaptation
        • loss and damage assessment 1 -- quantification of climate-related losses that cannot be avoided through adaptation
        • climate disinformation monitoring 1 -- systematic tracking and response to climate-related misinformation
        • climate attribution 2 -- scientific methods to quantify human influence on extreme weather events

        • fertilizer subsidy reform 1 -- policy changes to agricultural input support programs

        • fisheries subsidies 1 -- government financial support to fishing industry operations
        • multilateral system 1 -- international framework for sharing plant genetic resources
        • digital compliance assessment 1 -- technology-based systems for evaluating adherence to agricultural standards and guidelines
        • digital marketing regulation 1 -- policies controlling online promotion of products to specific populations
        • regulatory oversight 1 -- systematic monitoring and enforcement of standards and regulations
        • food insecurity experience scale 1 -- standardized tool for measuring household food access and security
        • food crisis prediction 3 -- analytical approaches for forecasting food security emergencies
        • food crisis early warning 2 -- systems to predict and alert about impending food security emergencies
        • malnutrition forecasting 1 -- predictive modeling to anticipate acute child malnutrition for early warning systems
        • artificial intelligence famine forecasting 1 -- machine learning approaches to predict famine conditions and food crises
        • impact-based forecasting 1 -- weather prediction approach that focuses on potential impacts rather than just meteorological conditions
        • food security monitoring 3 -- systematic tracking of food access, availability, and utilization indicators
        • food insecurity 3 -- lack of reliable access to sufficient quantities of affordable, nutritious food
        • hidden hunger 1 -- micronutrient deficiencies despite adequate caloric intake
        • double burden of malnutrition 3 -- coexistence of under- and overnutrition within individuals, households, and populations
        • malnutrition burden 1 -- coexistence of undernutrition, micronutrient deficiencies, and overweight/obesity
        • child malnutrition 2 -- inadequate nutrition in children leading to stunting, wasting, or underweight conditions
        • nutritional security 2 -- access to adequate nutrition and essential micronutrients
        • nutrition security 2 -- reliable access to adequate, safe, and nutritious food for healthy living
        • school feeding programs 6 -- institutional programs providing nutritious meals to school children
        • home-grown school feeding 4 -- programs linking local food production with school nutrition
        • school meal diversification 2 -- integration of diverse local crops into institutional feeding programs
        • institutional procurement 2 -- structured purchasing by organizations like schools or governments
        • inclusive water governance 2 -- water management frameworks that ensure equitable participation and benefit-sharing
        • water governance 1 -- institutional frameworks and processes for managing water resources
        • global water governance 1 -- international frameworks and institutions for managing water resources across borders
        • groundwater governance 2 -- institutional frameworks and practices for sustainable groundwater resource management
        • polycentric governance 1 -- multi-level decision-making involving multiple centers of authority
        • rangeland governance 2 -- institutional arrangements for managing communal grazing lands and resources
        • community-based governance 2 -- local management systems where communities control and manage shared resources
        • community-led water governance 1 -- participatory management of water resources through strategic infrastructure operation
        • participatory guarantee systems 1 -- community-based certification mechanisms for organic and agroecological production
        • organic certification 4 -- formal verification process ensuring agricultural products meet organic production standards
        • geographical indications 1 -- certification system linking product quality and characteristics to specific geographic origins
        • iso/iec 17025 1 -- international standard for testing and calibration laboratory competence
        • fair data principles 1 -- data management standards ensuring findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable datasets
        • data interoperability 1 -- enabling different systems to exchange and use data effectively
        • high-performance computing 1 -- computational infrastructure providing enhanced processing power and storage for complex data analysis
        • agent network optimization 1 -- systematic improvement of field agent performance and management
        • pay-as-you-go system 1 -- payment model allowing gradual ownership transfer through installment payments
        • irrigation-as-a-service 2 -- business model where irrigation services are provided by third parties rather than owned by farmers

      • one health approach 30 -- collaborative framework integrating human, animal, and environmental health

      • disease and pest management 2 -- integrated strategies for controlling agricultural diseases and pests
      • feed and forage innovations 2 -- improved feeding technologies and practices for livestock in mixed farming systems

      • pest risk identification 2 -- systematic surveillance and early warning systems for agricultural pest and disease threats

      • zoonotic disease epidemiology 2 -- study of disease patterns and transmission dynamics between animals and humans

      • rice-fallow intensification 2 -- agricultural practice of growing crops in previously fallow rice fields

      • stewardship 2 -- responsible management and oversight of biotechnology innovations
      • cassava production 2 -- cultivation and management of cassava crops
      • pest and disease management 2 -- integrated approaches to control crop pests and diseases
      • drought stress management 2 -- strategies to mitigate water deficit impacts on crop production
      • artificial insemination protocols 1 -- standardized procedures for reproductive services adapted to low-input systems
      • high-performance computing 1 -- computational infrastructure providing enhanced processing power and storage for complex data analysis
      • thermotolerant vaccines 1 -- vaccines designed to remain stable at ambient temperatures without refrigeration
      • fertility management 1 -- integrated approach to improving animal reproductive performance
      • breed evaluation 1 -- systematic assessment of animal genetic resources for performance traits
      • drought adaptation 1 -- ability of crops or livestock to maintain productivity under water stress conditions
      • best-worst scaling 1 -- survey method for assessing preferences by comparing best and worst options
      • nano-culture technology 1 -- plant tissue culture technique using minimal space and resources
      • rapid diagnostic testing 1 -- quick field-based methods for disease identification
      • rice-fish polyculture 1 -- integrated farming system combining rice cultivation with fish production
      • fuzzy cognitive mapping 1 -- participatory method to model stakeholder perceptions of system relationships
      • crop-livestock integration 1 -- farming approach combining crop and animal production for mutual benefits
      • energy insecurity measurement 1 -- assessment of household energy access and shortage experiences
      • randomized survey methodology 1 -- research design using random assignment to reduce bias in data collection
      • multi-criteria analysis 1 -- decision-making framework evaluating multiple competing objectives
      • multidimensional resilience 1 -- measurement of household capacity to withstand shocks across multiple well-being dimensions
      • agricultural water insecurity 1 -- inadequate access to water for farming activities
      • livestock intensification 1 -- increasing animal productivity through improved feeding, genetics, and management
      • behavioral analysis 1 -- systematic study of animal or human behavior patterns
      • acaricide resistance 1 -- reduced effectiveness of chemical treatments against ticks due to genetic adaptation
      • cluster randomized control trial 1 -- experimental design where groups rather than individuals are randomly assigned to treatments
      • community-based aquaculture 1 -- fish farming systems developed and managed by local communities
      • estrus detection 1 -- identification of optimal breeding timing in livestock reproduction
      • animal welfare assessment 1 -- evaluation of animal well-being in production systems
      • vaccine wastage 1 -- loss of vaccine doses due to handling, storage, or administration issues
      • reseeding 1 -- introduction of seeds to establish vegetation in degraded areas
      • wastewater aquaculture 1 -- fish farming using treated or untreated wastewater as input
      • livestock waste recovery 1 -- conversion of animal manure into useful products like biogas or compost
      • crop-aquaculture farming 1 -- integrated production system combining crop cultivation with fish farming
      • push-pull technology 1 -- intercropping system using companion plants to repel pests and attract beneficial insects
      • aquaponics 1 -- integrated farming system combining aquaculture and hydroponics
      • cost-recovery system 1 -- financial model where services generate revenue to cover operational costs
      • pollen tube growth analysis 1 -- study of pollen tube development to understand fertilization barriers
      • complementary input use 1 -- coordinated application of multiple agricultural inputs to maximize technology benefits
      • integrated nutrient management 1 -- holistic approach to managing soil nutrients using multiple sources
      • proximity to natural habitat hypothesis 1 -- theory that nearness to natural areas enhances pollination services in agricultural systems
      • agroecological performance 1 -- measurement of farming system sustainability across multiple dimensions
      • conventional tillage 1 -- traditional farming practice involving mechanical soil preparation through plowing and cultivation
      • multi-criteria decision-making 1 -- analytical approach for evaluating options based on multiple, often conflicting criteria
      • zoonotic disease transmission 1 -- spread of infectious diseases between animals and humans
      • difference-in-differences analysis 1 -- statistical method comparing treatment and control groups over time
      • fertilizer deep placement 1 -- precision application of fertilizers at optimal soil depths to improve efficiency
      • randomized evaluation 1 -- experimental research design using random assignment to measure intervention impacts
      • forest management practices 1 -- systematic methods for managing forest resources and ecosystems
      • climate-food nexus 1 -- interconnected relationship between climate change and food systems
      • agri-voltaic systems 1 -- co-location of solar panels and agricultural production
      • integrated herd health packages 1 -- comprehensive livestock health interventions using one health principles
      • renewable energy solutions 1 -- sustainable energy technologies addressing rural infrastructure and livelihood challenges
      • multinomial ordered logit regression 1 -- statistical method for analyzing ordered categorical outcomes with multiple response levels
      • somatic cell score 1 -- indicator of mammary gland health and milk quality based on immune cell counts in milk
      • high-performance liquid chromatography 1 -- analytical technique for separating, identifying and quantifying compounds in mixtures
      • phenolic compound extraction 1 -- process of isolating and purifying phenolic compounds from plant materials
      • blanket fertilizer recommendation 1 -- uniform fertilizer application strategy applied across broad geographic areas
      • disaster impact assessment 1 -- evaluation of natural disaster effects on livelihoods and economic outcomes
      • livestock mobility 1 -- movement of animals along traditional grazing routes for feed and water access
      • metatranscriptomics 1 -- analysis of total RNA from environmental samples to detect pathogens
      • push-pull system 1 -- integrated pest management strategy using repellent and attractive plants to control pests
      • oviposition repellency 1 -- deterrent effect on insect egg-laying behavior
      • herbicide injection 1 -- direct application of herbicides into plant tissue for targeted destruction
      • rotated empirical orthogonal functions 1 -- statistical technique for analyzing spatial patterns in climate data
      • solar-powered aquaculture 1 -- fish production systems using renewable solar energy
      • climate risk perception 1 -- farmers' subjective assessment and interpretation of climate-related threats
      • rice-based diversification 1 -- cropping systems that combine rice with other crops to enhance resilience and productivity
      • multiplex pcr 1 -- molecular technique enabling simultaneous detection of multiple pathogens in one assay
      • desert farming 1 -- agricultural production in arid environments using specialized techniques
      • cluster randomized controlled trial 1 -- experimental design where groups rather than individuals are randomly assigned to treatments
      • viral disease surveillance 1 -- systematic monitoring and detection of plant viral pathogens
      • technology assessment 1 -- systematic evaluation of technology performance, functionality and adoption readiness
      • population dynamics 1 -- changes in organism population characteristics over time and space
      • low trophic aquaculture 1 -- fish farming systems using species that feed low in the food chain
      • balanced nutrient management 1 -- optimized fertilizer application strategy to improve crop productivity and resource efficiency
      • transabdominal ultrasonography 1 -- non-invasive imaging technique for pregnancy detection and reproductive assessment in livestock
      • cropping systems design 1 -- systematic approach to planning and optimizing agricultural production systems
      • purse seine fisheries 1 -- commercial fishing method using large nets to encircle fish schools
      • biological inputs 1 -- environmentally friendly alternatives to chemical fertilizers and pesticides
      • agent network optimization 1 -- systematic improvement of field agent performance and management
      • fish genetic improvement 1 -- systematic breeding approach to enhance genetic traits of fish species for aquaculture
      • agroecological products 1 -- agricultural products produced using ecological principles and sustainable farming methods
      • scoping review 1 -- systematic research method to map existing literature and identify knowledge gaps
      • participatory validation 1 -- research approach involving stakeholders in confirming and refining findings
      • bodyweight estimation 1 -- prediction of animal weight using measurable physical characteristics
      • segmented regression 1 -- statistical approach dividing data into segments for improved model fit
      • bayesian inference 1 -- statistical method using prior knowledge and observed data to estimate parameters
      • crop failure 1 -- significant reduction or loss of agricultural production due to various stresses
      • soil fertility assessment 1 -- evaluation of soil nutrient content and quality for agricultural decision-making
      • resilience science 1 -- interdisciplinary field studying systems' capacity to cope, adapt, and transform under stress
      • biofuel production 1 -- industrial processes for converting biological materials into renewable transportation fuels
      • multicrop land suitability 1 -- spatial analysis to identify optimal locations for integrated cropping systems
      • rotational fallow practices 1 -- traditional land management alternating cultivation with rest periods for soil regeneration
      • agricultural innovation 1 -- development and adoption of new technologies and practices in farming systems
      • direct seeding 1 -- planting seeds directly in the field without transplanting seedlings
      • forest-to-pasture conversion 1 -- land use change from forest to grazing land for livestock
      • heat stress 1 -- physiological stress caused by exposure to high temperatures
      • resilience assessment 1 -- evaluation of system capacity to cope with and recover from shocks
      • catchment risk and resilience index 1 -- integrated framework to assess vulnerabilities and adaptive capacity across river basins
      • integrated technology package 1 -- bundled interventions combining multiple technologies to address interconnected challenges
      • animal health loss envelope 1 -- metric measuring financial burden of disease by comparing current vs ideal health scenarios
      • stochastic dynamic population modeling 1 -- probabilistic modeling approach for livestock population health and economics
      • microbial diversity assessment 1 -- analysis of bacterial community composition and structure in environmental samples
      • long-term experiments 1 -- extended research studies conducted over multiple years to assess agricultural and environmental outcomes
      • bioconversion 1 -- biological transformation of organic materials into useful products through living organisms
      • superabsorbent hydrogels 1 -- water-retaining polymers that improve soil moisture retention and reduce irrigation needs
      • randomized control trial 1 -- experimental design randomly assigning subjects to treatment and control groups
      • disease outbreak management 1 -- coordinated response to prevent and control disease spread
      • serosurvey 1 -- epidemiological study measuring antibodies in blood samples
      • risk mapping 1 -- spatial analysis of disease or hazard probability
      • mass vaccination campaign 1 -- large-scale immunization program targeting animal or human populations
      • livestock nutrition management 1 -- optimization of animal feeding practices for improved productivity
      • social network analysis 1 -- analytical method to study information flow and relationships within social networks
      • concentrate supplementation 1 -- feeding strategy providing concentrated nutrients to livestock
      • average daily gain 1 -- measure of livestock weight increase per day
      • dry matter production 1 -- amount of dry biomass produced by plants or consumed by animals
      • agricultural sustainability 1 -- farming practices that maintain productivity while preserving environmental resources
      • biocontrol 1 -- use of biological agents to control pests, diseases, or contaminants
      • reproductive biotechnology 1 -- application of biotechnological tools to monitor and improve reproductive performance in livestock
      • socio-ecological gains 1 -- joint improvements in productivity, quality, environmental sustainability, and equitable benefit distribution
      • integrated rice-fish farming 1 -- aquaculture system combining rice cultivation with fish production
      • inland valley suitability assessment 1 -- evaluation of wetland areas for rice cultivation potential
      • iron-coated seed technology 1 -- seed treatment technology that improves crop establishment and reduces emissions
      • cropping system diversification 1 -- practice of growing multiple crop species to improve system resilience and productivity
      • pest management 1 -- strategies to control harmful organisms affecting crops
      • black soldier fly farming 1 -- insect-based agricultural production system for protein and waste management
      • dairy production 1 -- livestock management system focused on milk production
      • aquaculture production 1 -- controlled cultivation of aquatic organisms for food production
      • integrated pathogen screening 1 -- comprehensive testing approach combining multiple methods to identify disease resistance
      • loop-mediated isothermal amplification 1 -- DNA amplification technique that operates at constant temperature for rapid detection
      • puddled transplanted rice 1 -- traditional rice cultivation involving seedbed preparation and transplanting in puddled soil
      • clustered randomized control trial 1 -- experimental design using randomized groups of clusters to test interventions
      • elevated temperature stress 1 -- plant stress condition caused by higher than optimal growing temperatures
      • indicator repository 1 -- curated knowledge base of metrics for tracking adaptation progress
      • weed control efficiency 1 -- measure of effectiveness in reducing weed populations and competition
      • resilience capacity index 1 -- composite measure of household or community ability to withstand and recover from shocks
      • chemical control trials 1 -- field experiments testing effectiveness of chemical treatments for pest and disease management
      • soil nutrient management 1 -- optimization of soil fertility for crop production
